name: 4-H Camp Overlook
description: A Cornell Cooperative Extension 4-H camp on a quiet lake in the far northern Adirondacks near Mountain View, offering affordable weeks of nature study, canoeing, and campfire tradition.

name: 4-H Camp Wyomoco
description: Wyoming County's 4-H camp in the hills near Varysburg, giving western New York kids an affordable week of nature study, archery, and campfire cooking in a hands-on outdoor-learning tradition.

name: 92NY Camp Yomi
description: The 92nd Street Y's beloved day camp, busing Manhattan kids to a leafy camp property outside the city for swimming, sports, and arts, then home each evening. A classic first camp for Upper East and West Side families.

name: Acadia Institute of Oceanography
description: A marine-science camp on Mount Desert Island next to Acadia National Park, where small cohorts of budding oceanographers do real fieldwork - trawls, dissections, water sampling - by day and live like campers by night.

name: Adirondack Camp
description: A century-old classic on its own Lake George peninsula, where sailing, ropes courses, and big campfire traditions carry more weight than trophies. Kids choose their own activities within a warm, spirited community.

name: Adirondack Woodcraft Camps
description: A century-old traditional camp on its own lake near Old Forge, built around canoe trips, woodcraft, and a genuine unplugged Adirondack routine that has barely changed since the 1920s.

name: Agassiz Village
description: A nonprofit camp on Thompson Lake founded in 1935 to serve under-resourced kids from Greater Boston and Maine, with heavy scholarship support and a focus on leadership and life skills.

name: Aldersgate Camp & Retreat Center
description: A small Methodist camp tucked against the Appalachian foothills near the Red River Gorge country, with creek play, hiking, and simple faith-filled weeks. Rustic, affordable, and sincere.

name: Aldersgate Camp & Retreat Center
description: The United Methodist camp of northern New Jersey near Swartswood Lake, with wooded trails, swimming, and week-long sessions that keep faith gentle and the outdoors front and center.

name: Alford Lake Camp
description: One of America's oldest girls' camps, near the Camden hills since 1907. Campers live in tents, sail and ride, and absorb an ethos of simplicity and global citizenship that alumnae carry for life.

name: Aloha Beach Camp
description: An LA beach day camp with daily bus pickup across the Valley and Westside, delivering kids to Malibu-area beaches for surfing, boogie boarding, jet skiing, and sandcastle summers. Flexible any-day enrollment is a working-parent favorite.

name: Aloha Camp
description: The Aloha Foundation's flagship girls camp on Lake Morey, more than a century old, where wooden sailboats, mountain trips, and unplugged simplicity shape teenage summers.

name: Aloha Hive
description: Aloha's younger-girls camp on Lake Fairlee, a tender first-sleepaway world of fairy houses, swim docks, and singing, run with the Aloha Foundation's signature warmth.

name: Alpine Camp for Boys
description: The premier boys camp on Little River atop Lookout Mountain, mixing wholesome competition, river swims, and mountain adventure with a strong Christian ethos. Southern families book brothers in years ahead.

name: Alto Frio Baptist Camp
description: One of Texas' oldest encampments, hosting Baptist youth camps in the Frio River canyon near Leakey for over a century. Kids come with their churches for river swimming, tabernacle worship, and canyon views that haven't changed in generations.

name: Anderson Camps
description: A western ranch camp near Vail run by the Anderson family for decades, mixing a strong horse program with rafting, climbing, and backcountry trips. Has a loyal following among families wanting an authentic ranch feel.

name: Phillips Academy Summer Session
description: Five weeks of rigorous academics and dorm life on the storied Phillips Academy campus, drawing ambitious students from around the world. A taste of elite boarding school, workload included.

name: Angeles Crest Christian Camp
description: Perched above 5,000 feet on the Angeles Crest Highway less than an hour from downtown LA, this Christian camp packs zip lines, a pool, and mountain vistas into fast-paced church-week summers. A rite of passage for many LA-area youth groups.

name: Annunciation Heights
description: A Catholic camp of the Archdiocese of Denver near Estes Park, combining daily Mass and adoration with hiking, archery, and mountain beauty in a joyful, reverent culture.

name: Apachi Day Camp (JCC Chicago)
description: JCC Chicago's flagship day camp, mixing pools, sports, and Israeli-flavored song and spirit at sites around the metro area, with strong inclusion staffing.

name: Appel Farm Arts Camp
description: A warm, inclusive arts camp on a South Jersey farm offering music, theater, dance, film, and visual arts with a strong social-values streak. Known as a haven where LGBTQ+ and arts-focused kids feel completely at home.

name: Calleva Summer Camp
description: An adventure day camp on a Potomac River farm in Poolesville, with kayaking, climbing, mountain biking, and real outdoor skills for DC-metro kids who want dirt under their nails. Rugged and hands-on.

name: Arrowhead Day Camp
description: A long-established Montgomery County day camp on wooded Fort Washington acreage, with pools, sports, and a full traditional program. A Philadelphia-suburb staple for generations of families.

name: Asbury Hills Camp
description: The United Methodist camp of South Carolina, tucked into the Blue Ridge foothills with mountain creeks, a zip line, and week-long sessions of camp songs and quiet faith.

name: Asbury Hills
description: The United Methodist camp of South Carolina, tucked into a mountain cove with creek hikes, low ropes, and gracious Wesleyan hospitality.

name: Asphalt Green Summer Day Camp
description: The Upper East Side sports nonprofit's flagship camp, built around its Olympic-caliber pool and turf field. Serious daily swim instruction is the calling card for Manhattan families.

name: AstroCamp
description: A physics and astronomy camp in the San Jacinto mountains where rocket launches and telescope nights mix with rock climbing and a zipline. It nails the balance between real science and classic summer camp.

name: Athens Y Camp for Boys
description: One of the South's oldest boys camps, running since 1898 in the mountains at Tallulah Falls with a classic program of sports, lake time, and character-building traditions. Multi-generational loyalty here runs deep.

name: Adventure Unlimited Ranches
description: The A/U Ranches in the Arkansas Valley serve Christian Science families with a serious western program — colt training, rafting the Arkansas, and fourteener climbs — grounded in the community's spiritual practice.

name: Aviator Sports Summer Day Camp
description: A sports-centric day camp inside the huge Aviator complex at Floyd Bennett Field, with ice rinks, gymnastics, rock walls, and turf fields all under one roof. Weekly themes and rainy days are never a problem.

name: Avid4 Adventure
description: Outdoor-sports day camps at Bay Area sites where kids rotate through real climbing, mountain biking, kayaking, and paddleboarding with certified instructors and top-notch gear. Built to turn suburban kids into lifelong outdoor athletes.

name: Avid4 Adventure
description: A Front Range day-camp powerhouse that gets kids genuinely climbing, mountain biking, kayaking, and stand-up paddleboarding with pro-grade gear and instruction, with locations across Boulder and Denver.

name: Banner Day Camp
description: The North Shore's premier private day camp, a Lake Forest institution with heated pools, door-to-door buses, and a polish that rivals East Coast day camps — at matching prices.

name: Bar 717 Ranch (Camp Trinity)
description: A working ranch camp deep in Trinity County where kids ride, do farm chores, blacksmith, and swim in the Hayfork Creek swimming holes. Off-grid feeling, family-run since 1930, and adored by its fiercely loyal community.

name: Barbara C. Harris Camp
description: The Episcopal Diocese of Massachusetts' modern camp in Greenfield, NH, with excellent facilities, an openhearted progressive faith culture, and sessions welcoming all backgrounds.

name: Bay Cliff Health Camp
description: A remarkable therapy camp on Lake Superior in Michigan's Upper Peninsula, where children with physical disabilities receive a full summer of therapies — PT, OT, speech — woven into real camp life, largely free to families.

name: Bear Creek Camp
description: A huge Lutheran camp on thousands of forested acres in the Pocono foothills near Wilkes-Barre, known for joyful, high-energy staff and a hiking-and-campfire program that keeps kids outside all week.

name: Bearskin Meadow Camp
description: Run by Diabetic Youth Families in a high Sierra meadow near Kings Canyon, one of the oldest type 1 diabetes camps in the West, where campers hike and swim while learning to manage their condition among peers who get it.

name: Beaumont Scout Reservation
description: St. Louis's close-to-town Scout reservation in the Meramec hills, a workhorse property hosting Cub camps, shooting sports, and weekend Scouting all summer long.

name: Beaver Camp
description: A Mennonite-rooted camp on Beaver Lake at the western edge of the Adirondacks, where affordable week sessions mix banana-boat rides and campfires with gentle Christian mentoring.

name: Beber Camp
description: A polished Jewish camp on Lower Nemahbin Lake between Milwaukee and Chicago, with a B'nai B'rith heritage, strong waterskiing and sports, and a choice-based schedule. Culturally Jewish with lots of Chicago families.

name: Belvoir Terrace
description: An arts-intensive girls camp set on a Gilded Age estate in Lenox, with professional-caliber instruction in dance, theater, music, and visual arts. Individualized schedules feel more conservatory than camp.

name: Ben Delatour Scout Ranch
description: The Longs Peak Council's rugged scout ranch near Red Feather Lakes, offering merit-badge weeks plus a genuine backcountry feel with horse treks and outpost camps in the Laramie foothills.

name: Bender JCC Day Camp
description: The Bender JCC of Greater Washington's day camps in Rockville, offering swim, sports, and specialty tracks with a big Jewish-cultural community and robust inclusion programming. A Montgomery County institution.

name: Berkshire Soccer Academy for Girls
description: An all-girls soccer camp on a lakeside Berkshires campus where professional-level coaching meets real camp evenings of campfires and lake swims. Training is serious; the culture is confidence-building.

name: Bert Adams Scout Camp
description: The Atlanta Area Council's Scout camp east of the city, running busy merit-badge summers with a large lake, aquatics program, and newer dining and program facilities. The close-to-town counterpart to Woodruff.

name: Beth Tfiloh Camps
description: Baltimore's big Jewish day camp on a 33-acre wooded campus with a lake, run by the Beth Tfiloh congregation with kosher food and a traditional-but-welcoming Jewish program.

name: Bethany Birches Camp
description: A Mennonite-rooted camp in the woods of Plymouth with pay-what-you-can pricing, shelter-style sleeping under the trees, and a gentle, service-minded faith culture.

name: Bethelwoods Camp and Conference Center
description: A Presbyterian camp outside York with a small lake, high ropes, and week-long summer sessions that mix classic activities with easygoing faith formation.

name: Betsy-Jeff Penn 4-H Educational Center
description: North Carolina's flagship 4-H camp on a historic estate near Reidsville, mixing canoeing and climbing with hands-on nature and science education at a public-program price.

name: Big Lake Youth Camp
description: A Seventh-day Adventist camp on Big Lake beneath Mount Washington in the central Cascades, famous for wakeboarding, horses, and a vegetarian kitchen. Draws kids well beyond the Adventist community.

name: BIMA at Brandeis (Arts Program)
description: A selective Jewish arts intensive for teens on the Brandeis University campus, where serious young musicians, writers, dancers, and visual artists create in studio majors while exploring pluralistic Jewish life.

name: Birch Rock Camp
description: A tiny, beloved boys' camp on McWain Pond where 90 campers live simply, swim the lake, and cheer each other's small victories. Widely praised as one of Maine's kindest camp communities.

name: Bishops Gate Golf Academy Summer Camp
description: A boutique Florida golf academy running immersive summer camps built around individualized development plans, data-driven coaching, and college-placement savvy. Draws ambitious junior golfers from around the world.

name: Black Lake Bible Camp
description: A longtime nondenominational Bible camp on Black Lake just southwest of Olympia, with a big waterfront, gaga pits, and classic cabin-group competition. Affordable and unabashedly evangelistic.

name: Black River Farm and Ranch
description: A Michigan girls' horse camp that has built its whole program around riding — Western fundamentals, games on horseback, and barn life — at a price many families can manage. A Midwest favorite for first sleepaway summers.

name: Black Rock Retreat
description: A Mennonite camp and retreat center in Lancaster County's farm country, offering day and overnight camps with a low ropes course, hikes, and Bible teaching.

name: Blue Lake Fine Arts Camp
description: A major fine-arts camp in the Manistee National Forest near Muskegon, where thousands of young musicians, dancers, and artists each summer rehearse in ensembles and perform in the huge outdoor bandshell. Its international exchange program is a rite of passage for Michigan band kids.

name: Blueberry Cove Camp
description: A tiny 4-H camp on the saltwater shore of Tenants Harbor where kids explore tide pools, row boats, and eat from the garden - coastal Maine childhood distilled into a week or two.

name: Bluestone Camp & Retreat
description: A Presbyterian camp near Bluestone Lake in the southern West Virginia mountains, with lake and creek activities, hiking, and faith-centered community at an accessible price.

name: B'nai B'rith Beber Camp
description: A pluralistic Jewish camp on Lake Beulah an hour from Milwaukee and Chicago, with an elective program, upgraded facilities, and a warm big-tent approach to Jewish life.

name: B'nai B'rith Camp
description: The oldest Jewish camp in the Pacific Northwest, on Devils Lake near Lincoln City on the Oregon coast, mixing lake sports and beach days with joyful Shabbat traditions. Known as 'BB Camp' to a century of alumni.

name: B'nai B'rith Camp (BB Camp)
description: The Pacific Northwest's century-old Jewish camp on Devils Lake near the Oregon coast, run by the Portland Jewish community with a famously inclusive, laid-back culture and salty coastal air.

name: B'nai B'rith Perlman Camp
description: A pluralistic Jewish camp in Wayne County with a strong Israeli-staff presence, kosher kitchen, and a big-hearted, spirit-driven culture that welcomes kids from every kind of Jewish background.

name: Boston Children's Museum Summer Camp
description: A downtown Boston day camp inside the Children's Museum for the youngest campers, blending exhibit exploration, STEAM projects, and outdoor play by the week. A gentle, enriching first-camp experience.

name: Boxwell Scout Reservation
description: Middle Tennessee Council's large Scout reservation on Old Hickory Lake near Nashville, running multiple camps at once with big aquatics, shooting sports, and STEM programs. Generations of Nashville Scouts have summered here.

name: Brant Lake Dance Centre
description: A serious dance and theater camp for girls in the Adirondacks, offering daily ballet, jazz, and modern technique with visiting professional faculty, balanced by lake afternoons and a normal camp social life.

name: Brantwood Camp
description: A tuition-free camp on Pack Monadnock serving youth from under-resourced communities since 1904, with separate boys' and girls' sessions built around challenge, mentorship, and mountain air.

name: Brethren Woods Camp and Retreat Center
description: A Church of the Brethren camp in a Shenandoah Valley hollow, where creek stomping, nature discovery, and simple-living faith traditions shape gentle week-long sessions.

name: Brevard Music Center Summer Institute
description: A prestigious classical music institute in the Blue Ridge Mountains where high schoolers rehearse and perform alongside a professional summer festival. Admission is by audition and the orchestral training is conservatory-level.

name: Brewster Day Camp
description: A cheerful flag-raising, tie-dye-and-sailing day camp in the heart of the Cape, serving both local kids and summer families week by week. Cape Cod childhood, bottled.

name: Broad Creek Memorial Scout Reservation
description: A large Scouting reservation on Broad Creek in the wooded hills of northern Harford County, hosting troops for merit-badge weeks with a strong shooting sports, aquatics, and high-adventure lineup.

name: Brown Ledge Camp
description: A girls camp on Malletts Bay of Lake Champlain famous for total freedom of choice — no mandatory activities, just superb riding, waterskiing, and theater whenever a camper decides she's ready.

name: Brush Ranch Camps
description: A historic private camp on the Pecos River in the Sangre de Cristos northeast of Santa Fe, blending a strong arts and horse program with mountain adventure on a beautiful riverside campus. Recently reopened after years closed.

name: Bryant Pond 4-H Camp
description: The University of Maine's conservation-focused 4-H camp on Lake Christopher, famous in-state for fishing, shooting sports, and backcountry skills taught by Registered Maine Guides.

name: Boston University Tanglewood Institute
description: The nation's premier summer program for serious young classical musicians, held next door to Tanglewood with access to Boston Symphony concerts and coaching. Audition-based and intense, with extraordinary results.

name: Buckley Country Day Camp
description: A day camp on the leafy Buckley Country Day School campus in Roslyn, pairing traditional camp activities with the polish of an independent-school setting. A refined North Shore option for younger campers.

name: Buck's Rock Camp
description: A famous freedom-of-choice arts camp where teens wander among 30 open studios - glassblowing, publishing, circus, ceramics - and make what they want, when they want. Founded by refugees from Nazi Germany on progressive education ideals.

name: Buck's Rock Performing and Creative Arts Camp
description: A self-directed creative arts camp founded by refugee educators in 1942, with working studios in everything from glassblowing and publishing to sketch comedy. Campers design their own days — a paradise for independent, artsy kids.

name: Buffalo Trail Scout Ranch
description: A remote 9,000-acre ranch in the Davis Mountains of far West Texas, where Scouts ride, climb, and backpack under some of the darkest night skies in the country.

name: Burgundy Farm Summer Day Camp
description: A farm-and-woods day camp on the Burgundy Farm school campus inside the Beltway, where barn animals, a pond, and progressive camp culture feel a world away from the DC suburbs.

name: Butler's Day Camp
description: A long-running country day camp on a wooded Montgomery County farm in Darnestown, with pools, horseback riding, and a full traditional program. A DC-suburb favorite for decades.

name: Byron Bible Camp
description: A long-running evangelical camp on the James River near Huron, a fixture of east-river South Dakota summers with river swims, ball fields, and earnest Bible weeks.

name: Cali Camp
description: A canyon day camp on fifteen woodsy acres in Topanga, blending pools, a challenge course, and creekside nature play with a laid-back, artsy Topanga spirit. Feels like sleepaway camp that sends you home for dinner.

name: Calvin Crest
description: A Presbyterian-rooted camp in the sugar pines above Oakhurst near Yosemite's southern gate, running classic cabin camps, horse programs, and high-adventure weeks. Quiet, woodsy, and warmly low-key.

name: Camp Abbey
description: The Archdiocese of New Orleans' summer camp on the Bogue Falaya River, running separate boys' and girls' weeks of swimming, crafts, and Mass under the pines.

name: Camp Abe Lincoln
description: The Quad Cities YMCA's century-old camp in the wooded Mississippi River hills west of Davenport, a friendly, affordable first-camp choice for Iowa and Illinois families.

name: Camp Abilities
description: A developmental sports camp at SUNY Brockport for athletes who are blind or visually impaired, pairing each camper with a trained coach for a week of goalball, beep baseball, tandem biking, and track.

name: Agape Kure Beach Ministries
description: A Lutheran outdoor ministry with a wooded camp near Raleigh and a beach site at Kure Beach, offering inclusive faith formation, ropes, and coastal weeks.

name: Camp Agawak
description: A century-old girls' camp on Blue Lake in the Wisconsin Northwoods where traditions like Blue-and-White team competitions anchor a huge menu of waterfront and land activities. Generations of families return summer after summer.

name: Camp Agawam
description: A nonprofit boys' camp on Crescent Lake run by the Agawam Council since 1919, known for character education, simple living, and alumni devotion that borders on religious. Small and single full-season.

name: Camp Agnes Arnold
description: A classic piney-woods Girl Scout camp near Conroe serving Houston girls since midcentury, with lakes for canoeing, treehouse-style units, and strong sisterhood traditions.

name: Camp Agudah
description: The flagship Agudath Israel boys' camp in Ferndale, a Catskills institution where learning sedarim, leagues, and grand sing-offs have shaped generations of yeshiva families.

name: Camp Airy
description: A long-running Jewish boys camp in the Catoctin Mountains, sister to Camp Louise, that has served Baltimore and Washington families for a century with strong athletics, arts, and warm Shabbat traditions.

name: Camp Ajawah
description: A small, scouting-style camp on Linwood Lake north of the Twin Cities, run since 1929 with canvas platform tents, separate boys' and girls' sessions, and proudly old-fashioned campcraft.

name: Camp Akeela
description: A camp on Lake Fairlee designed for quirky, bright kids who think differently — many with autism-spectrum profiles — pairing classic camp fun with deliberate social-connection coaching.

name: Camp Akita
description: First Community Church's beloved camp in the Hocking Hills, a Columbus institution where a warm, open-hearted brand of faith meets ziplines, cliffs, and campfire worship.

name: Camp Al-Gon-Quian
description: The Ann Arbor YMCA's century-old camp on Burt Lake, a classic northern Michigan experience — sailing, riding, polar bear dips — at accessible Y prices.

name: Camp Alamisco
description: The Seventh-day Adventist camp on Lake Martin, with waterskiing, sailing, and vegetarian dining on one of Alabama's prettiest lakes. Wholesome, affordable weeks with strong returning-staff culture.

name: Camp Albemarle
description: A Presbyterian camp on Bogue Sound near the Crystal Coast, where kids sail, crab, and paddle tidal waters during affordable week-long summer sessions.

name: Camp Aldersgate
description: An Arkansas camp hosting medical and special-needs specialty weeks for children with a wide range of conditions and disabilities, with accessible facilities and hospital partnerships. Low fees and scholarships open it to most families.

name: Camp Aldersgate
description: A United Methodist camp in the Tug Hill/Adirondack foothills near Greig, offering classic week-long sessions of swimming, riding, and campfire worship on a quiet private lake.

name: Camp Aldersgate
description: A United Methodist camp on a quiet pond in western Rhode Island offering affordable weeks of swimming, crafts, and campfire worship in a gentle, welcoming setting.

name: Camp Alexander
description: A Scouts BSA summer camp on Eleven Mile Canyon's doorstep where troops earn merit badges in everything from climbing to fly fishing during classic week-long encampments.

name: Camp All-American
description: Perimeter Church's huge, polished sports day camp in Alpharetta, mixing quality coaching with high-energy worship rallies and a dedicated special-needs program. One of metro Atlanta's biggest summer operations.

name: Camp All-Star
description: A coed multi-sport camp on the Kents Hill School campus where campers choose their own sport-heavy schedules using prep-school facilities, including an ice arena and turf fields.

name: Camp Alleghany for Girls
description: A historic traditional girls camp on the Greenbrier River in southeastern West Virginia, with riflery, riding, canoeing, and a century of white-middy uniform traditions in a beautiful mountain valley.

name: Camp Allegheny
description: An evangelical Christian camp in the Allegheny foothills of Somerset County with horse weeks, sports, and energetic chapel sessions at a family-friendly price. A western-PA church-camp staple.

name: Camp Allen
description: One of the country's oldest camps dedicated to children and adults with disabilities, offering adapted swimming, arts, and overnight camp joy on a wooded Bedford campus.

name: Camp Allen
description: The Episcopal Diocese of Texas's big piney-woods camp and conference center, offering a warm, liturgy-friendly summer camp with horses, lakes, and inclusive traditions.

name: Camp Alonim
description: Set on the sprawling Brandeis-Bardin campus, Alonim is known for joyful Israeli dance, music, and horseback riding in a proudly pluralistic Jewish setting. Rikud (dance) culture here is legendary in LA Jewish circles.

name: Camp Alta Mons
description: A United Methodist camp in the mountains of southwest Virginia with a waterfall, pond, and gentle programming for church families around Roanoke and Blacksburg.

name: Camp Alvernia
description: Founded by Franciscan Brothers in 1886 on Centerport Harbor, this is one of America's oldest camps — a waterfront day camp with sailing, swimming, and old-fashioned camp values. The harbor setting is hard to beat.

name: Camp ANCHOR
description: The Town of Hempstead's cherished beachfront program for children and adults with special needs, staffed by an army of devoted volunteers at Lido Beach. Families describe it as a second family more than a camp.

name: Camp Andree Clark
description: The first camp ever given to the Girl Scouts, operating in Westchester's woods since 1921 — a piece of Girl Scout history that still runs classic outdoor sessions today.

name: Camp Androscoggin
description: A premier full-season boys' camp on Androscoggin Lake, running since 1907 with superb athletics, great facilities, and a tight-knit camper brotherhood heavy on tradition. Enrollment is competitive.

name: Camp Anita Bliss Coler (Camp ABC)
description: The Fresh Air Fund's free girls' camp at Sharpe Reservation in Fishkill, where NYC girls swim, create, and build sisterhood across two-week sessions in the Hudson Highlands.

name: Camp Aranzazu
description: A coastal camp on Copano Bay near Rockport built for children with chronic illnesses and special needs, with fishing piers, kayaking, and bay ecology woven into fully accessible programming. Partner organizations bring groups all summer.

name: Camp Arbutus Hayo-Went-Ha
description: The girls' camp of the State YMCA of Michigan, on Arbutus Lake near Traverse City, sending girls on the same ambitious wilderness trips as its brother camp — Pictured Rocks, Isle Royale, and beyond.

name: Camp Arcadia
description: A gracious, tradition-rich girls' camp on Pleasant Lake run by the same family since 1916, known for its wooden sailboat fleet, unplugged ethos, and gentle intergenerational community.

name: Camp Arcadia
description: A century-old Lutheran family resort and camp on Lake Michigan's shore, where generations return for beach weeks, morning devotions, and famously tight community.

name: Camp Archbald
description: One of Pennsylvania's oldest Girl Scout camps, on Ely Lake in the Endless Mountains since 1920 — a rustic, deeply loved place families fight to keep thriving.

name: Camp Arnold
description: The Salvation Army's camp at the foot of Mount Rainier near Eatonville, intentionally priced so low-income families across Puget Sound can send kids to a full week of camp. Horses, a lake, and a mission of access.

name: Camp Arrowhead
description: An Episcopal camp on Rehoboth Bay in coastal Delaware, where campers sail, crab, and swim on the water while living in an intentionally warm, inclusive Christian community. One of the state's few true sleepaway camps.

name: MetroWest YMCA Camp
description: The MetroWest Y's day camp on a wooded lake property in Hopkinton, with swimming, boating, and traditional camp skills at a community price. A well-loved outdoorsy option west of Boston.

name: Camp Asbury
description: A United Methodist camp on Silver Lake in western New York near Perry, offering affordable week sessions of swimming, boating, and gentle faith formation for families across the region.

name: Camp ASCCA (Easterseals Alabama)
description: One of the nation's premier barrier-free camps, an Easterseals facility on Lake Martin where campers with disabilities ride an accessible zipline, tube behind boats, and fish off adapted docks. It serves both children and adults all summer.

name: Camp ASCCA
description: Alabama's Easterseals camp on Lake Martin, one of the nation's leading fully accessible camps, where campers with disabilities ski, zip line, and fish with adaptive equipment and expert staff. Life-changing is the word families use.

name: Camp Asto Wamah
description: A small century-old camp on Columbia Lake with wooden cabins, canoe races, and a fiercely loyal multi-generational community. Simple, sweet, and reasonably priced for an overnight camp.

name: Camp Atwater
description: One of the oldest Black-owned summer camps in America, run by the Urban League of Springfield on Lake Lashaway since 1921. A proud legacy of leadership development with classic lakefront camp life.

name: Camp Augusta
description: A tiny, philosophy-driven camp in the Sierra foothills famous in camp-professional circles for its wish-craft: campers co-design their own activities, from blacksmithing to fairy tea parties. Intensely intentional, wonderfully weird.

name: Camp Avoda
description: A traditional Jewish boys camp on Tispaquin Pond that has been running on sports, spirit, and Shabbat since 1927. Small enough that every boy plays and everyone knows everyone.

name: Camp Avondale
description: The Istrouma Area Council's piney-woods camp north of Baton Rouge, a long-running Louisiana tradition with a spring-fed lake and full merit badge program.

name: Camp Awosting
description: The oldest continuously running boys camp in America, on Bantam Lake since 1900, now known for toys like go-karts and wakeboarding alongside classic camp brotherhood. Flexible sessions make it an easy on-ramp.

name: Camp Baco
description: A full-season brother camp to Che-Na-Wah on Balfour Lake, family-run for generations with strong athletics, waterskiing, and deep bunk loyalty. Many campers stay all ten summers.

name: Camp Baker
description: The Oregon Trail Council's scout camp on Siltcoos Lake near Florence, with sailing, motorboating merit badges, and the Oregon Dunes next door. Coastal-lake scouting with a sandboarding bonus.

name: Camp Balcones Springs
description: A high-energy coed camp in the hills near Marble Falls, known for polished facilities, spirited color-war-style competition, and a private spring-fed lake. It draws heavily from Houston, Dallas, and Austin families.

name: Camp Baldwin
description: A high-desert-meets-forest scout camp on the dry east side of Mount Hood near Dufur, known for mountain biking, shooting sports, and big-sky stargazing. A favorite for troops wanting sun instead of coast fog.

name: Camp Ballibay
description: A fully elective, audition-free fine arts camp on a Pennsylvania hilltop farm where kids choose their own schedule across theater, music, visual art, video, and riding. Famously accepting culture — no cuts, no competition, uniforms optional on everything.

name: Camp Barakel
description: A large, donor-supported Bible camp in the woods of northeast Michigan that has kept week-long camp astonishingly affordable for generations of church kids — two full lakeside sites and zero pretense.

name: Camp Barnabas
description: A beloved Missouri camp built specifically for kids with disabilities and chronic illnesses, pairing each camper with a trained Christian buddy for a full week of classic camp fun.

name: Camp Barnes
description: A free summer camp run by the Delaware State Police on the coastal marshes near Bethany Beach, giving Delaware kids week-long sessions of boating, fishing, swimming, and outdoor fun at no cost.

name: Camp Barney Medintz
description: The Marcus JCC of Atlanta's big, spirited Jewish camp in the north Georgia foothills, with two lakes, a huge activity menu, and a strong inclusion program. It's the summer anchor for much of Atlanta's Jewish community.

name: Camp Barstow
description: The Indian Waters Council's Scout camp on Lake Murray, with one of the state's best scout waterfronts — sailing, kayaking, and motorboating merit badges on big open water.

name: Camp Bartlett
description: A big, friendly Scout camp in the aspens above Bear Lake on the Idaho-Utah line, with horses, shooting sports, and a long tradition of serving troops from across the Intermountain West.

name: Camp Barton
description: A classic Scout camp on the western shore of Cayuga Lake near Taughannock Falls, serving central NY troops with merit-badge weeks, a strong aquatics program, and gorge hikes.

name: Camp Bauercrest
description: A tight-knit Jewish boys sports camp on Lake Attitash near the New Hampshire border, where fathers, sons, and grandsons have worn the same team colors for generations. Small, loud, and fiercely loyal.

name: Camp Bear Track
description: A private brother-sister camp on Greers Ferry Lake in the Ozark foothills, blending ski boats and tribal competitions with a gentle Christian ethos. Draws campers from Little Rock, Memphis, and Dallas alike.

name: Camp Becket
description: The boys half of the storied Becket-Chimney Corners YMCA camps, built around character, service, and rustic lakeside tradition rather than trophies. Candlelight ceremonies here go back over a century.

name: Wellesley Recreation Summer Camp
description: Wellesley's town recreation day camp, a classic parks-and-rec program with sports, games, and field trips at an affordable resident price. The reliable hometown choice for local families.

name: Camp Belknap
description: A YMCA-heritage boys camp on Lake Winnipesaukee that runs on tradition and a famously strong leadership ladder, where many campers return as counselors. Rustic cabins, sailing, and campfires over flash.

name: Camp Ben Frankel
description: A small, scrappy Jewish camp on Little Grassy Lake in southern Illinois, serving St. Louis and downstate families with an affordable, big-hearted community where every camper is known by name.

name: Camp Berea
description: A high-energy evangelical Christian camp on Newfound Lake with blob launches, wake sports, and big worship nights, running one-week sessions for kids, middle schoolers, and teens.

name: Camp Bernadette
description: The Diocese of Manchester's girls camp on Lake Wentworth, sister to Camp Fatima, blending classic lake-camp activities with light Catholic tradition. Affordable and multigenerational for many NH families.

name: Camp Bethel
description: A century-old Church of the Brethren camp in Virginia's Blue Ridge near Roanoke, with a lake, horses, and a warm, service-minded peace-church culture.

name: Camp Betsey Cox
description: A free-spirited girls camp on a Pittsford hillside farm where campers choose their days entirely — horses, bread baking, woodshop, or wandering the herb garden barefoot.

name: Camp Bette Perot
description: Northeast Texas's big Girl Scout camp in the East Texas piney woods near Palestine, with an extensive horse barn, lakes, and capacity that makes it a council flagship.

name: Camp Billings
description: A nonprofit coed camp on Lake Fairlee running since 1906, keeping classic camp — canoes, campfires, color teams — genuinely affordable for Upper Valley families and beyond.

name: Camp Birch Knoll
description: A girls' camp on North Twin Lake in Phelps offering a broad classic program — sailing, riding-free but water-rich — with warm traditions and a family feel at the far north edge of Wisconsin.

name: Camp Birch Trail
description: A girls' camp in northwest Wisconsin known for its intentional, body-positive culture and thoughtful staff training as much as its ropes course and waterfront — a favorite of Twin Cities and Chicago families alike.

name: Camp Birchmont
description: A large coed camp on 300 hilltop acres above Lake Wentworth in Wolfeboro, with a huge activity menu and separate boys' and girls' campuses that come together for events. Polished facilities, international campers.

name: Camp Birchwood for Girls
description: A girls' camp on Steamboat Lake near Itasca State Park where campers design their own schedules around a strong stable of horses and a classic Minnesota waterfront.

name: Camp Bishopswood
description: The Episcopal Diocese of Maine's camp on Lake Megunticook near Camden, offering affordable one- and two-week sessions with classic waterfront fun and an open-hearted, all-are-welcome ethos.

name: Camp Blessing Texas
description: A Christian camp near Brenham created for children with special needs and their siblings, pairing each camper with a dedicated buddy for a week of fishing, swimming, and celebration. Families describe it as life-giving for the whole household.

name: Camp Blue Diamond
description: A Church of the Brethren camp ringed by state forest in the ridges of Huntingdon County, where simple living, creek hikes, and peace-church values shape quiet, screen-free weeks.

name: Camp Blue Haven
description: A Church of Christ camp tucked into the Sangre de Cristo foothills of New Mexico, drawing Texas and New Mexico kids for cool mountain air, horses, and a cappella singing.

name: Camp Blue Ridge
description: A Seventh-day Adventist camp high in the Blue Ridge near the Parkway, with horses, a mountain lake, and wholesome, health-minded programming including vegetarian dining.

name: Blue Star Camps
description: The South's flagship Jewish camp, family-run since 1948 in the Hendersonville mountains. Shabbat traditions and Jewish culture anchor a huge menu of sports, arts, and mountain adventure for a big, spirited community.

name: Camp Bnos
description: Agudath Israel's storied girls' camp in the Catskills, where machanayim tournaments, choir, and dramatic productions anchor a warm, frum summer community many mothers pass down to their daughters.

name: Camp Bob Cooper
description: Clemson Extension's 4-H camp on a Lake Marion peninsula, where county 4-H weeks fill the summer with fishing, swimming, and lakefront campfires in the Santee Cooper country.

name: Camp Bob Waldorf
description: A heavily subsidized camp on the Max Straus campus in the Verdugo Mountains above Glendale, run by Jewish Big Brothers Big Sisters to serve LA kids of all backgrounds who could not otherwise afford camp. Values-driven and joyfully diverse.

name: Camp Boggy Creek
description: The SeriousFun camp co-founded by Paul Newman and Norman Schwarzkopf near Eustis, where children with serious illnesses attend medically supported, completely free weeks of fishing, boating, and pure kid joy.

name: Camp Bomazeen
description: A rustic Scout camp on Great Pond in the Belgrade Lakes, long used for summer merit-badge camps and weekend troop camping. Simple, woodsy, and steeped in central-Maine scouting history.

name: Camp Bon Coeur
description: A rare camp built for children with congenital heart conditions, based in Louisiana with cardiac nurses on site so heart kids can swim, play, and be ordinary campers safely. Families travel from across the country for it.

name: Camp Bonnie Brae
description: The oldest continuously operating Girl Scout camp in the country, tucked on a Berkshires lake since 1919 — riding, sailing, and a century of songs echoing off Benton Pond.

name: Camp Bothin
description: Girl Scouts of Northern California's camp in a Marin County valley beneath Mount Tamalpais, blending horse program and maker-style arts with redwood-canyon hikes.

name: Camp Bournedale
description: A private boys camp on Great Herring Pond near the Cape Cod Canal, mixing serious sports leagues with a strong lakefront and a famously loyal staff. It also runs a well-known school science program in the off-season.

name: Boston Explorers Day Camp
description: An adventurous city day camp that uses Boston itself as its campus, riding the T to beaches, parks, and museums for a different urban expedition each week. Great for independent, curious kids.

name: Camp Bratton-Green
description: The Episcopal Diocese of Mississippi's camp near Canton, cherished for its gentle inclusive spirit - including a long-running session for campers with disabilities - and easygoing pool-and-pinewoods rhythm.

name: Camp Bristol Hills
description: Ontario County's 4-H camp in the Bristol Hills above Canandaigua Lake, a friendly, budget-kind week of nature study, camp crafts, and counselor-led mischief.

name: Camp Brookwoods
description: An evangelical Christian boys camp on Lake Winnipesaukee with an unusually broad program — sailing, mountain trips, even flight and trades electives — paired with daily faith life. Sister camp Deer Run is next door.

name: Camp Buck Toms
description: Great Smoky Mountain Council's Scout camp on a peninsula in Watts Bar Lake, beloved for its sunset waterfront and classic Scoutcraft program. Knoxville troops return year after year.

name: Collin Street Camp Buckner
description: A Baptist camp on Lake Buchanan in the Texas Hill Country run by Buckner ministries, serving church kids and foster youth with lake fun and caring discipleship.

name: Camp Buckner
description: A Christian camp in the granite hills near Lake LBJ outside Burnet, mixing wakeboarding, horses, and paintball with intentional discipleship. It has grown a devoted following among Austin and Hill Country church families.

name: Camp Buckskin
description: A specialized camp near Ely for kids with ADHD, learning differences, and social-skills challenges, pairing a structured traditional camp program with academic support and genuine northwoods adventure.

name: Camp Buffalo Bill
description: A Scouts BSA camp in the Shoshone National Forest between Cody and Yellowstone's east gate, where troops fish, shoot, and hike in genuine grizzly country with mountain walls on every side.

name: Camp Bullowa
description: The Greater New York Councils' Scout camp in Rockland County near Stony Point, along Harriman's edge, hosting city troops for merit-badge weeks amid Lower Hudson Valley hills.

name: Camp Burgess & Hayward
description: South Shore YMCA's twin camps on Cape Cod - Burgess for boys, Hayward for girls across the pond - with kettle-pond swimming, a farm, and sailing trips. A beloved, affordable Cape institution.

name: Camp Burton
description: A camp and conference center on Vashon Island's Quartermaster Harbor with Baptist roots, hosting its own summer weeks plus partner camps on its beach and meadow campus. Mellow island camp with a saltwater shoreline.

name: Camp Butternut Springs
description: A northwest Indiana Girl Scout camp in the dunes-country woods near Valparaiso, serving Chicagoland and Region girls with lake swims and classic camp craft.

name: Camp Butwin
description: The St. Paul JCC's day camp on a surprisingly woodsy 100-acre site in Eagan, giving Twin Cities kids a lake swim, Shabbat sing-alongs, and classic camp days with bus service from around the metro.

name: Camp Butwin (St. Paul JCC)
description: The St. Paul JCC's day camp on a private lake in Eagan, where east-metro Twin Cities kids get a woodsy, water-filled Jewish summer that has served the community for decades.

name: Camp Caesar
description: A county-run 4-H camp in the mountains of Webster County, West Virginia, hosting a full summer of affordable youth and community camps with a pool, ballfields, and classic Appalachian camp traditions.

name: Camp Calumet
description: A Lutheran camp on Lake Ossipee with an 'everybody belongs' ethos, day and resident programs, and one of the warmest inclusion track records in New England church camping.

name: Camp Berea Basecamp
description: Camp Berea's adventure basecamp program on Newfound Lake, adding climbing and wilderness trips to the evangelical camp's lakefront core for older teens.

name: Camp Calvin Crest
description: A Presbyterian camp in the wooded bluffs above the Platte River near Fremont, where eastern Nebraska kids do creek hikes, campfires, and quiet faith weeks.

name: Camp CAMP
description: Run by the Children's Association for Maximum Potential, Camp CAMP on the Guadalupe near Center Point serves kids with significant medical and developmental needs — including many no other camp will take — with near one-to-one staffing.

name: Camp CaPella
description: A small camp on Phillips Lake near Bangor dedicated to children and adults with disabilities, offering accessible waterfront fun and respite in an intimate, high-support setting.

name: Camp Capers
description: A small Episcopal camp on the Guadalupe River in the Hill Country, where generations of West Texas kids have swum, sung, and worshiped in an easygoing, inclusive community.

name: Camp Caraway
description: A North Carolina Baptist camp for boys in the Uwharrie foothills near Asheboro, pairing sports, lake time, and riflery with straightforward gospel teaching at a church-camp price.

name: Camp Caribou
description: A family-run boys' camp on a private peninsula near Waterville, in the same family for generations. Big activity menu from sports to woodworking, with a reputation for warmth and homesick-kid whispering.

name: Camp Carolina
description: A century-old boys camp on the edge of Pisgah National Forest where campers pick their own daily activities, from whitewater kayaking to mountain biking. The vibe is muddy, adventurous, and proudly low-tech.

name: Camp Caroline
description: A Disciples of Christ camp on Dawson Creek near the Neuse, giving eastern North Carolina kids an affordable week of waterfront play, campfires, and low-key faith formation.

name: Camp Carpenter
description: The Daniel Webster Council's Cub Scout camp on a pond outside Manchester, where young Scouts get their first taste of BB ranges, canoes, and cabin nights in a gentle one-week format.

name: Camp Cartwheel
description: The Nevada Childhood Cancer Foundation's free camp on cool Mount Charleston above Las Vegas, giving kids with critical illnesses and their siblings a medically supported week of normal camp joy.

name: Fort Caswell
description: The North Carolina Baptist assembly on a historic fort site at the mouth of the Cape Fear River, where church youth groups gather for beach weeks, worship, and rec.

name: Camp Catherine Capers
description: A small horse-centered girls camp near Lake St. Catherine where riders of all levels get daily barn time, swim across the road at the lake, and live like a big farm family.

name: Camp Cavell
description: A YWCA-founded camp on Lake Huron north of Port Huron, over a century old, serving a wonderfully mixed community of metro Detroit and Thumb-area kids on a big freshwater beach.

name: Camp Cayuga
description: A big, activity-packed coed camp in the Poconos with flexible session lengths and a huge elective menu, from go-karts to a working stable. Good fit for families who want choices rather than a fixed program.

name: Camp Cedar Cliff
description: An evangelical Christian camp in the mountains east of Asheville, adjacent to the Billy Graham Training Center, mixing zip lines, sports, and gospel-centered teaching in one- and two-week doses.

name: Camp Cedar Falls
description: The Southern California Seventh-day Adventist camp in the San Bernardino Mountains, offering quiet, wholesome cabin weeks with horses, crafts, and Sabbath rest. Small, steady, and sincere.

name: Camp Cedar
description: A full-season boys' camp in Casco with top-tier facilities, deep athletic programming, and a fiercely loyal camper base that returns year after year. Expect polish, spirit, and a long waitlist.

name: Camp Cedarledge
description: Eastern Missouri's Girl Scout resident camp on bluffs above the Big River south of St. Louis, with strong riding, climbing, and a proud eighty-year history.

name: Camp Cedars
description: The Mid-America Council's main summer camp along the Platte River woodlands near Fremont, serving Omaha troops with a broad merit badge slate and sandy-bottomed lake swims.

name: Camp Celo
description: A tiny Quaker farm camp in the Black Mountains where children do farm chores, hike, and live simply in a community grounded in Quaker values of peace and cooperation. Famously hard to get into.

name: Camp CenterStage
description: A small performing-arts camp in Livermore where campers rehearse and mount a full production in under two weeks, with lake time between rehearsals and an emphatically welcoming culture.

name: Camp Chateaugay
description: A friendly coed camp on Lower Chateaugay Lake in the far northern Adirondacks with a broad waterski-to-theater program and a noticeable international camper contingent.

name: Camp Chatuga
description: A family-owned coed camp in South Carolina's mountain corner, where a private lake, horses, and goofy all-camp traditions have stayed charmingly consistent since 1956.

name: Camp Chawanakee
description: One of the West's largest Scout camps, spread along the pines of Shaver Lake in the Sierra with a huge sailing and canoeing waterfront. Troops come from all over California for its lakeside program.

name: Camp Che-Na-Wah
description: The sister camp to Baco on Balfour Lake near Minerva — a full-season girls' camp with strong swim and arts programs and an unusually tight, multi-generation alumni community.

name: YMCA Camp Cheerio
description: A high-country YMCA camp on the Blue Ridge escarpment near Roaring Gap, beloved by Piedmont families for its horse program, mountaintop views, and decades-deep traditions.

name: Camp Chen-A-Wanda
description: A classic full-summer coed camp in the far northern Poconos where color war and bunk loyalty run deep. Families come back generation after generation for the seven-week, everyone-knows-everyone experience.

name: Camp Cherry Valley
description: Catalina Island's other beloved Scout camp, tucked into a palm-lined cove near Two Harbors with kayaking, snorkeling, and island hikes. A little smaller and mellower than its west-end neighbor.

name: Camp Chestnut Ridge
description: A United Methodist camp on 500 acres of Piedmont forest and farmland near Hillsborough, offering both day and overnight weeks with a farm, lake, and creation-care bent.

name: Camp Chi
description: JCC Chicago's big, energetic camp in the Wisconsin Dells area, running since 1921, with everything from waterskiing to a teen travel wing and a proudly welcoming Jewish community.

name: Camp Chi (JCC Chicago)
description: JCC Chicago's flagship camp in the Wisconsin Dells, with a huge activity menu from wakeboarding to ceramics and an easygoing, culturally Jewish vibe. Big, well-resourced, and a Chicago institution for a century.

name: Camp Chief Ouray
description: The overnight camp of YMCA of the Rockies at Snow Mountain Ranch, running classic mountain sessions with hiking, riding, and archery at 8,700 feet. One of Colorado's oldest camps and a gentle first-sleepaway option.

name: Camp Chinqueka
description: Awosting's sister camp on Mount Tom Pond in Litchfield County, an all-girls camp with waterskiing, riding, and arts in a nurturing, screen-free setting. Brother-sister events connect the two camps all summer.

name: Camp Chipinaw
description: A nearly century-old camp above Swan Lake in Sullivan County with a big athletics program, an equestrian center, and deep color-war traditions shared with its sister teen program.

name: Camp Chippewa
description: A United Methodist camp in the oak woods near Ottawa, Kansas, hosting summer youth weeks with lake time, campfire worship, and eastern Kansas church community.

name: Camp Chippewa for Boys
description: A small, classical boys' camp on Cass Lake founded in 1935, emphasizing canoe expeditions — including trips to Hudson Bay waters — archery, sailing, and unplugged simplicity.

name: Camp Cho-Yeh
description: A fast-growing Christian camp in the East Texas piney woods north of Houston, with a pretty lake, strong staff training, and a welcoming vibe for rookies.

name: Camp Choconut
description: A tiny, deliberately rustic boys camp on hundreds of farm acres in Susquehanna County where campers do real work - building projects, farm chores, wilderness trips - and swim in a spring-fed lake. No electricity in cabins, and proud of it.

name: Camp Chosatonga for Boys
description: Kahdalea's brother camp, run by the same family on adjoining mountain land. Boys progress through woodsmanship, whitewater, and climbing with an emphasis on courtesy, grit, and quiet Christian values.

name: Camp Christopher
description: Catholic Charities' historic camp in the Cuyahoga Valley near Akron, running day and overnight sessions for a century with Mass, lake swims, and a famously loyal alumni base.

name: Camp Chrysalis
description: A Lutheran camp on the Guadalupe outside Kerrville, run by Cross Trails Ministry with one-week sessions built around river time, Bible study, and unhurried community. Warm, affordable, and welcoming to kids of all backgrounds.

name: Camp Claire
description: A tiny, old-fashioned church camp on the Connecticut River in Lyme, where a few dozen kids swim off the dock, sing after dinner, and unplug completely. The kind of camp people assume no longer exists.

name: Camp Cleawox
description: A Girl Scout camp on Cleawox Lake amid the Oregon Dunes near Florence, where girls canoe from camp to sand mountains and back. Dune hikes, lake swims, and platform-tent living on the coast.

name: Camp Cloud Rim
description: A Girl Scout camp perched at 9,000 feet on Lake Brimhall above Park City, where girls paddle, sail, and craft at one of the highest waterfront camps in the country.

name: Camp Cloverleaf
description: Florida 4-H's residential camp on Lake Francis in the state's rural heartland, with fishing, kayaking, and ag-and-nature classes at an extension-service price. Honest, old-style camp for Heartland kids.

name: Camp Cobbossee
description: One of the oldest boys' sports camps in the country, on Cobbossee Lake since 1902. Strong baseball and all-around athletics with over a century of traditions and a proud alumni roster.

name: Camp Cody
description: A big, modern coed camp on Ossipee Lake with glossy facilities, a huge elective menu from wake sports to robotics, and a notably international camper body.

name: Camp Wildwood NH
description: A small Christian camp on Newfound Lake in central New Hampshire where church families send kids for swimming, canoeing, and campfire devotions.

name: Camp Coker
description: A longleaf-pine camp in South Carolina's Pee Dee region with a blackwater lake and generations of Sandhills troops behind it. Old-school Carolina Scouting.

name: Camp Colley
description: The City of Phoenix's own mountain camp near Happy Jack, created to give city kids — many attending on scholarship — a low-cost week of forest, campfires, and firsts far from the desert floor.

name: Camp Comeca
description: A United Methodist camp in the canyons south of Cozad in central Nebraska, a longtime summer gathering point for the state's Methodist youth.

name: Camp Comstock
description: A Girl Scout camp on the bluffs of Cayuga Lake near Ithaca, mixing badge-style skill-building with lake swims, platform tents, and generations of scouting tradition.

name: Camp Concord
description: The City of Concord's municipal camp near Fallen Leaf Lake at Tahoe, offering affordable youth weeks with hikes to Desolation Wilderness viewpoints, lake days, and classic campfire programs. City-camp pricing, national-park scenery.

name: Camp Conestoga
description: A Laurel Highlands camp in Pennsylvania's Somerset County farm-and-forest country, a smaller reservation with a devoted regional following and honest mountain camping.

name: Camp Conowingo
description: A Girl Scout resident camp in the wooded hills above the Susquehanna in Cecil County, with horseback riding, a pool, and traditional tent-and-cabin camping that generations of Maryland Scouts grew up on.

name: Camp Conrad-Chinnock
description: Southern California's longtime camp for kids with type 1 diabetes, high in the San Bernardino Mountains, where medical staff outnumber worries and campers graduate into leadership and counselor tracks.

name: Camp Conrad Weiser
description: A YMCA Christian camp on South Mountain near Reading, Pennsylvania, offering pond swimming, ropes, and values-based programming for Berks County families.

name: Camp Constantin
description: The Circle Ten Council's water camp on Possum Kingdom Lake, famous among Texas Scouts for sailing, water skiing, and cliff-ringed blue water in ranch country.

name: Camp Courage (True Friends)
description: True Friends' flagship accessible camp on Cedar Lake in Maple Lake, purpose-built for campers with physical and developmental disabilities — adaptive waterfront, therapeutic riding, and staff who make everything possible.

name: Camp Courageous
description: An Iowa institution serving thousands of campers with disabilities year-round, funded almost entirely by donations rather than government money. Campers canoe, ride the zipline, and explore caves with individualized support.

name: Camp Courageous of Iowa
description: Iowa's donation-supported camp for people with disabilities, running year-round near the Maquoketa River bluffs with adapted caving, canoeing, and zipline adventures.

name: Camp Courant
description: One of the nation's oldest free day camps, serving Hartford children since 1894 with buses, meals, swimming, and enrichment at no cost to families. A quietly heroic Hartford institution.

name: Camp Cowen
description: A Presbyterian camp along the Gauley River in the Webster County mountains, offering simple, affordable faith-centered weeks with river swimming, hiking, and campfire worship.

name: Camp Cowles
description: The Inland Northwest Council's scout camp on Diamond Lake near Newport, serving Spokane-area troops for generations. Sailing, swimming, and merit badge classics on a quiet pine-ringed lake.

name: Camp Coyote
description: A ranch-style coed camp north of Houston near Huntsville where horses, fishing, and old-fashioned outdoor play take priority over polish. Its Texas-ranch flavor appeals to families wanting boots-and-saddle summers close to the city.

name: Camp Crestfield
description: A Presbyterian camp near Slippery Rock serving Pittsburgh-area churches with creek play, low ropes, and warm, small-group cabin life. Known for gentle staff and a strong welcome for first-time campers.

name: Camp Crestridge for Girls
description: Ridgecrest's sister camp for girls in the same Blue Ridge cove, pairing tribes and traditions with horses, crafts, and Christ-centered counselors.

name: Camp Crooked Creek
description: The Lincoln Heritage Council's Scout camp south of Louisville, with a full merit-badge program, pool and lake aquatics, and STEM offerings alongside classic Scoutcraft.

name: Camp Cross
description: A boat-access Episcopal camp on a forested point of Lake Coeur d'Alene near Harrison, cherished for its remoteness, canoeing, and generations of tight-knit camper community.

name: Camp Crystal Lake
description: A rare public-school-district-owned sleepaway camp, run by Alachua County schools on a clear sand-bottom lake, mixing environmental education with classic camp fun. Gainesville kids grow up on it - and yes, they've heard the movie jokes.

name: Camp Daggett
description: A community treasure on Walloon Lake near Petoskey operating since 1925 under the motto 'the other fellow first,' with affordable week-long sessions and a gorgeous stone lodge.

name: Camp Daisy and Harry Stein
description: Arizona's Jewish overnight camp in the cool pines above Prescott, run by Congregation Beth Israel, giving desert kids a mountain summer with Reform-flavored Jewish community.

name: Camp Daisy Hindman
description: A Flint Hills Girl Scout camp southwest of Topeka running since the 1930s, where prairie sunsets, horses, and a spring-fed pool define Kansas girlhood summers.

name: Camp DaKaNi
description: Camp Fire's wooded day camp tucked inside Oklahoma City, where kids build forts, shoot archery, and cook over fires like it's still 1955 — in the best way. A rare pocket of woodsy childhood in the metro.

name: Camp Daniel Boone
description: A high-country camp on the East Fork of the Pigeon River outside Waynesville, prized for cool Smoky Mountain air, trout water, and treks toward Cold Mountain right from camp.

name: Camp Dark Waters
description: A small Quaker camp on the cedar-dark Rancocas Creek in the Pine Barrens, where cooperation, simplicity, and canoe trips matter more than competition. Affordable, gentle, and beloved by generations of South Jersey families.

name: Camp Dart-Lo
description: Camp Fire's riverside day camp on the Little Spokane River north of Spokane, a shady summer institution with river wading, archery, and all-camp songs. Generations of Spokane kids have started camping here.

name: Camp David of the Ozarks
description: A small Christian camp near Rolla that serves children of incarcerated parents, pairing mentorship and horses with year-round follow-up support. Donor-funded so campers attend at little or no cost.

name: Camp Davy Crockett
description: The Sequoyah Council's Scout camp on Cherokee Lake in upper East Tennessee, with a strong aquatics and merit-badge program and a proud frontier theme. A dependable, affordable Scout summer.

name: Camp de Benneville Pines
description: The Unitarian Universalist camp of Southern California, high in the San Bernardino pines, where summer youth camps emphasize radical welcome, creativity, and social conscience over competition. A haven for kids who never quite fit the sporty camp mold.

name: Camp Deer Run
description: The girls' half of Christian Camps and Conferences on Lake Winnipesaukee, offering riding, watersports, and arts alongside daily devotions and a warm mentoring culture.

name: Camp Deerhorn
description: A boys' camp near Rhinelander run by the same family since 1930, mixing waterskiing, riding, and sports with an emphasis on kindness — the 'Deerhorn Creed' is taken seriously. Father-son weekends are a signature.

name: Camp Deerpark
description: A Mennonite camp in the Shawangunk foothills near Cuddebackville, serving city and country kids alike with affordable weeks of hiking, swimming, and a gentle, service-minded Anabaptist ethos.

name: Camp Deerwood
description: A traditional family-run boys camp on Squam Lake with wooden sailboats, ball fields, and a no-frills ethic that generations of families return to summer after summer.

name: Camp del Corazon
description: A free camp on Catalina Island for children with heart disease, founded by two pediatric cardiology nurses, with a full cardiac medical team on site so kids with serious heart conditions can kayak and hike safely.

name: Camp DeSoto
description: A beloved girls camp on Lookout Mountain in Mentone with a century of history, known for its emphasis on inner beauty and inclusion - no phones, no makeup, all encouragement - around a spring-fed lake. Waitlists are long and loyal.

name: Camp Dixie
description: A century-old independent camp in the Rabun County mountains where kids choose their own activities, from riding to woodshop to the spring-fed lake. Deliberately small, screen-free, and proudly old-fashioned.

name: Camp Don Bosco
description: The Archdiocese of Seattle's CYO camp in the Tolt River valley near Carnation, pairing Catholic community and daily prayer with archery, crafts, and river play. Lots of parish groups and multigenerational families.

name: Camp Don Lee
description: A United Methodist sailing camp on the wide Neuse River near the North Carolina coast, where kids learn to skipper sailboats between campfires and creation-care lessons.

name: Camp Dora Golding
description: A century-old Orthodox boys camp in the Poconos combining daily learning and davening with a genuinely strong sports and swim program. Known for spirited leagues and a devoted alumni base.

name: Camp Dorothy Thomas
description: Tampa Bay's Girl Scout resident camp along the Alafia River, best known for its equestrian program and for making Florida wilderness feel friendly to first-timers.

name: Camp Doublecreek
description: An Austin-area institution since 1971, this Round Rock day camp puts kids on horses, in pools, and across sports fields with bus pickup around the metro. Many staff are former campers, and it shows in the culture.

name: Camp Downer
description: A homegrown Vermont camp in Downer State Forest with 4-H roots, where kids from all over the state swim in the pool, do barn chores, and live simply among the pines for a very fair price.

name: Camp Dudley at Kiniya
description: The girls' campus of venerable Camp Dudley, set on Lake Champlain in Vermont, where 'The Other Fellow First' motto drives a culture of leadership, singing, and service.

name: Camp Duffield
description: A Baptist camp in the Cattaraugus County countryside near Delevan, south of Buffalo, offering low-cost gospel-centered weeks of swimming, crafts, and campfire worship for western New York kids.

name: Camp Eagle Hill
description: A relaxed Hudson Valley camp in Elizaville that runs a mainstream program side by side with a dedicated track for campers with learning differences, keeping the whole community genuinely mixed.

name: Camp Eagle
description: A remote adventure camp on the spring-fed Nueces River in the western Hill Country, big on mountain biking, climbing, and cliff-jumping alongside serious Christian teaching.

name: Camp Easterseals UCP
description: A fully accessible camp in the mountains near New Castle serving campers with physical and developmental disabilities, with adaptive riding, swimming, and zip-lining run by a specialized staff.

name: Camp Easton
description: The Inland Northwest Council's scout camp on Gotham Bay of Lake Coeur d'Alene, with a huge sailing and watersports program off its long dock. Merit badges with a million-dollar lake view.

name: Camp Echo (McGaw YMCA)
description: The Evanston McGaw YMCA's overnight camp on Ryerson Lake in Fremont, Michigan, mixing Chicago-area and Michigan kids in a warm, traditions-rich program running since 1907.

name: Camp Echo
description: A high-energy, well-appointed coed camp in Burlingham with top-tier facilities, spirited leagues, and a devoted Long Island and metro following that fills bunks years in advance.

name: Camp EDMO
description: A Bay Area STEAM day camp with sites across Northern California, mixing maker projects, coding, and nature play with an unusually deliberate social-emotional curriculum. A nonprofit that keeps sliding-scale spots at every site.

name: Camp Egan
description: A United Methodist camp on the clear Barren Fork Creek near Tahlequah, hosting gentle week-long sessions of creek wading, crafts, and vespers in the Cookson Hills. Quietly beloved across eastern Oklahoma.

name: Camp Ehawee
description: A Girl Scout camp in Wisconsin's driftless coulee country north of La Crosse, small and sweetly rustic with riding, a pond, and valley hikes.

name: Camp El Tesoro
description: Camp Fire's historic camp near Granbury has welcomed all kids since 1934, with horses, river canoeing, and a notable grief camp program alongside classic sessions. Inclusive, affordable, and quietly excellent.

name: Camp Elim
description: A traditional Bible camp near Pikes Peak running age-grouped weeks of games, chapel, and mountain adventure with a tight-knit summer staff. Simple, sincere, and well loved by regional church families.

name: Camp Elliott Barker
description: A rustic camp in the Sangre de Cristos near Angel Fire, long used for 4-H, scouting, and youth groups, offering affordable outdoor weeks of hiking, nature study, and campfires at high elevation.

name: Camp Emerald Bay
description: A storied Scout camp on the remote west end of Catalina Island, reachable only by boat, with scuba, sailing, and a working marine science lab on a private cove. One of the most distinctive waterfronts in Scouting.

name: Camp Emerson
description: A smaller, gentler Berkshires coed camp where kids pick their own schedules and quirky interests are celebrated. Known for being a soft landing for first-time and sensitive campers.

name: Camp Emunah
description: The first Chabad girls' overnight camp, founded in the 1950s in Greenfield Park, where chassidic warmth, dance heads, and grand productions define a joyful frum summer.

name: Camp Encore/Coda
description: A family-run music camp on Stearns Pond where serious ensemble rehearsal and private lessons share the day with swimming and sports - kids get real musical growth without giving up summer.

name: MathPath
description: A four-week summer program for mathematically gifted middle schoolers, hosted on rotating college campuses, where kids dive into proof-based math well beyond the school curriculum alongside hikes and games. A feeder into the elite high school math camps.

name: Camp Ewalu
description: A Lutheran camp in northeast Iowa's trout-stream country, with creek stomping, prairie restoration, and quiet, earthy faith formation in simple cabins.

name: SportsCenter of Connecticut Camp
description: A sports-focused day camp inside a huge indoor-outdoor complex in Shelton, with ice skating, batting cages, and turf fields keeping kids active rain or shine. Weekly enrollment and lots of variety.

name: Camp Fairlee
description: An Easterseals camp on Maryland's Eastern Shore serving children and adults with disabilities, with an accessible pool, adapted ropes course, and high-support programming in a wooded Kent County setting.

name: Camp Fantastic
description: The flagship week of the Special Love organization, a free camp for children with cancer held at the 4-H center in Front Royal with National Cancer Institute clinicians on site. Decades of campers have grown up and come back to volunteer.

name: Camp Farley
description: A nonprofit 4-H camp on Mashpee-Wakeby Pond offering day and overnight sessions with barn animals, archery, and pond swimming at genuinely accessible prices. Cape Cod camping without the Cape Cod price tag.

name: Camp Farnsworth
description: A historic Girl Scout camp on Lake Abenaki in Thetford, among the oldest in the country, where badge work meets classic Vermont lake-camp fun at a scout-council price.

name: Camp Farwell
description: One of America's oldest girls camps, tucked beside a pond in Newbury, with a strong riding program, a big international camper mix, and a cozy village-like campus.

name: Camp Fatima
description: A Catholic boys camp on Upper Suncook Lake run by the Diocese of Manchester, known for spirited traditions and its Exceptional Citizens' Week serving campers with disabilities. Solid value for a full sleepaway program.

name: Camp Favorite
description: Girl Scouts of Eastern Massachusetts' Cape Cod camp, with platform tents in the pines, kettle-pond swimming, and salty day adventures. Affordable outdoor sisterhood, badge book optional.

name: Camp Felix
description: The New York Foundling's free camp in Putnam Valley for children in foster care and under-resourced families, built around trauma-informed staff and the simple gift of a normal summer.

name: Camp Fellowship
description: A Presbyterian camp on the shores of Lake Greenwood, where sailing, banana-boat rides, and lakeside worship fill affordable week-long sessions for midlands and upstate kids.

name: Camp Fern
description: An East Texas classic on a cypress-ringed lake near Marshall, running separate boys' and girls' programs on one property since 1934. Sailing, riding, and tribe traditions carry a strong old-camp feel rare outside the Hill Country.

name: Camp Fernwood
description: A classic full-season girls' camp on Thompson Lake in Poland, family-run for over a century with strong riding, sailing, and a warm, tradition-soaked sisterhood.

name: Camp Fiddlecreek
description: A Girl Scout camp in the wooded hills near Labadie west of St. Louis, with a pool, climbing tower, and hiking trails threading the Missouri River bluffs. A friendly, affordable outdoor progression camp for area girls.

name: Camp Findley
description: A United Methodist camp on Findley Lake in New York's far southwest corner, offering low-cost weeks of tubing, crafts, and lakeside worship for families from the Erie-Chautauqua region.

name: Camp Firwood
description: The Firs' high-energy Christian camp on Lake Whatcom outside Bellingham, famous locally for wakeboarding, blob launches, and all-camp games. Counselor relationships are the heart of the week.

name: Fiver Children's Foundation Camp
description: A free camp in central New York that commits to kids from underserved NYC and upstate communities for ten full years — summer sessions plus year-round mentoring, college prep, and a fierce alumni family.

name: Camp Fleischmann
description: The Nevada Area Council's summer camp near Lake Almanor in the shadow of Lassen Peak, drawing troops from Reno and beyond for climbing, shooting sports, and mountain program.

name: Camp Fletcher
description: Camp Fire Alabama's historic camp in the woods southwest of Birmingham, running inclusive day camps (with some overnight tastes) around creeks, trails, and a strong everyone-belongs ethos since 1926.

name: Camp Fontanelle
description: A United Methodist camp in the timbered hills northwest of Omaha, known locally for its ziplines, treehouse village, and welcoming small-camp feel.

name: Camp For All
description: A fully barrier-free campus near Burton that partners with dozens of Texas nonprofits to give kids with illnesses and disabilities a true camp week — ziplines, horseback, and swimming all engineered for every body. A model facility of its kind.

name: Camp Forest Springs
description: An independent Bible camp on Spirit Lake in north-central Wisconsin known for intentional small-group discipleship, a strong staff-to-camper ratio, and solid waterfront facilities.

name: Camp Foss
description: A century-old girls camp on Bow Lake with a low-pressure, confidence-first culture — swimming, crafts, and campfire traditions handed down through generations of Foss girls.

name: Camp Foster YMCA
description: The classic Iowa Great Lakes YMCA camp on East Lake Okoboji, where cabin ball, lake swims, and hundred-year-old traditions define summer for kids across Iowa and beyond.

name: Camp Four Echoes
description: The Girl Scouts of Eastern Washington and Northern Idaho's lakeside camp on the southwest shore of Lake Coeur d'Alene, teaching generations of girls to swim, paddle, and lead. Pine woods, platform tents, and lake sunrises.

name: Camp Fowler
description: A welcoming Reformed Church camp on Lake Fowler near Speculator with week-long sessions, a strong creation-care ethos, and an intentionally inclusive, come-as-you-are community.

name: Camp Frank A. Day
description: The West Suburban YMCA's century-old overnight camp on Lake Quacumquasit, a mid-priced classic with strong waterfront and a big-hearted staff culture. A popular first sleepaway for Boston-area kids.

name: Camp Friedenswald
description: A Mennonite camp in the 'peaceful woods' of southwest Michigan near the Indiana border, known for its gentle community ethos, fen boardwalks, and singing that fills the dining hall.

name: Camp Friedlander
description: The Dan Beard Council's flagship camp east of Cincinnati, a polished operation on a private lake with a big aquatics tower, modern ranges, and one of the region's larger summer staffs.

name: Camp Friendship
description: A big international coed camp in central Virginia whose standout is a full equestrian center with its own dedicated riding program, plus a classic menu of lake, sports, and adventure activities. Popular with both local and overseas families.

name: Camp Furnace Hills
description: A Lancaster County Girl Scout camp in the wooded Furnace Hills, where riding and old-fashioned camp crafts fill the week for girls from Pennsylvania Dutch country.

name: Camp Galil
description: The Habonim Dror youth-movement camp in Bucks County, where campers help run the community, work a small farm, and soak up progressive Zionist values. Youth leadership is the point - teens basically run the place.

name: Camp Galil (Habonim Dror)
description: The Habonim Dror movement's Bucks County kibbutz-style camp, where campers share chores, vote in asefot, and absorb progressive Labor Zionist values along with the usual camp fun. Youth-led, scrappy, and idealistic since 1946.

name: Camp Galilee
description: An Episcopal camp right on the east shore of Lake Tahoe at Glenbrook, where campers kayak crystal-clear water, hike the Sierra, and end days with vespers on the beach.

name: Camp Gallagher
description: A teen sailing camp on Puget Sound's Key Peninsula where crews live aboard boats and tents, learn to skipper keelboats, and sail multi-day voyages. Small, salty, and beloved by kids who want real responsibility.

name: Camp Gan Israel (Parksville)
description: The flagship Chabad-Lubavitch overnight boys' camp in the Catskills, part of the worldwide Gan Israel network founded by the Rebbe, mixing chassidic learning and farbrengens with leagues, lake, and trips.

name: Camp Gan Israel Running Springs
description: Chabad of California's mountain overnight camp near Lake Arrowhead, running separate boys' and girls' sessions with chassidic warmth, mountain adventure, and the Gan Israel spirit for West Coast kids of every background.

name: Camp Garaywa
description: Mississippi Baptists' girls camp in Clinton, run by the WMU with missions-focused programming, pool time, and big sing-alongs at a price nearly any family can manage. A rite of passage for Baptist girls statewide.

name: Camp Geiger
description: The Pony Express Council's camp in the Missouri River bluffs north of St. Joseph, another proud keeper of the Mic-O-Say tradition with a tight, loyal camp community.

name: Camp Geneva
description: A Christian camp right on the Lake Michigan shore north of Holland, where beach sunsets, dune time, and joyful faith-filled weeks have drawn West Michigan families since 1937.

name: Camp Geronimo
