description: The Grand Canyon Council's flagship Scouts BSA camp beneath the Mogollon Rim, where Arizona troops spend merit-badge weeks among tall pines and spring-fed creeks that feel a world away from the desert.

name: Camp Ghormley
description: A nondenominational Christian camp in the pines near White Pass, west of Naches, serving Yakima Valley churches for decades. Simple cabins, mountain air, and earnest, affordable programming.

name: Camp Gifford
description: The Salvation Army's camp on Deer Lake north of Spokane, serving eastern Washington kids for a century with heavily subsidized week-long sessions. Swimming, boating, and a big-hearted staff.

name: Camp Gilboa
description: The West Coast home of Habonim Dror, a youth-led Labor Zionist movement camp in the Big Bear area where campers help run daily life and debate big ideas. Intentionally rustic, fiercely idealistic, and deeply beloved by its small community.

name: Camp Gilboa (Habonim Dror)
description: Habonim Dror's West Coast camp in the San Bernardino Mountains, where LA kids get communal meals, youth leadership, Hebrew culture, and progressive Zionist values at 7,000 feet. Intentionally small and camper-governed.

name: Camp Glen Arden
description: A small traditional girls camp near Lake Summit with a strong riding program and multi-generational traditions, including end-of-session banquets that alumnae still talk about decades later.

name: Camp Glen Brook
description: A Waldorf-inspired farm camp near Mount Monadnock where kids milk cows, blacksmith, swim in the pond, and live largely by hand and by season. Deeply calm, screen-free culture.

name: Camp Glisson
description: The North Georgia United Methodist camp at Cane Creek Falls near Dahlonega, where a waterfall runs right through camp and weeklong sessions blend faith, creek hikes, and small-group community. A rite of passage for Georgia Methodist kids.

name: Camp Glyndon
description: A Maryland camp for children with type 1 diabetes, run by the American Diabetes Association at a wooded site outside Baltimore, where medical staff manage the diabetes so kids can enjoy a full traditional camp week.

name: Camp Good Days and Special Times
description: A free camp on Keuka Lake for children touched by cancer — patients, survivors, and siblings — founded by a Rochester father in 1979 and staffed with medical support and boundless volunteers.

name: Camp Gorsuch
description: Alaska's main Scout camp on Mirror Lake outside Anchorage, where the midnight sun stretches campfire time and moose wander the edges of the archery range.

name: Camp Gorton
description: A Scout camp in the Finger Lakes hills near Dundee, west of Watkins Glen, serving regional troops with classic merit-badge programming, a lake waterfront, and shooting sports.

name: Camp Granite Lake
description: A newer private camp in Coal Creek Canyon with a private lake, elective-style scheduling, and a warm, inclusive culture run by veteran camp directors. Feels like an East Coast camp transplanted to the Rockies.

name: Camp Grant Walker
description: Louisiana's statewide 4-H camp in the piney woods near Pollock, where parish groups rotate through summer weeks of swimming, shooting sports, and hands-on ag and STEM tracks. For many rural Louisiana kids it's the first night ever spent away from home.

name: Camp Gravatt
description: An Episcopal camp in the sandhill pines near Aiken with a blackwater lake, high ropes, and a gentle chapel rhythm that has served midlands families for generations.

name: Camp Gray
description: The Diocese of Madison's Catholic camp near Devil's Lake, where energetic young staff lead a classic camp week wrapped in daily Mass, adoration, and a whole lot of joy.

name: Camp Green Cove
description: Mondamin's sister camp on Lake Summit, run with the same quietly radical philosophy: girls choose their own adventures, from mountaineering to serious whitewater, with no awards and no fuss.

name: Camp Green Lane
description: A century-old coed camp in Montgomery County with deep Philadelphia roots, strong bunk traditions, and a warm, culturally Jewish community feel. Many campers are children and grandchildren of alumni.

name: Camp Greenbrier for Boys
description: One of the oldest continuously operating camps in the country, a traditional boys camp on the Greenbrier River in the West Virginia mountains, emphasizing tennis, swimming, and old-fashioned sportsmanship.

name: Camp Greene Wood
description: A Girl Scout day camp on forested acres in DuPage County where girls build fires, shoot archery, and work through badge adventures close to home.

name: Camp Greentop
description: A historic accessible camp inside Catoctin Mountain Park, run by the League for People with Disabilities, serving campers with and without disabilities in fully adapted cabins built by the CCC in the 1930s.

name: Camp Grier
description: An affordable outdoor-adventure camp in the Old Fort mountains with deep mountain-biking and paddling roots, plus a mission of getting kids of all backgrounds into the backcountry.

name: Camp Grimes
description: The Mecklenburg County Council's mountain camp near Lake James in the South Mountains, giving Charlotte Scouts their week of ridgelines, ranges, and lake swims.

name: Camp Grossman (JCC Greater Boston)
description: JCC Greater Boston's flagship day camp on a wooded Westwood campus with pools, sports courts, and Shabbat traditions - a dependable summer home for families across Boston's southern suburbs.

name: Camp Grove Point
description: A Girl Scout resident camp on the Sassafras River where it meets the Chesapeake, with sailing, canoeing, and platform-tent living on a quiet Eastern Shore peninsula.

name: Camp Guggenheim
description: The Diocese of Ogdensburg's Catholic youth camp on Lower Saranac Lake, blending swimming, hikes, and guitar-led liturgies into affordable week-long sessions for teens.

name: Camp Haiastan
description: The oldest Armenian-American summer camp in the country, on Uncas Pond in Franklin, where campers from around the world connect with Armenian language, dance, and heritage alongside classic camp fun.

name: Camp Hale
description: A remarkable nonprofit camp on Squam Lake, run by United South End Settlements, that brings Boston kids to the lake at little or no cost — a 120-year tradition of access to the outdoors.

name: Camp Half Moon
description: A longtime Berkshires camp on Lake Buel offering both day and overnight programs with flexible session lengths. Traditional activities and a friendly, low-pressure vibe.

name: Camp Hamilton
description: A CYO camp on Lake Hannan outside Monroe, with 300 forested acres, a busy waterfront, and Catholic traditions woven through the week. Sister camp to Camp Don Bosco under the Archdiocese of Seattle.

name: Camp Hancock (YMCA of the Rockies)
description: The wilderness backpacking arm of YMCA of the Rockies' Estes Park Center, sending small crews of teens on multi-day treks into Rocky Mountain National Park and the surrounding high country.

name: Makemie Woods
description: A Presbyterian camp in the woods of tidewater Virginia between Richmond and Williamsburg, with a lake, low ropes, and gentle, welcoming faith formation.

name: Camp Hanover
description: A Presbyterian camp in the woods east of Richmond built around small-group 'village' living, creek explorations, and gentle, inclusive faith.

name: Camp Hantesa
description: A Camp Fire camp above the Des Moines River near Boone, running for a century with horse programs, crafts, and inclusive council-fire traditions for all genders.

name: Camp Happyland
description: The Salvation Army's camp near the Rapidan River, giving Washington-area kids — many on full scholarship — a joyful, heavily subsidized week of pool time, sports, and encouragement.

name: Camp Harbor View
description: A nearly free harbor-island day camp for Boston kids, built by city philanthropists, with boats ferrying campers to sailing, sports, and leadership programs each morning. One of the city's proudest summer institutions.

name: Camp Hardtner
description: The Episcopal Diocese of Western Louisiana's camp near Pollock, famous among its alumni for fierce lifelong loyalty, joyful liturgy, and a radically welcoming culture. Small-camp magic that Louisiana Episcopalians talk about for decades.

name: Hardtner Camp
description: The Episcopal Diocese of Western Louisiana's camp in the central Louisiana pines, with a strong small-group model, creek and pool, and warm inclusive worship.

name: Camp Harmony
description: A century-old Church of the Brethren camp along the Stonycreek River in Somerset County, running affordable faith-and-forest weeks with swimming, hiking, and old-fashioned campfire community.

name: Camp HASC
description: A famed Orthodox summer program in Parksville serving children and adults with developmental disabilities, staffed by hundreds of devoted counselors and known in the community simply as 'the happiest place on earth.'

name: Camp Havaya
description: The Reconstructionist movement's camp in the Poconos, famous for radical inclusivity - multiracial, LGBTQ-affirming, all abilities - wrapped in a creative, low-competition program. Every kid gets to be fully themselves.

name: YMCA Camp Hayo-Went-Ha
description: Michigan's oldest camp, on Torch Lake since 1904, where boys work up from cabin life to serious wilderness trips — Isle Royale backpacking and Canadian canoe expeditions included.

name: Camp Hazen YMCA
description: A century-old YMCA camp on Cedar Lake in the Connecticut River Valley with a huge waterfront, ropes courses, and a famously spirited staff. One of the state's most popular first sleepaway camps.

name: Incarnation Center Grace Camp
description: The youngest-camper program at Connecticut's historic Episcopal-founded Incarnation Center, easing first-timers into rustic lake life with shorter sessions.

name: Krislund Camp
description: A Presbyterian camp in central Pennsylvania's Penns Valley, with a swimming pool, creek, and gentle programming serving churches around State College.

name: Camp Hebron
description: A Mennonite camp on a mountain ridge north of Harrisburg with a well-loved horse program, night hikes, and quiet, service-hearted Christian culture.

name: Camp Hemlocks (Easterseals)
description: An Easterseals camp in Hebron built fully accessible from the ground up, serving campers with physical and developmental disabilities with an adapted waterfront and one-to-one support where needed.

name: Camp Henry
description: A Presbyterian-rooted camp on Kimball Lake near Newaygo with a great blob-and-boats waterfront and a big-hearted staff culture that draws kids from across West Michigan.

name: Camp Henry
description: A Presbyterian-rooted camp on the shore of stunning Lake Logan south of Canton, where mountain swims, canoe sunrises, and campfire worship make for an unhurried, welcoming week.

name: Camp Herrlich
description: A nonprofit camp on a reservoir-side woodland in Patterson that has quietly served NYC and Putnam County kids for nearly a century, with sliding-scale fees and a homey, unflashy program.

name: Camp Hess Kramer
description: Wilshire Boulevard Temple's historic canyon camp near the Malibu coast, a Reform community touchstone for generations of LA families. The 2018 Woolsey Fire hit the site hard, and its rebuild has been a labor of love.

name: Camp Hidden Meadows
description: A coed adventure camp high in the Monongahela National Forest, where campers choose from horseback riding, mountain biking, climbing, and caving in a remote, unplugged mountain setting.

name: Camp High Rocks
description: A small, mountaintop boys camp with a serious wilderness program — backpacking, paddling, climbing — plus a beloved riding barn. Intentionally noncompetitive, with a big-brother culture between age groups.

name: Camp Highland
description: A Christian adventure camp in the mountains near Ellijay built around wakeboarding, mountain biking, paintball, and high ropes, with intentional discipleship woven through the adrenaline. Teens especially love it.

name: Camp Highlands for Boys
description: Family-run since 1904 on Plum Lake, Highlands is one of America's oldest boys' camps, prizing woodcraft, canoe trips, and character over flash. Many campers are fourth- or fifth-generation.

name: Camp Highroad
description: A United Methodist camp in Virginia's horse country west of DC, with a strong equestrian program, pool, and welcoming faith-and-nature focus.

name: Camp Hilbert
description: The Weinstein JCC's day camp on a lakefront property west of Richmond, blending Jewish culture and Shabbat traditions with a full camp menu of swimming, sports, and arts.

name: Camp Hillard
description: One of Westchester's oldest and most established day camps, run by the same family for generations on a manicured Scarsdale campus. Daily swim instruction and a famously well-oiled program for younger kids.

name: Camp Veritans
description: One of North Jersey's oldest day camps, on a wooded Wayne campus with a lake, pools, and a full traditional program since the 1920s. Family-owned with deep roots in Passaic County.

name: Camp Hilltop
description: A century-old family-run camp in the western Catskills near Hancock with a private lake, riding stable, and a noticeably international camper body drawn by flexible session lengths.

name: Cathedral Camp Deerpark
description: An Episcopal Diocese of New York camp on the Neversink River in the lower Catskills, offering swimming, hiking, and inclusive chapel time for city and suburban kids.

name: West River UMC Camp
description: A United Methodist camp on the West River off the Chesapeake Bay in Maryland, with sailing, crabbing, and bayside vespers for church families near Annapolis.

name: Camp Wanake
description: A United Methodist camp in the rolling hills of eastern Ohio near Canton, with a horse program, farm, and welcoming, unhurried faith formation.

name: Solomon Schechter Day Camp
description: A warm Jewish day camp run by Westchester's Schechter school, mixing traditional camp fun with Hebrew, Israeli culture, and Shabbat celebrations. Kosher and observant in flavor, welcoming to families across the community.

name: Camp Ho Mita Koda
description: The oldest camp in the world for children with type 1 diabetes, founded in 1929 by the doctor who helped bring insulin to Cleveland patients. Revived by the community in recent years, it keeps its historic wooded campus and mission.

name: Camp Ho Non Wah
description: A Lowcountry camp under the live oaks and Spanish moss of Wadmalaw Island near Charleston, where crabbing docks and saltmarsh kayaking replace the usual mountain lake.

name: Camp Hobé
description: A nearly free camp for Utah kids with cancer and their siblings, run each summer in Settlement Canyon near Tooele with full medical support and a joyful, normalizing week of classic camp.

name: Camp Hoblitzelle
description: The Salvation Army's large camp south of Dallas, giving thousands of under-resourced Metroplex kids free or near-free weeks of swimming, ropes courses, and character-building each summer. A quiet engine of opportunity.

name: Camp Hochelaga
description: A century-old YWCA girls camp on Lake Champlain's South Hero island, where affordable sessions, big-sister traditions, and swims off the rocky point build lifelong 'Hochelaga girls'.

name: Camp Hoffman
description: Girl Scouts of Southern New England's century-old camp on Larkin Pond, with platform tents, canoeing, and campfire sisterhood at prices working families can manage.

name: Camp Holiday Trails
description: A Charlottesville camp for children with medical needs — from diabetes to heart conditions — with a med team backed by the nearby UVA health system and a pay-what-you-can pricing philosophy. Horses, pool, and ropes course included.

name: Camp Hollis
description: Oswego County's children's camp on a bluff over Lake Ontario, founded by a family-court judge to guarantee every local kid a week of camp regardless of income — sunsets included.

name: Camp Hollymont for Girls
description: A Christian girls camp near Asheville with polished facilities on a private-school campus feel, offering dance, riding, and lake days alongside gentle faith emphasis.

name: Camp Hollywoodland
description: The girls' counterpart to Griffith Park Boys' Camp, a city-run sleepaway camp in a canyon below the Hollywood sign where LA girls have sung campfire songs since 1925. Affordable, historic, and improbably wild for its zip code.

name: Camp Honey Creek for Girls
description: A family-owned girls' camp where Honey Creek meets the Guadalupe, running tribe traditions, horseback riding, and river swims since the 1950s. Smaller and homier than the marquee Hunt camps, with a loyal multi-generation following.

name: Shrine Mont Camps
description: The Episcopal Diocese of Virginia's camps at Shrine Mont in the Shenandoah mountains, with several age-based sites, an open-air cathedral, and a beloved inclusive tradition.

name: Camp Hopewell
description: A small Presbyterian camp in the Lafayette County hills near Oxford, offering laid-back weeks of lake swimming, crafts, and community, plus day options for younger kids. Intentionally small and gentle.

name: Camp Horizons
description: A dedicated camp in eastern Connecticut for children and young adults with developmental disabilities, with high staffing ratios and a program built entirely around each camper's abilities.

name: Camp Horizons
description: A coed adventure camp in the hills north of Harrisonburg with a strong international staff, ropes courses, horses, and a flexible elective program on a former farm property.

name: Camp Horseshoe
description: A high-energy boys' sports camp on Pelican Lake near Rhinelander with polished facilities, spirited color-war style competition, and a largely Chicago-area camper base.

name: Camp Horseshoe
description: A Scout reservation on Horseshoe Run in the Monongahela National Forest of Tucker County, hosting troops for merit-badge weeks with strong aquatics, shooting sports, and mountain high adventure.

name: Camp Howard
description: CYO Portland's Catholic camp in the woods near Corbett east of the city, with a pool, sports fields, and outdoor Mass under the firs. A steady, affordable tradition for Oregon parish families.

name: Camp Howe
description: A 4-H-rooted community camp in the Hampshire hills known for genuinely including kids of every ability and background. Affordable, rustic, and unusually well trained in supporting different needs.

name: Camp Huawni
description: A family-run coed camp in the deep East Texas pines near Timpson, built around a spring-fed lake, homemade traditions, and three generations of the same founding family. Unpretentious, warm, and loved by its regulars.

name: Camp Huntington
description: A co-ed camp in High Falls for children and young adults with developmental disabilities, blending recreation with individualized life-skills and social goals in a leafy Hudson Valley setting.

name: Camp Huston
description: The Episcopal Diocese of Olympia's camp in the Cascade foothills at Gold Bar, offering a gentle, inclusive week of crafts, creek hikes, and evening chapel. Known for its warm, low-pressure spirituality.

name: Camp Id-Ra-Ha-Je
description: A large, long-running Bible camp in the mountains near Bailey — the name stands for 'I'd Rather Have Jesus' — with horses, a blob lake, and old-fashioned camp meetings at a budget-friendly price.

name: Camp Illahee
description: A classic girls camp in a green Brevard valley, known for warm traditions, strong riding and gymnastics programs, and multi-generational loyalty. 'Heavenly world' is the name's translation, and alumnae take that seriously.

name: Camp Illiana
description: A Christian church camp in southwest Indiana serving congregations across Indiana and Illinois for 75-plus years, with lake activities and week-long themed sessions.

name: YMCA Camp Ingersoll
description: A big, well-run YMCA day camp on a pond in Portland known for its ropes courses, spirited staff, and week-by-week flexibility. Regularly cited among central Connecticut's favorite day camps.

name: Camp Interlaken JCC
description: Milwaukee JCC's coed camp on Finger Lake in Eagle River, blending a full Northwoods program with joyful, song-filled Jewish community — Shabbat on the lake is the weekly highlight.

name: Camp Invention
description: The National Inventors Hall of Fame's weeklong day program, run by local teachers at thousands of schools nationwide from its Ohio headquarters. Kids take apart machines, prototype inventions, and get comfortable with creative failure.

name: Camp Io-Dis-E-Ca
description: A Lutheran (LCMS) camp near Coralville Lake outside Iowa City, offering traditional cabin weeks with catechism-flavored teaching, canoeing, and campfires.

name: Camp Istrouma
description: A United Methodist camp on the Amite River outside Baton Rouge, running affordable week-long sessions of canoeing, campfires, and worship for kids across southeast Louisiana. A humble, well-loved local fixture.

name: Camp Iti Kana
description: Girl Scouts of Greater Mississippi's resident camp in the pines near Wiggins, offering budget-friendly weeks of swimming, crafts, and camp-out skills for girls across south Mississippi.

name: Camp Jabberwocky
description: A legendary Martha's Vineyard camp for people with disabilities, famous for its raucous theater productions, beach days, and the whole island cheering its parades. Heavily subsidized and deeply loved.

name: Camp Jack Hazard
description: A rustic high-Sierra camp at 6,000 feet on the Stanislaus River near Sonora Pass, where wilderness backpacking trips into the Emigrant Wilderness are the heart of the program. Nonprofit-run, affordable, and deliberately unplugged.

name: Camp Jacobson
description: The Sid Jacobson JCC's day camp on a green East Hills campus, blending classic camp activities with a warm Jewish community feel and strong inclusion programming. Flexible sessions make it popular with North Shore families.

name: Camp JCA Shalom
description: A warm, pluralistic Jewish camp in the Malibu hills run by the Shalom Institute, with an organic farm, animal center, and decades of LA families in its DNA. Down-to-earth rather than flashy.

name: Camp Jeanne d'Arc
description: A century-old girls' camp on Upper Chateaugay Lake run by the same family for generations, with waterskiing, riding, and a warm big-sister culture that keeps campers coming back.

name: Camp John Currie 4-H Camp
description: Kentucky 4-H's western camp right on Kentucky Lake, giving Purchase-area kids affordable weeks of swimming, fishing, and hands-on nature classes with their county 4-H groups.

name: Camp John Marc
description: A Texas Hill Country-style camp near Dallas hosting week-long medical specialty sessions in partnership with children's hospitals — spina bifida week, burn survivors week, and more — at little to no cost to families.

name: Camp Johnsonburg
description: A Presbyterian camp in northwest New Jersey's hills, prized for its inclusive small-group model, lake fun, and campers who return as counselors year after year.

name: Camp Jordan
description: The Down East Family YMCA's overnight camp on Branch Lake near Ellsworth, a friendly and affordable Downeast option with a classic waterfront and easy trips toward Acadia.

name: Camp JORI
description: Rhode Island's Jewish overnight camp on Worden Pond, small enough that every camper is known and warm enough that families call it mishpacha. Strong scholarship culture and a gentle, inclusive vibe.

name: Camp Jorn YMCA
description: A welcoming coed YMCA camp on Rest Lake in Manitowish Waters offering classic Northwoods sessions at Y prices, with a rebuilt modern lodge and a strong scholarship tradition.

name: Camp Joslin
description: The Barton Center's brother camp for boys with type 1 diabetes, pairing a full woodsy camp program with round-the-clock medical support. Campers leave more confident managing their diabetes than many adults.

name: Camp Judaea
description: A Zionist, Israel-centered Jewish camp in the NC mountains where Hebrew songs, Israeli counselors, and strong ruach (spirit) infuse classic camp activities. Draws kids from across the Southeast.

name: Camp Judson
description: An American Baptist camp in the Black Hills near Mount Rushmore, where mountain creek play and gospel-centered weeks come with granite views.

name: Camp Juliette Low
description: An independent girls camp on Lookout Mountain founded in 1922 in the spirit of the Girl Scouts' founder, with platform tents, campfires, and a real unplugged outdoor ethic. Girls gain grit here without competition or fuss.

name: Camp Kabeyun
description: A fully elective, noncompetitive boys camp on Lake Winnipesaukee where campers choose their own days — sailing, woodshop, tennis, or just reading on the point. Gentle, old-fashioned, and beloved by quieter kids.

name: Camp Kachina
description: A Girl Scout camp on the cedar-lined shores of Lake Belton offering horseback, canoeing, and classic outdoor skill progression at Scout-friendly prices. Rustic platform tents and cabins keep the focus on nature.

name: Camp Kahdalea for Girls
description: A family-run Christian girls' camp near Brevard with a serious wilderness program — backpacking, climbing, whitewater — wrapped in gentle, joyful traditions (brother camp: Chosatonga).

name: Camp Kamaji
description: A century-old girls' camp on Lake Washburn in northern Minnesota with a full-choice program, a fierce culture of sisterhood, and alumnae who talk about it like a second home.

name: Camp Kanuga
description: The historic Episcopal camp at Kanuga near Hendersonville, blending mountain-lake tradition with an inclusive, questioning-friendly Christian spirit.

name: Camp Kaufmann
description: Girl Scouts Heart of the Hudson's big resident camp in Dutchess County, with a pool, ponds, horses, and a gentle on-ramp for first-time campers from the metro area.

name: Camp Kawaga
description: One of the oldest boys' camps in the Northwoods, sitting on Lake Kawaguesaga since 1915, blending serious athletics with wilderness trips and a deep well of songs and traditions.

name: Camp Kawanhee
description: A nonprofit boys' camp on Webb Lake beneath Mount Blue, alumni-run since 1920. Log lodges, wooden sailboats, mountain trips, and a loyal multi-generational community in a truly beautiful corner of western Maine.

name: Camp Kaylie
description: An Orthodox camp in Wurtsboro built on full inclusion, where campers with and without disabilities share bunks and activities on a modern campus with a stunning aquatics center.

name: Camp Kennedy Space Center
description: A weeklong day camp run inside the Kennedy Space Center Visitor Complex, with rocket builds, astronaut encounters, and behind-the-scenes access most tourists never get. For Space Coast families it's a summer rite of passage.

name: Camp Kennybrook
description: A family-owned Catskills camp on a private lake in Monticello where generations of the same families return for team sports, lake days, and a famously tight-knit bunk culture.

name: Camp Ker-Anna
description: A Catholic day camp on the grounds of the Diocese of Providence in Cumberland, mixing pool time, sports, and gentle faith formation. A northern Rhode Island summer tradition for decades.

name: Camp Kettleford
description: The Girl Scouts' woodsy day camp in Bedford where girls build fires, learn archery, and earn badges under the pines — no scout membership experience needed to feel at home.

name: Camp Kickapoo
description: The Andrew Jackson Council's Scout camp on the Natchez Trace side of Clinton, hosting summer day camps and troop weeks with archery, BMX-style fun, and piney-woods scouting close to Jackson.

name: Camp Kieve
description: A storied boys' camp on Damariscotta Lake famous for its progression of wilderness trips - by the teen years boys are running Allagash rivers and Maine peaks. Character education is baked into everything.

name: Camp Killooleet
description: A single-session coed camp in the Green Mountains long run by the Seeger family, where folk music, banjos by the campfire, and a gentle egalitarian ethos define the summer.

name: Camp Killoqua
description: Camp Fire Snohomish County's lakeside camp on private Crabapple Lake near Stanwood, with 185 acres of woods and wetlands. A gentle, community-minded camp that many local families attend for generations.

name: Camp Kilowan
description: Camp Fire Willamette's woodsy camp on Teal Creek in the Coast Range near Dallas, Oregon, with creek play, crafts, and nearly a century of song-heavy Camp Fire tradition. Small, inclusive, and dearly loved.

name: Camp Kinard
description: A Lutheran camp in the South Carolina midlands near Leesville, hosting gentle week-long summer sessions of crafts, pool time, and Lutheran hymn-singing under the pines.

name: Camp Kinder Ring
description: The Workers Circle's historic camp on Sylvan Lake, where secular Yiddish culture, social-justice values, and classic waterfront summers have mixed for nearly a century.

name: Camp Kinderland
description: A century-old secular Jewish culture camp with proudly progressive roots, where social justice themes mix with swimming, arts, and classic bunk life. Rustic, idealistic, and utterly distinctive.

name: Camp Kingfisher
description: The Chattahoochee Nature Center's summer day camp in Roswell, where kids canoe the river, meet resident raptors and reptiles, and get properly muddy. A favorite for animal-obsessed Atlanta kids.

name: Camp Kingswood
description: A JCC-affiliated Jewish coed camp on Woods Pond that keeps tuition and pretension lower than the famous private Maine camps, with kosher dining and a heimish, welcoming community.

name: Camp Kiniya
description: Camp Dudley's sister camp for girls on Lake Champlain in Vermont, sharing the same character-first Christian values, waterfront tradition, and vespers.

name: Camp Kinneret
description: A classic family-run day camp on a shady Agoura Hills campus, running since 1953 with pools, ropes courses, and traditions that many LA parents attended themselves. Buses cover much of the west Valley and Conejo corridor.

name: Camp Kippewa
description: A small girls' camp on Cobbosseecontee Lake in Monmouth that offered flexible session lengths and a personal, family-camp feel with strong riding and waterfront programs.

name: Camp Kirby
description: A small Camp Fire camp occupying the sandy tip of Samish Island between Padilla and Samish Bays, north of Mount Vernon. Tide flats, driftwood forts, and open-air sleeping shelters make it a throwback in the best way.

name: Camp Abnaki
description: A YMCA boys' camp on Lake Champlain's Grand Isle since 1901, blending classic Vermont waterfront with character-building rooted in Christian YMCA values.

name: Kivu
description: A Durango adventure camp for teens that pairs rafting, wakeboarding, and San Juan mountain trips with honest conversations about faith rather than canned answers.

name: Camp Knutson
description: A Lutheran Social Service camp on Whitefish Lake in Crosslake that hosts specialty weeks for kids with autism, skin conditions, heart disease, and Down syndrome — beautiful facilities, expert medical support, and pure acceptance.

name: Camp Kohahna
description: The sister camp to Camp Leelanau near Sleeping Bear Dunes, where girls ride, sail, and beach-hike along Lake Michigan within a warm community grounded in Christian Science values.

name: Camp Men-O-Lan
description: A Mennonite/BIC camp in the Bucks County countryside north of Philadelphia, offering lake swims, low ropes, and quiet, service-minded Christian community.

name: Camp Kooch-i-ching
description: A storied island wilderness camp on Rainy Lake where boys progress from in-camp woodcraft to epic canoe expeditions across Ontario and Manitoba — one of the great North American tripping camps.

name: Camp Kootaga
description: A longtime Scout camp on the Little Kanawha River in Wood County, West Virginia, where troops earn merit badges through canoeing, shooting sports, and river-country outdoor skills.

name: Camp Korey
description: A SeriousFun Children's Network camp in Mount Vernon serving kids with serious medical conditions, entirely free to families, with full medical staffing behind the scenes. Adaptive archery, boating, and pure camp joy.

name: Camp Kostopulos
description: Utah's premier camp for people with disabilities, in Emigration Canyon minutes from Salt Lake City, with adaptive horseback riding, ropes, and arts backed by decades of specialized staff experience.

name: Camp Krietenstein
description: A quieter western-Indiana camp on a small lake near Terre Haute, where a modest size means first-year Scouts get plenty of individual attention.

name: Camp KSC
description: Kennedy Space Center's weeklong day camp where kids build and launch rockets, train like astronauts, and tour real launch facilities on the Space Coast. A bucket-list week for STEM kids.

name: Camp Kudzu
description: Georgia's camp for children with type 1 diabetes, running weeklong sessions at partner campuses like Camp Twin Lakes with endocrinologists and nurses embedded in every cabin group. Scholarships keep it accessible.

name: Camp Kulaqua
description: The Seventh-day Adventist camp and retreat center at Hornsby Spring near High Springs, with a huge water park zone, horses, and even a small zoo, all alongside vegetarian dining and worship. Facility-rich for a church camp.

name: Camp Kupugani
description: A deliberately multicultural camp in northern Illinois that centers communication skills, empowerment, and diversity alongside ropes courses and river fun, with girls-only and coed sessions.

name: Camp Kweebec
description: A longtime coed camp outside Philadelphia in the Perkiomen Valley, close enough for easy drop-offs but with a full traditional sleepaway program of sports, pool, and theater. A Philly-area staple for generations.

name: Camp La Jita
description: Southwest Texas's Girl Scout camp on the spring-fed Sabinal River in the Hill Country near Utopia — cypress-shaded swimming holes make it one of the prettiest camps in the state.

name: Camp La Junta
description: A boys' camp on the Guadalupe near Hunt that stands out in the Hill Country for its noncompetitive philosophy — boys choose their own activities and success is measured personally, not on a scoreboard. Family-run since 1928.

name: Camp La Llanada
description: A Girl Scout resident camp on 360 scrubby lakeside acres near Lake Wales, giving central Florida girls affordable weeks of paddling, archery, and starry-night unit living.

name: Camp La-No-Che
description: Central Florida's big Scout camp on the edge of the Ocala National Forest, with a spring-fed lake, huge aquatics program, and enough zip lines and ranges to fill several summers.

name: Camp Ladore
description: The Salvation Army's big lakeside camp in the northern Poconos, providing low-cost and often free camp weeks to kids from eastern Pennsylvania cities. Wholesome program, huge lake, and a mission-first heart.

name: Camp Lakamaga
description: Girl Scouts River Valleys' century-old camp on Big Marine Lake near Stillwater, a Minnesota classic of canoes, cabins, and loon-serenaded campfires.

name: Camp Lake Greeley
description: A flexible-session coed camp wrapped around its own Pocono lake, with a broad mix of sports, arts, and water activities. Popular with families who want a traditional camp without committing to a full seven weeks.

name: Camp Lake Hubert
description: The sister camp to Camp Lincoln, across the lake, giving girls the same broad elective menu — waterski, tennis, riding trips — with shared brother-sister events sprinkled through the summer.

name: Camp Lake Stephens
description: The United Methodist camp on a wooded lake outside Oxford, serving north Mississippi with weeklong sessions of canoeing, ropes course, and campfire worship. Comfortably traditional and reasonably priced.

name: Lakeview Ministries
description: A Lutheran (LCMS) camp on a lake in southern Indiana, serving church families around Indianapolis and Louisville with swimming, canoeing, and Bible study.

name: Camp Lakota
description: A big, energetic coed camp on sandy-bottomed Masten Lake in the Catskills, running for 90-plus years with go-karts, waterskiing, gymnastics, and a full theater alongside classic bunk life.

name: Camp Lakota
description: Girl Scouts of Greater Los Angeles' mountain camp near Frazier Park, a pine-forest world of horses, archery, and campfires barely ninety minutes from the city.

name: Camp Lanakila
description: The Aloha Foundation's boys camp on Lake Morey, where kindness is coached as deliberately as archery, and woodworking, trips, and campfire rituals fill screen-free weeks.

name: Camp Laney
description: A boys camp on Lookout Mountain in Mentone with a structured, sports-forward program, river swimming under the falls, and famously loyal multigenerational families. Simple cabins, big traditions.

name: Camp Lantern Creek
description: A small girls' camp north of Houston built around creativity and voice — songwriting, pottery, archery, and messy fun — with an intentionally body-positive, no-mean-girls culture. A haven for artsy kids who dread sports camps.

name: Camp Lassen
description: A northern California Scout camp in the pines near Lassen's volcanic country, serving troops and Cub families from the Sacramento Valley. Mountain biking, shooting sports, and cool mountain air.

name: Camp Laurel (Girl Scouts of Connecticut)
description: Girl Scouts of Connecticut's overnight camp in rural Lebanon, with platform tents, archery, and lake days at classic Girl Scout prices. No troop membership drama required - any girl can come.

name: Camp Laurel South
description: A high-amenity coed camp on Crescent Lake running two four-week sessions packed with top-shelf facilities and nonstop programming - the full-throttle Maine camp experience in a half-summer format.

name: Camp Laurelwood
description: Connecticut's only Jewish overnight camp, tucked in the woods near the Madison shoreline with a lake, sports, and Shabbat traditions that feel like extended family. Mid-sized, warm, and unpretentious.

name: Camp Lavi
description: A popular Modern Orthodox camp in the northern Poconos with parallel boys' and girls' divisions, lively color war, and a warm religious Zionist atmosphere that draws heavily from the New York area.

name: Camp Lawroweld
description: A Seventh-day Adventist camp on Webb Lake near Mount Blue offering horseback riding, waterskiing, and mountain adventure in affordable week-long sessions.

name: Camp Lebanon
description: A warm, well-run Bible camp on Cedar Lake near Upsala, central Minnesota, where week-long youth camps mix lake fun with earnest, relationship-first ministry.

name: Camp Ledgewood
description: Northeast Ohio's Girl Scout camp inside the Cuyahoga Valley, where ledges, waterfalls, and national-park trails give Cleveland and Akron girls a wild backyard.

name: Camp Lee
description: A Christian camp in the Choccolocco foothills near Anniston hosting summer youth weeks with a lake blob, ropes courses, and energetic worship. A dependable east Alabama church-camp pick.

name: Camp Leelanau for Boys
description: A boys' camp near Sleeping Bear Dunes rooted in Christian Science principles, pairing Lake Michigan adventures — surfing days included — with a thoughtful, character-first culture.

name: Camp Leif Ericson
description: Sioux Falls' iconic YMCA day camp along the Big Sioux River, a rite of passage for generations of local kids with its Viking lore, archery, and river-woods adventures.

name: Camp Lenox
description: A century-old sports-focused coed camp on the Shaw Pond shoreline in the southern Berkshires. Serious coaching in a spirited, traditional camp wrapper.

name: Camp Li-Lo-Li
description: A Christian camp in the Allegheny foothills near Randolph offering affordable week sessions where creek swims and cabin devotions go hand in hand — 'Living Life for the Lord' is right in the name.

name: Camp Li-Wa
description: Interior Alaska's Christian horse camp outside Fairbanks, where campers ride under near-24-hour summer daylight and help with a working stable. The go-to overnight camp for Fairbanks-area families.

name: Camp Libbey
description: Northwest Ohio's Girl Scout camp on the wooded banks of the Maumee River near Defiance, a 1930s-vintage camp with riding and river ecology built into the week.

name: Camp Lincoln
description: A boys' camp on Lake Hubert in the Brainerd Lakes area, paired with Camp Lake Hubert for girls, running since 1909 with a big elective program and a polished, family-owned operation.

name: Camp Lincoln
description: One half of North Country Camps, a small, deliberately low-key boys' camp on Augur Lake where wilderness trips, swimming, and unplugged simplicity have barely changed in a hundred years.

name: Camp Lindenmere
description: A fully elective coed camp in the Poconos where campers build their own day, with unusually strong music, circus, and arts programs alongside standard sports. Attracts creative kids who want freedom of choice.

name: Camp Little Notch
description: A former Girl Scout camp on its own private lake near Fort Ann, revived by alumnae as an independent outdoors camp for girls and gender-expansive youth, with a proudly rustic, empowering culture.

name: Camp Live Oak
description: A nature-based day camp operating inside Fort Lauderdale's state parks, where campers kayak, snorkel, and study coastal ecology with the beach as their classroom. A standout outdoor option in urban South Florida.

name: Camp Livingston
description: The Cincinnati Jewish community's century-old overnight camp in southeastern Indiana, with a lake, Israeli culture woven through the day, and a haimish, everyone-belongs feel.

name: Camp Lokanda
description: A classic family-owned camp on Lake Louise Marie near Glen Spey, mixing traditional bunk life and lake sports with a flexible schedule that lets kids sample everything.

name: Camp Loll
description: A famously rustic Scouts BSA camp on Grassy Lake near Yellowstone's southern boundary, reached by a long dirt road and beloved for pristine wilderness, waterfall hikes, and old-school scout spirit.

name: Camp Loll
description: A famously rustic camp on Lake of the Woods near the southwest corner of Yellowstone, beloved for its wilderness ethic, waterfall hikes, and staff traditions. No electricity in campsites, no regrets.

name: Camp Lone Star
description: A Lutheran (LCMS) camp on the Colorado River between Houston and Austin, serving Texas Lutheran families with classic cabin weeks, river fun, and solid Bible study.

name: Camp Lonehollow
description: A newer, amenity-rich coed camp on the Sabinal River headwaters near Lost Maples, with striking lodge architecture and a huge elective menu from ziplines to filmmaking. It offers the Hill Country experience with a distinctly modern polish.

name: Camp LongRidge
description: A Christian camp on a private lake near Ridgeway with wakeboarding, blobs, and paintball wrapped around energetic worship nights — a midlands favorite for church groups and families.

name: Camp Lookout
description: A small United Methodist camp on the brow of Lookout Mountain in Georgia's far northwest corner, with sunset overlooks, caving trips, and a gentle, community-first spirit. Affordable weeklong sessions run all summer.

name: Camp Lou Henry Hoover
description: A northern New Jersey Girl Scout camp on Swartswood Lake named for the former First Lady who led the Girl Scouts — lake swims, platform tents, and decades of Jersey campfire tradition.

name: Camp Loucon
description: The United Methodist camp in the woods near Nolin Lake, offering gentle weeklong sessions - including special-needs weeks - with a small lake, crafts, and vespers under the trees. Kindness is the brand.

name: Camp Louemma
description: A traditional family-owned coed camp in the hills of Sussex County running flexible sessions with pools, lake activities, and big color-war energy. A lower-key, closer-to-home alternative to the Poconos giants.

name: Camp Loughridge
description: Tulsa's classic Christian day camp on wooded acres with a private lake in the middle of the city, where kids canoe, zip, and hear a daily devotion before coming home for dinner. Multi-week Tulsa tradition for many families.

name: Camp Louise
description: A historic Jewish girls camp on Catoctin Mountain in western Maryland, paired with brother Camp Airy, known for a strong performing-arts program and a century of sisterhood traditions.

name: Camp Low Country
description: The Girl Scout camp of the lowcountry, set among live oaks and Spanish moss near the old Santee Canal, with horses, paddling, and swampy nature study in true coastal-Carolina style.

name: Camp Loyaltown
description: A Catskills camp in the shadow of Hunter Mountain serving children and adults with intellectual and developmental disabilities, with high staffing ratios, adaptive activities, and decades of devoted return campers.

name: Camp Lurecrest
description: A Christian camp on the shore of Lake Lure where wakeboarding, blob wars, and mountain views come with energetic gospel-centered teaching. Draws heavily from Charlotte-area churches.

name: Camp Luther
description: Nebraska's LCMS Lutheran camp near Schuyler, offering week-long Christ-centered sessions with a pond, sports fields, and family-camp traditions on the side.

name: Camp Luther
description: A Lutheran camp on Spirit Lake in Three Lakes serving Wisconsin congregations for generations, with classic cabin weeks, family camps, and lots of lake time woven around daily worship.

name: Camp Lutherhaven
description: A long-running Lutheran camp on Lake Coeur d'Alene in the Idaho panhandle, with a busy waterfront, high ropes, and a large summer program serving the Inland Northwest.

name: Camp Lutherlyn
description: A large Lutheran camp near Butler with a well-known equestrian center, an environmental education site, and creative specialty weeks alongside classic cabin camp. A western-PA church camp with real program depth.

name: Lutherlyn
description: A large ELCA camp north of Pittsburgh with an environmental education center, planetarium, horses, and generations of western Pennsylvania Lutheran campers.

name: Camp Lutherwood Washington
description: A small Lutheran camp on Lake Samish south of Bellingham with a gentle, welcoming faith culture and lots of canoe time. Progressive, inclusive, and proudly unflashy.

name: Camp Ma-He-Tu
description: An affordable girls' camp on a lake inside Harriman State Park, run by a nonprofit women's organization for generations — cabin life, lake swims, and confidence-building an hour from Manhattan.

name: Camp Mac
description: A family-owned brother-sister camp at the foot of the Talladega Mountains, where lake mornings, riflery, and horse rides run on cherished routines passed down since 1948. Alabama families are famously loyal to it.

name: Camp Alexander Mack
description: A Church of the Brethren camp on Waubee Lake in northern Indiana, offering canoeing, crafts, and peace-church community for nearly a century.

name: Camp Magruder
description: The United Methodist camp squeezed between Lake Magruder and the Pacific at Rockaway Beach, with ocean-edge campfires and an open-hearted Methodist welcome. Coastal church camp at its most scenic.

name: Camp Mah-Kee-Nac
description: A classic Berkshires boys camp on Stockbridge Bowl where sports are taken seriously but brotherhood and tradition matter more. Generations of families return for the color wars and lakefront summers.

name: Camp Mak-A-Dream
description: A cost-free Montana camp near Gold Creek for children and young adults affected by cancer, offering medically supported weeks of art, adventure, and community in a beautiful mountain valley.

name: Camp Maluhia
description: Maui's long-running scout camp on the slopes of the West Maui Mountains above Wailuku, hosting summer scout weeks with ocean views, hiking, and traditional camp skills. A Maui scouting fixture since the 1920s.

name: Camp Manatoc
description: A beloved Depression-era camp surrounded by Cuyahoga Valley National Park, with WPA-built lodges, a storied dining hall, and pine forests that feel far wilder than its Akron address suggests.

name: Camp Manito-wish YMCA
description: A legendary YMCA wilderness camp in Boulder Junction where every camper goes on trail — canoeing, backpacking, or kayaking — with older teens taking multi-week expeditions to Canada and beyond. Leadership development is the point of everything.

name: Camp Manitoqua
description: A Christian camp in the south Chicago suburbs running popular summer day camps (plus overnight options) with ropes courses, sports, and gentle faith content on 90 wooded acres.

name: Camp Maranatha
description: A Christian camp and retreat center tucked into Idyllwild's pines, serving church youth camps each summer with campfires, hikes, and mountain air. Modest facilities, sincere community.

name: Camp Mardela
description: A Church of the Brethren camp on the Eastern Shore near the Choptank River, offering simple, affordable, peace-church-rooted weeks of crafts, swimming, and campfire community.

name: Camp Mariah
description: The Fresh Air Fund's career-awareness camp at Sharpe Reservation in Fishkill, championed by Mariah Carey, where NYC middle-schoolers pair classic camp with career exploration — all completely free.

name: Camp Marimeta
description: A spirited girls' camp on Meta Lake with strong gymnastics, dance, and waterski programs and a devoted, largely Midwest camper community built over generations.

name: Camp Marin Sierra
description: The Marin Council's high-country camp off I-80 near Emigrant Gap, with a small lake, granite country hikes, and a laid-back program that leaves room for troop time. A quiet Sierra classic.

name: Camp Maripai
description: The Girl Scouts–Arizona Cactus-Pine council's beloved camp near Prescott, mixing horses, crafts, and pine-forest adventures with decades of songs and traditions.

name: Camp Marist
description: A Catholic coed camp on Ossipee Lake run by the Marist Brothers, mixing a big international camper community with lake sports, riding, and gentle faith traditions.

name: Camp Mark Seven
description: A rare fully ASL-immersive camp for deaf, hard-of-hearing, and coda youth on Fourth Lake near Old Forge, where campers get a classic Adirondack waterfront summer in their own language.

name: Camp Marshall
description: Worcester County's 4-H camp on Lake Whittemore, best known for its horseback riding program and barnyard full of animals. Unpretentious, affordable, classic New England camping.

name: Camp Marshall
description: A Presbyterian camp on the south shore of Flathead Lake near Polson, best known for its horse program and lakefront setting, serving Montana church families for generations.

name: Camp Mary Atkinson
description: North Carolina Coastal Pines' longtime resident camp near Raleigh, with a lake, pool, and horses serving girls from across the eastern half of the state.

name: Camp Mary White
description: A historic Girl Scout camp in the pine forests of the Sacramento Mountains near Cloudcroft, where girls ride, hike, and sleep in rustic units the way campers here have for nearly a century.

name: Camp Marydale
description: A Girl Scout camp in the rolling Felicianas near St. Francisville, best known for its horse program and shaded creek-bottom trails. Generations of Baton Rouge-area girls learned to ride here.

name: Camp Marymount
description: A Catholic camp outside Nashville that has run boys' and girls' sessions since 1939, with a spring-fed lake, horses, and Mass under the trees. Nashville Catholic families treat it as an institution.

name: Camp Mataponi
description: A big, high-energy girls' camp on Sebago Lake with polished facilities, a huge activity roster, and legendary spirit days. One of the marquee names among Maine's competitive girls' camps.

name: Camp Matollionequay
description: The girls camp of YMCA of the Pines on Lake Stockwell in the Pine Barrens, pairing sister-camp traditions with riding, sailing, and a warm, confidence-first culture. Brothers often attend Ockanickon across the water.

name: Camp May Flather
description: Girl Scouts Nation's Capital's mountain camp on the North River in the Shenandoah Valley, a 1930s classic where DC-area girls ride, hike, and splash in mountain water.

name: Camp McCall
description: The South Carolina Baptist camp for boys in the upstate mountains near Sunset, where riflery, sports, and creek time come with earnest gospel preaching at a rock-bottom price.

name: Camp McDowell
description: The Episcopal Diocese of Alabama's big-hearted camp in the canyons near Nauvoo, known for swimming holes, folk school arts, and a radically welcoming community. Progressive church camp at its best.

name: Camp Mechuwana
description: A United Methodist camp on Annabessacook Lake in Winthrop running affordable week-long themed sessions - arts, music, nature - with a big-tent, everyone-belongs approach to church camping.

name: Camp Med-O-Lark
description: Maine's flagship arts camp, on Washington Pond, where the schedule is fully elective - campers build their own days from dance, circus, ceramics, theater, and more in a proudly individualistic community.

name: Camp Mendocino
description: Boys & Girls Clubs of San Francisco's historic camp in a redwood river canyon near the Mendocino coast, giving thousands of city kids a deeply subsidized two weeks of creek swims, camp jobs, and belonging. One of California's great camp equity stories.

name: Camp Mennoscah
description: A Mennonite camp on the sandy Ninnescah River in south-central Kansas, cherished for river floats, four-part hymn singing, and a gentle peace-church pace.

name: Camp Menominee
description: A sports-forward boys' camp on Sand Lake near Eagle River where daily athletics mix with waterskiing and classic Northwoods fun, capped by legendary all-camp events.

name: Camp Menzies
description: Girl Scouts Heart of Central California's high-Sierra camp near Arnold, where girls hike granite country, swim in a mountain pool, and run camp traditions that stretch back to the 1940s. Sisterhood at 5,000 feet.

name: Camp Meriwether
description: The Cascade Pacific Council's flagship scout camp on the Oregon coast south of Cape Lookout, where troops camp within earshot of the surf. Merit badges, dune hikes, and beach fires since the 1920s.

name: Camp Merri-Mac
description: A Christian girls' camp in Black Mountain with tribe traditions, strong gymnastics and whitewater programs, and a brother camp (Timberlake) across the ridge.

name: Camp Merrie-Woode
description: A storied girls camp on Fairfield Lake beneath the granite face of Old Bald, famous for its green-and-white uniforms, canoeing tradition, and fierce alumnae devotion. Spots often pass from mother to daughter.

name: Camp Merriwood
description: A spirited private girls camp on Upper Baker Pond in Orford with strong tennis and waterfront programs and a fiercely loyal alumnae network that spans generations.

name: Camp Merrowvista
description: An American Youth Foundation camp in the Ossipee Mountains built around 'my own self, at my very best' — village living, biking and hiking treks, and unusually intentional leadership development.

name: Camp Merry Heart
description: Easterseals New Jersey's barrier-free camp near Hackettstown, serving children and adults with disabilities through fully adapted swimming, boating, crafts, and campfires with high staff ratios and on-site nursing.

name: Camp Mesorah
description: A modern-Orthodox coed camp on a private lake near Guilford in central New York, blending daily learning with waterskiing, sports, and a spirited, family-run community feel.

name: Camp Metigoshe
description: A Lutheran camp in the Turtle Mountains on the Canadian border, the summer heartbeat for North Dakota church kids with lake days, songs, and wilderness sites.

name: Camp Micah
description: A coed Jewish camp on Peabody Pond built by veteran camp directors, mixing strong facilities and programming with a warm, culturally Jewish (not strictly observant) community feel.

name: 4-H Camp Middlesex
description: A friendly 4-H camp near the New Hampshire line where kids tend animals, shoot archery, and swim in the pond like it is still 1965 - in the best way. One of the more affordable overnight options in the state.

name: Camp Mihaska
description: The Salvation Army's Ozark camp near Bourbon, giving St. Louis-area kids from tight-budget households nearly free weeks of swimming, music, and character programs in the Meramec River hills. A long-running engine of first camp experiences.

name: Camp Mikell
description: The Episcopal Diocese of Atlanta's beloved mountain camp near Toccoa, known for its waterfall, deeply welcoming community, and generations of families who keep coming back. Weeklong sessions mix worship, creek play, and classic camp traditions.

name: Camp Millhouse
description: A small, long-running South Bend camp dedicated entirely to campers with physical and developmental disabilities, where one-on-one attention is the norm.

name: Camp Miniwanca
description: The American Youth Foundation's camp on a mile of Lake Michigan dunes, running parallel boys' and girls' camps around the 'four-fold way' of balanced living — dune runs at sunset are the stuff of alumni legend.

name: Camp Minsi
description: A Pocono plateau camp on Stillwater Lake where Lehigh Valley troops have summered for a century amid blueberry bushes, black bears, and big-lake sunsets.

name: Camp Mishawaka
description: A traditional camp on Lake Pokegama founded in 1910, with separate boys' and girls' programs sharing a storied site — canoe trips, tennis, and a deep bench of multigenerational families.

name: Camp Chief Little Turtle
description: A mountain adventure camp in the Arkansas River Valley near Buena Vista, where campers raft, ride horses, and climb amid the Collegiate Peaks. Note: verify current operating status before enrolling.

name: Camp Misty Meadows
description: Girl Scouts of San Jacinto's equestrian-flavored resident camp north of Houston, where one of the largest girl-serving horse programs in Texas anchors the week.

name: Camp Misty Mountain
description: Girl Scouts of Greater Atlanta's big northwest Georgia resident camp, with a strong equestrian center, climbing tower, and lakefront under the ridge lines near Rome. Modern facilities by scout-camp standards.

name: Camp Mitchell
description: The Episcopal Diocese of Arkansas' camp atop Petit Jean Mountain, where bluff-top sunsets, pool afternoons, and inclusive chapel life define low-key, affordable weeks. The mountain views alone are worth the trip.

name: Camp Mitigwa
description: Central Iowa's council camp in the wooded Des Moines River valley, a steady classic with river bluffs, ranges, and the friendly feel of a camp where staff grew up as campers.

name: Camp Mitre Peak
description: A Girl Scout camp beneath its namesake desert peak between Alpine and Fort Davis, offering high-desert hiking, horses, and spectacular night skies. One of the few camps serving far West Texas girls.

name: Camp MiVoden
description: A Seventh-day Adventist camp on Hayden Lake in the Idaho panhandle with waterskiing, horses, and a rock wall, plus vegetarian meals and Sabbath rest built into the rhythm. High-energy program, serene setting.

name: Camp Mogen Avraham
description: A century-old Orthodox boys' camp in Swan Lake balancing morning learning with a strong sports and waterfront program, part of the beloved Mogen Av camp family.

name: Camp Mokule'ia
description: The Episcopal Diocese of Hawaii's beachfront camp on Oahu's North Shore, where summer weeks mix ocean play, aloha-spirited community, and gentle spirituality under the ironwood trees. Camp with the beach ten steps away.

name: Camp Monadnock
description: A YMCA-rooted Christian camp near Mount Monadnock offering pond swimming, hiking, and classic New England cabin life for southern New Hampshire and Massachusetts families.

name: Camp Mondamin
description: A deliberately old-fashioned boys camp on Lake Summit with a national reputation for whitewater paddling and self-directed adventure. No trophies, no bugle contests — just mountains, rivers, and time to master them.

name: Camp Monroe
description: A family-owned Jewish cultural camp in Orange County barely an hour from the George Washington Bridge, mixing kosher-style dining and Shabbat traditions with a classic all-around program.

name: Camp Monroe
description: A small Presbyterian camp in the Sandhills near Laurel Hill, offering old-fashioned lake swims, campfires, and faith community at one of the lowest price points in the state.

name: Camp Mont Shenandoah
description: A storied girls camp on the Cowpasture River in the Allegheny highlands, where riding, river swims, and nearly a century of white-uniform traditions bind generations of Virginia families.

name: Camp Moosilauke
description: A boys camp on Upper Baker Pond dating to 1904, pairing serious sports instruction with lake life and White Mountain hikes — brother camp to Merriwood across the water.

name: Camp Morrison
description: An Idaho Scout camp on the shore of Payette Lake in resort-town McCall, mixing a strong sailing and canoeing waterfront with mountain trails right out of camp.

name: Camp Mosey Wood
description: Girl Scouts of Eastern Pennsylvania's Pocono resident camp on a private lake near White Haven, with sailing, riding, and a classic tiered program from Brownies to CITs.

name: Camp Moshava Alevy
description: Bnei Akiva's West Coast camp in the San Bernardino Mountains, bringing religious Zionist camping - davening, learning, Israeli dance, mountain adventure - to LA-area Modern Orthodox kids.

name: Camp Moshava (Habonim Dror)
description: Habonim Dror's mid-Atlantic camp in rural Maryland, known as Mosh, where communal living, Hebrew slang, and campers-run-the-camp democracy carry the Labor Zionist torch for DC and Baltimore families.

name: Camp Moshava Indian Orchard
description: The flagship Bnei Akiva camp in the Poconos, where religious Zionist education, Hebrew, and Shabbat set the rhythm around sports, swimming, and tripping. A tight community that many Modern Orthodox families treat as non-negotiable.

name: Camp Moshava Wild Rose
description: The Midwest home of the religious-Zionist Bnei Akiva movement, in Wild Rose, Wisconsin, mixing full Shabbat and kosher living with sports, swimming, and famously spirited dancing.

name: Camp Motorsport
description: A one-of-a-kind camp in Southside Virginia where campers drive go-karts and mini race cars, learn basic mechanics, and climb a ranked racing ladder over classic camp evenings.

name: Camp Mount Luther
description: A small ELCA camp on a wooded central Pennsylvania mountainside, offering rustic cabins, hiking, and unhurried Lutheran faith formation.

name: Camp Mountain Chai
description: San Diego's Jewish community camp high in the San Bernardino mountains near Big Bear, mixing classic alpine camp activities with warm, big-tent Jewish life. Popular with SoCal and Arizona families alike.

name: Camp MoVal
description: The United Church of Christ's Missouri camp near Union, offering gentle, affirming weeks of creek hikes, crafts, and campfire worship on wooded Bourbeuse River valley land. Known for welcoming every kid exactly as they are.

name: Camp Mowglis
description: The 'School of the Open' on Newfound Lake, where boys progress through Jungle Book-named dens, row wooden crew boats, and climb in the White Mountains. Character-building with a capital C.

name: Camp Muffly
description: The Monongalia County 4-H camp in the wooded hills south of Morgantown, offering affordable week-long sessions of swimming, crafts, and outdoor skills with 4-H's signature tribe traditions.

name: Camp Mundo Vista
description: Camp Caraway's sister camp for girls, run by North Carolina Baptists near Asheboro with a missions-minded flavor, lake activities, and big-sisterly counselor culture.

name: Camp Munk
description: A heimish Orthodox boys' camp in Ferndale rooted in the German-Jewish yekkishe tradition, pairing serious learning with baseball, swimming, and a famously warm, orderly bunk culture.

name: Camp Mystic
description: A storied girls' camp on the Guadalupe River near Hunt, where generations of Texas families have sent daughters for tribe traditions, riverfront activities, and a gentle Christian atmosphere. Its century-long legacy runs deep in the Hill Country camping community.

name: Nashoba Valley Day Camp
description: A traditional pond-side day camp in Westford, north of Boston, with swimming, boating, and outdoor skills at a community price. Rustic, friendly, and outdoorsy for Merrimack Valley families.

name: Camp Nageela
description: A Catskills outreach camp near Fallsburg that welcomes Jewish kids of every background to a warm, hands-on introduction to observance, wrapped inside a genuinely fun, subsidized camp week.

name: Camp Nah-Jee-Wah
description: The younger-kids camp of the big NJY Camps campus in Milford, designed as a gentle first sleepaway with go-karts, ponies, and lake time plus Jewish culture woven throughout. Kids often graduate up to Cedar Lake Camp next door.

name: Camp Nah-Jee-Wah (NJY Camps)
description: The younger-kids camp of the big NJY Camps campus in the Poconos, with kosher food, a giant lake, and a gentle first-overnight-camp culture serving Jewish families across the New York metro.

name: Camp Nakanawa
description: A storied girls camp on the Cumberland Plateau, running since 1920 with its famous Amazon-Valkyrie team rivalry, a private lake, and traditions handed down through four generations. Full-summer immersion in a deliberately unplugged world.

name: Camp Namanu
description: Camp Fire Columbia's century-old camp on the Sandy River near Portland, with 550 acres of forest, fairy-tale traditions like the Namanu elves, and a famously inclusive culture. One of Oregon's defining summer camps.

name: Camp Napowan
description: A central-Wisconsin camp between two lakes that has hosted Chicago-area troops for generations — sailing, a strong ecology lodge, and the pine-and-oak feel of classic Badger State camping.

name: Camp Nashoba North
description: A family-run coed camp on Crescent Lake blending real skill-building - strong tennis, sailing, and a surprisingly good rock band program - with a close, easygoing community.

name: Camp Natarswi
description: A Girl Scout camp on Togue Pond at the foot of Katahdin, where girls hike in Baxter State Park, canoe, and earn outdoor skills in one of the most dramatic settings of any camp in the East.

name: Camp Nathanael
description: A mission-run Bible camp in the eastern Kentucky mountains that has served Appalachian kids since the 1940s at almost no cost, with creek swimming, Bible classes, and mountain hospitality.

name: Camp Nawakwa
description: A Lutheran camp in the orchard country near Gettysburg, offering nearly a century of craft shops, creek walks, and campfire liturgy for Pennsylvania families.

name: Camp Nebagamon
description: A storied boys' camp near Lake Superior famous for its wilderness tripping program into the Boundary Waters and Porcupine Mountains, its gentle non-competitive ethos, and the 'keep the fires burning' spirit alumni carry for life.

name: Camp Nejeda
description: New Jersey's dedicated camp for children with type 1 diabetes, where every counselor and cook understands carb counts and the med staff turn glucose management into a life skill. Many campers return as counselors.

name: Camp NEOFA
description: A small, no-frills camp in rural Waldo County run by the Northeast Odd Fellows Association, keeping traditional lake-and-woods camping affordable for ordinary families.

name: Camp Nesher
description: A Modern Orthodox camp in northeastern Pennsylvania under the NJY Camps umbrella, offering full shomer Shabbat living with strong sports and separate-swim programming in a warm, inclusive setting.

name: Camp Netimus
description: A small, old-fashioned girls camp in the woods near Milford where campers choose their own activities and riding is a highlight. The vibe is warm, unplugged, and refreshingly free of cliques.

name: Camp Newfound
description: The girls' half of the Newfound-Owatonna community on Crystal Lake, run on Christian Science principles with an international camper base. Sailing, arts, and a famously supportive sisterhood.

name: Camp Nicolet
description: A girls' camp east of Eagle River on Franklin Lake where campers largely choose their own activities, from sailing to theater, in an unpretentious, encouraging atmosphere.

name: Camp No-Be-Bo-Sco
description: A classic northern New Jersey Scout camp in the Kittatinny ridge country — famous to the outside world as the filming location of a certain 1980 summer-camp horror movie, and famous to Scouts for its lake and spirit.

name: Nock-A-Mixon Day Camp
description: A big country day camp on 100-plus wooded acres in upper Bucks County, with a lake, pools, horseback riding, and a full traditional program. Buses from across the Philadelphia suburbs.

name: Camp Nooteeming
description: The Dutchess County Scouts' camp in the woods near Salt Point, a longtime local base for merit badges, pond swims, and outdoor-skills weeks in the eastern Hudson Valley.

name: Camp North Star
description: A flexible coed camp in Poland Spring with sessions from one to eight weeks and an enormous elective menu, making it one of the most accessible entry points into Maine sleepaway camping.

name: Camp Northern Frontier
description: A rugged Christian wilderness camp for boys near Star Lake in the northwestern Adirondacks, built around canoe trips, riflery, and cabin discipleship in a deliberately no-frills setting.

name: Camp Northwood
description: A structured Adirondack camp near Remsen built specifically for campers with ADHD, learning differences, and high-functioning autism, pairing a high staff ratio with genuine social-skills coaching inside a real camp program.

name: Camp Nor'wester
description: A storied San Juan Islands camp on Lopez Island where campers live in teepees and cabins, sail the Salish Sea, and paddle a 36-foot Haida-style canoe. Intentionally unplugged, with deep traditions dating to 1935.

name: Camp Notre Dame
description: A Catholic camp on Spofford Lake where generations of families have sent kids for sandy-beach swims, campfire skits, and a friendly, faith-flavored week that won't break the budget.

name: Camp Nubar
description: AGBU's Armenian heritage camp on a private lake in the western Catskills, where kids from the diaspora mix classic camp activities with Armenian language, dance, and enormous amounts of pride.

name: Camp Nuhop
description: An Ohio camp serving kids with ADHD, autism, and learning differences for over fifty years, with tiny cabin groups and an outdoor-education backbone in the Mohican forest. Its sliding-scale pricing keeps specialized camping within reach.

name: Camp O-AT-KA
description: A century-old boys' camp on the shore of Sebago Lake with Episcopal roots, a stone chapel, and a big-hearted brotherhood ethos. More affordable and more flexible on session length than most classic Maine camps.

name: Camp Oak Hill
description: A Christian camp north of Durham with separate boys and girls villages, a lake, horses, and counselors recruited for ministry-mindedness as much as skill.

name: Camp Oakhurst
description: A century-old New Jersey Shore camp for children and young adults with physical disabilities, run by the Oakhurst organization with fully accessible facilities and adaptive recreation. One of the oldest continuously operating disability camps in the country.

name: Camp Ocala 4-H
description: A 4-H residential camp inside the Ocala National Forest on Sellers Lake, with canoeing, ecology classes, and true scrub-and-springs Florida wilderness around every cabin.

name: Camp Ocean Pines
description: A small nonprofit camp on a pine bluff above the Pacific in Cambria, mixing tide pooling, archery, and arts with ocean sunsets from the campfire bowl. Affordable, gentle, and one of the few true coastal sleepaway camps between LA and Santa Cruz.

name: Camp Ockanickon
description: The boys camp of YMCA of the Pines, set among cedar lakes deep in the New Jersey Pine Barrens, where canoeing dark tea-colored water and campfire brotherhood have defined summers since 1906.

name: Camp of the Cross
description: A Lutheran camp overlooking Lake Sakakawea near Garrison, offering week-long sessions where big-lake adventure meets small-camp Christian community.

name: Camp of the Hills
description: A donor-funded Christian camp near Marble Falls that brings under-resourced kids to the Hill Country for a week of ziplines, swimming, and mentorship at little to no cost. Its mission focus makes it one of the region's quiet gems.

name: Camp of the Rising Son
description: A Christian camp on the historic French Camp Academy grounds along the Natchez Trace, running separate boys' and girls' weeks with lake sports, horses, and heartfelt discipleship. Mississippi families prize its counselor culture.

name: Camp-of-the-Woods
description: A historic Christian family resort on Lake Pleasant in the Adirondacks, where kids' camp programs run alongside family vacation weeks with beach, sports, and Bible teaching.

name: Camp Ogichi Daa Kwe
description: The sister camp to Kooch-i-ching on Rainy Lake, sending girls on serious wilderness canoe trips — including weeks-long Canadian expeditions — within a fiercely supportive sisterhood.

name: Camp Ojibwa
description: An intensely spirited boys' sports camp in Eagle River famous for its Collegiate League and a fiercely loyal, largely Chicagoland alumni network stretching back to 1928. Sports are the heartbeat here.

name: Camp Okizu
description: Northern California's free camp for families affected by childhood cancer, a Sierra foothills institution rebuilt with remarkable community support after wildfire loss. Oncology camps, sibling camps, and family weekends all at no cost.

name: Camp Okoboji
description: A Lutheran (LCMS) camp on West Lake Okoboji in Iowa's Great Lakes region, a longtime summer gathering spot for Midwest Lutheran families with beach, boats, and Bible study.

name: Camp Old Indian
description: The Blue Ridge Council's century-old Scout camp at the foot of the mountains above Travelers Rest, running merit-badge weeks with a proud, tradition-heavy staff culture.

name: Camp Oljato
description: A boat-in Scout camp on the far shore of Huntington Lake in the High Sierra — gear arrives by barge, and the week revolves around sailing, canoeing, and alpine hikes. Remote in the most charming way.

name: Camp Olmsted
description: A Methodist mission camp on the Hudson's west bank at Cornwall, serving low-income NYC children for over a century with subsidized sessions, river views, and gentle faith-based community.

name: Camp Olson YMCA
description: An independent YMCA camp on Little Boy Lake in the heart of Minnesota lake country, with horses, sailing, and a big scholarship program that keeps the camper body wonderfully mixed.

name: Camp Olympia
description: A big, spirited coed camp on Lake Livingston founded by former Texas athletes, with strong waterskiing, golf, and an Olympic-themed team competition that runs all session. A Houston-area favorite for decades.

name: Camp Olympia at Norbeck
description: A traditional Montgomery County day camp with pools, sports, and arts on a green Rockville-area campus. A dependable DC-suburb choice with strong swim instruction and camp spirit.

name: Camp Omega
description: A Lutheran (LCMS) camp on Horseshoe Lake in southern Minnesota offering week-long Bible camps with a classic waterfront, ropes course, and gentle Christ-centered programming.

name: Camp Onas
description: A Quaker camp in Bucks County running affordable one- and two-week sessions built on simplicity, cooperation, and outdoor play rather than competition. A gentle, values-forward first sleepaway experience.

name: Camp Onaway
description: A deeply traditional single-session girls camp on Newfound Lake where sailing, pageants, and quiet Sunday rituals have barely changed in a century — and that's exactly why families love it.

name: Camp Ondessonk
description: A rugged Catholic camp in the Shawnee National Forest where kids sleep in open-air treehouse cabins named for the North American Martyrs and swim beneath sandstone bluffs.

name: Camp Oneka
description: A small girls camp on Fairview Lake that has been quietly running since 1908, with white-uniform traditions, strong swimming and canoeing, and an intentionally screen-free, low-drama culture.

name: Camp Orchard Hill
description: A nondenominational Christian camp in the Back Mountain area near Wilkes-Barre with high-energy programming - wake sports, paintball, big rec games - and an upbeat gospel message. Popular with NEPA families.

name: Camp Orr
description: An Ozark adventure camp on the banks of the Buffalo National River beneath towering bluffs — canoeing America's first national river is the centerpiece of every week.

name: Camp Osito Rancho
description: A Girl Scout camp at 7,000 feet above Big Bear Lake that has hosted Southern California girls since the 1930s — alpine hikes, archery, and classic mountain-cabin camp life.

name: Camp Oty'Okwa
description: Big Brothers Big Sisters of Central Ohio's camp on 700 acres of Hocking Hills cliff country, serving many kids who couldn't otherwise afford camp, with strong nature education.

name: Camp Outright
description: A residential camp week for LGBTQ+ youth and allies, run with Outright Vermont at Common Ground Center, where campers get classic camp joy in a place built to affirm them.

name: Camp Overlook
description: A United Methodist camp on the slopes of Massanutten Peak overlooking the Shenandoah Valley, hosting affordable week-long camps with mountain hikes and campfire worship.

name: Camp Owatonna
description: A small boys' camp on Crystal Lake in Harrison rooted in Christian Science values, paired with Camp Newfound for girls. Gentle, wholesome, and unusually tight-knit, drawing families from around the world.

name: Camp Parsons
description: One of the oldest scout camps in the American West, operating on Hood Canal since 1919 under the Olympics' Dosewallips shadow. Scouts earn merit badges, sail the canal, and hike into Olympic National Park.

name: Camp Pasquaney
description: One of America's oldest summer camps, perched above Newfound Lake since 1895, known for its theater tradition, long expeditions, and an almost collegiate culture of honor and self-governance among older boys.

name: Camp Patmos
description: A Baptist camp on Kelleys Island in Lake Erie — campers arrive by ferry for a week of island swimming, campfires, and straightforward Bible teaching.

name: Camp Paumalū
description: The Girl Scouts of Hawaii's camp in the hills above Oahu's North Shore, blending ridge hikes, Hawaiian cultural arts, and classic Girl Scout traditions with Pacific views. A green, breezy escape from town.

name: Camp Paumalu
