description: Girl Scouts of Hawaii's camp on 200 green acres above Oahu's North Shore, weaving Hawaiian culture, ziplines, and ocean views into island girls' summers.

name: Camp Pecometh
description: A United Methodist camp on the Chester River on Maryland's Eastern Shore, with a zip line, riverfront swimming, and joyful faith formation. A beloved summer tradition for Delmarva church families.

name: Camp Pegasus
description: A specialized Main Line day camp for kids who struggle socially, weaving social-skills coaching into games, sports, and projects with high staff ratios. A trusted option for children with ADHD, anxiety, or on the spectrum.

name: Camp Pembroke
description: The all-girls camp of the Cohen Camps family, set on Oldham Pond south of Boston with joyful Jewish spirit woven through a full waterfront and arts program. Known for big song sessions and sisterhood.

name: Camp Pemigewassett
description: A fifth-generation family-run boys camp on Lower Baker Pond, famous for its nature program, Gilbert-and-Sullivan shows, and a gentle culture where it's cool to be curious.

name: Camp Peniel
description: A Bible-centered camp on Cow Creek near Marble Falls that has kept its program simple and affordable since the 1940s: creek swimming, sports, campfires, and daily teaching. Families who want substance over amenities keep coming back.

name: Camp Perkins
description: A Lutheran outdoor ministry camp in the Sawtooth National Recreation Area near Ketchum, where kids hike alpine lakes, canoe, and gather for campfire worship beneath jagged peaks.

name: Camp Phillip
description: A WELS Lutheran camp near Wautoma offering week-long Bible camps with farm animals, pond fishing, and an earnest, family-like atmosphere at modest cost.

name: Camp Phillips
description: The Chippewa Valley Council's lake camp in northwest Wisconsin, a friendly mid-sized operation with solid aquatics and a strong Cub Scout family camp alongside the troop program.

name: Camp Piankatank
description: A Christian camp on the wide, salty Piankatank River in Virginia's Middle Peninsula, where sailing, tubing, and crabbing meet warm gospel-centered community.

name: Camp Pigott
description: The Mount Baker Council's scout camp on Hughes Lake in the foothills east of Snohomish, with a big dining lodge, shooting sports, and a full merit badge program. A workhorse summer camp for troops across northwest Washington.

name: Camp Pinebrook
description: A gentle, mid-sized Westchester day camp on 15 wooded acres in West Harrison, with two heated pools and a nurturing bent toward younger campers. A popular first-camp choice for central Westchester families.

name: Camp Pinecliffe
description: A full-season girls' camp in Harrison with a century of history, excellent athletics, and deeply held traditions - Green and White team spirit runs through generations of families.

name: Camp Pinewood
description: A coed elective camp on a private lake near Hendersonville that draws many campers from Florida and abroad. Kids build their own schedules from waterskiing, tennis, arts, and more.

name: Camp Pinnacle
description: A Georgia Baptist girls camp in the Rabun County mountains, blending missions-focused programming with classic lake and mountain fun at a very accessible price. Generations of Georgia girls have made their first friendships here.

name: Camp Pinnacle
description: A revived historic camp near Flat Rock now focused on modern adventure — mountain biking, paddleboarding, climbing — with upgraded lodges and a screen-free, confidence-building mission.

name: Camp Pinnacle
description: A Christian & Missionary Alliance camp in the Helderberg Mountains near Voorheesville, offering week-long sessions of hiking, swimming, and faith formation just west of Albany.

name: Camp Pioneer
description: The Greater Niagara Frontier Council's Scout camp near Angola, south of Buffalo, a longtime summer base for merit badges, aquatics, and outdoor skills serving western New York troops.

name: Camp Pioneer
description: A scout camp high in the Cascades near Detroit Lake, with alpine lake canoeing, fishing, and trail access toward Mount Jefferson. Classic mountain scouting for Willamette Valley troops.

name: Camp Pioneer
description: A Scout reservation in the Tygart Valley of the eastern West Virginia mountains, hosting troops for merit-badge weeks with shooting sports, climbing, and mountain high-adventure amid the Monongahela National Forest.

name: Camp Pisgah
description: The Girl Scout camp of the western Carolinas, tucked beside Pisgah National Forest with a lake, stables, and badge-friendly outdoor skills weeks at scout-friendly prices. Open to non-Scouts too.

name: Camp Pocono Trails
description: A wellness and healthy-lifestyle camp in the Poconos (part of New Image Camps) focused on fitness, nutrition education, and confidence-building in a judgment-free setting. Kids leave with habits, not just memories.

name: Camp Pok-O-MacCready
description: One of the oldest camps in the Adirondacks, near Lake Champlain, known for a huge horseback program, a working farm museum, and 46er hiking trips. Brother-sister campuses run side by side.

name: Camp Ponderosa
description: A Christian camp near McCall and Payette Lake, offering active summer weeks of watersports, mountain adventure, and worship in Idaho's beloved lake country.

name: Camp Pondicherry
description: Girl Scouts of Maine's resident camp near Bridgton, with riding, archery, paddling, and short sessions that make it an easy, affordable first taste of overnight camp.

name: Camp Pontiac
description: A premier full-season camp in Copake with pristine facilities, elite-level sports coaching, and a famously spirited color war — a big-personality camp that draws a devoted metro-area crowd.

name: Camp Potomac Woods
description: A Girl Scout camp on wooded bluffs above the Potomac near Leesburg, close enough to Washington for easy drop-offs but leafy enough to feel like a real escape.

name: Camp Powhatan
description: The main camp of Virginia's vast Blue Ridge Scout Reservation, one of the largest council-owned properties in Scouting, with its sister high-adventure camp Ottari up the mountain.

name: Camp Powhatan
description: A traditional boys' camp on Pleasant Lake in Otisfield offering the full classic Maine menu - waterfront, athletics, campcraft - with a steady, unflashy culture and strong counselor retention.

name: Camp Poyntelle
description: A nonprofit Jewish community camp on a lake in the far northern Poconos, long affiliated with New York Y communities, blending classic camp activities with Shabbat traditions and a warm, unfussy culture.

name: Camp Puh'tok
description: A rustic camp in the rolling farmland of northern Baltimore County with a Native American heritage theme, strong horseback program, and creek-and-woods activities. A nonprofit serving Baltimore-area families for generations.

name: Camp Pupukea
description: The Aloha Council's scout camp on the Pupukea plateau above Oahu's North Shore, where troops earn merit badges between guava forest trails and snorkel outings at nearby Shark's Cove. Scouting with a distinctly Hawaiian flavor.

name: Camp Quinebarge
description: A midsize coed camp on Lake Kanasatka near Winnipesaukee with a farm-animal barn, woodshop, and a quirky, inclusive spirit. It has worked to rebuild its reputation after a rocky, understaffed 2021 season.

name: Camp Rainey Mountain
description: A traditional camp in the Blue Ridge foothills of Rabun County, Georgia, whose lakeside campfires and mountain-hollow campsites have changed little in eighty years — by design.

name: Camp Ramah in California
description: The Conservative movement's West Coast flagship in the Ojai hills, blending Hebrew, daily tefillah, and kosher living with sports, arts, and a famously tight alumni network. Its Amitzim program for campers with disabilities is a national model.

name: Camp Ramah Darom
description: The Conservative movement's Ramah camp for the Southeast, set on a stunning mountain campus near Clayton with kosher dining, Hebrew woven into daily life, and serious outdoor adventure. Strong inclusion programming through its Tikvah track.

name: Camp Ramah in Northern California (Ramah Galim)
description: The newest Ramah, set near Monterey Bay with an ocean-forward twist - surfing, marine science, and horseback tracks alongside traditional Jewish camping. Smaller and more intimate than its Ojai sibling.

name: Camp Ramah Galim (Northern California)
description: The newest Ramah, on the Monterey Bay coast, where ocean sports, surfing, and marine science anchor a Conservative Jewish program. Small and community-minded, drawing Bay Area families.

name: Camp Ramah in the Rockies
description: Ramah's wilderness base camp at 8,000 feet in the Colorado Rockies, built around backpacking, climbing, mountain biking, and a working farm rather than bunks-and-ballfields camping. Kosher and shomer Shabbat, with a crunchy, outdoorsy spirit.

name: Camp Ramah in Wisconsin
description: The flagship of the Conservative movement's Ramah network, founded 1947 in Conover, where Hebrew, learning, and full Shabbat observance weave through a big traditional camp program. Its Tikvah program for campers with disabilities is a pioneer in the field.

name: Camp Ramah in New England
description: The Conservative movement's flagship New England camp, blending Hebrew, learning, and observance with a big traditional camp program. Its Tikvah inclusion program for campers with disabilities is a national model.

name: Camp Ramapo
description: Ramapo for Children's century-old Rhinebeck campus serves kids with social, emotional, and learning challenges, using an extraordinary one-to-one staffing model to give them a real, successful camp summer.

name: Camp Ramblewood
description: A coed camp in the woods of Harford County with pools, sports, and a big activity menu, drawing families from the Baltimore and Philadelphia corridors. Also known as a summer music-festival venue in the off-season.

name: Camp Rancho Framasa
description: A CYO camp in the wooded hills of Brown County, Indiana, run by the Archdiocese of Indianapolis with an inclusive, choice-based program and Catholic roots worn lightly.

name: Camp Rap-A-Hope
description: A free week of camp for Alabama children who have or have had cancer, staffed by medical volunteers so campers in treatment can swim, do crafts, and forget hospitals for a while.

name: Camp Raven Knob
description: One of the largest Scout camps in the Southeast, folded into the Blue Ridge foothills near Mount Airy under its namesake rock knob. Troops from many states make the pilgrimage every summer.

name: Camp Raymond
description: A high-elevation Scouts BSA camp west of Flagstaff at 6,700 feet, running traditional merit-badge weeks with shooting sports, climbing, and cool ponderosa nights.

name: Camp Redcloud
description: An adventure-forward Christian camp deep in the San Juans near Lake City, using horses, ropes, and fourteener country to push kids out of their comfort zones with a ministry heart.

name: Camp Redwood
description: A family-run camp in Orange County just 90 minutes from Manhattan, offering flexible sessions and a broad program with a notably international bunk mix.

name: Camp Regis-Applejack
description: A Quaker-influenced coed camp on Upper St. Regis Lake founded by a family committed to inclusion since the 1940s. Gentle, noncompetitive culture with sailing, theater, and teen wilderness trips.

name: Camp Resolute
description: A long-running Scout camp on Little Pond in Bolton hosting Cub Scout and Scouts BSA summer programs for the Heart of New England Council. Merit badges, campfires, and canoe-tipping drills since the early 1900s.

name: Camp Ridgecrest for Boys
description: A Baptist-heritage boys' camp in the Blue Ridge outside Asheville, with a famous tribal system, mountain trips, and steady Christian mentoring since 1929.

name: Camp Riley
description: A camp for children with physical disabilities at Indiana University's Bradford Woods outdoor center, with fully accessible facilities, adaptive equipment, and a strong tie to Riley Hospital for Children. Generous scholarships keep costs low.

name: Camp Riley at Bradford Woods
description: A renowned camp for children with disabilities and serious medical needs, held at Indiana University's 2,500-acre Bradford Woods outdoor center with adapted everything.

name: Camp Rim Rock
description: A big traditional girls' camp in the West Virginia hills best known for its riding program — one of the largest camp stables on the East Coast — alongside classic swimming, arts, and campfire life. Lots of DC-area families are second- and third-generation.

name: Camp Rio Vista for Boys
description: Texas' oldest continuously operating summer camp, Rio Vista has run boys' sessions on Johnson Creek near Ingram since 1921. Paired with sister camp Sierra Vista, it delivers a traditional program with a century of Hill Country know-how.

name: Camp Rising Sun
description: A full-scholarship international leadership camp in Rhinebeck where selected teens from dozens of countries run their own community, give instructions and projects, and debate ideas under the stars. Admission is by competitive application.

name: Camp Riva-Lake
description: A tiny, century-old girls camp on Tims Ford Lake near Sewanee, where fewer than a hundred campers share skiing, sailing, and riding with an almost family-reunion intimacy. Many staff are former campers.

name: Camp River Ranch
description: The Girl Scouts of Western Washington's big riverside camp in the Snoqualmie Valley near Carnation, best known for its horse program. Girls progress from first trail rides to real horsemanship over the summers.

name: Camp Zeke Freehold
description: A traditional day camp on wooded acreage in Monmouth County's Freehold, with pools, sports, and a full activity program. A friendly, well-run option for central Jersey Shore-area families.

name: Rolling Hills Day Camp
description: A friendly mid-sized day camp on a green Chatham-area campus, with swim, sports, and arts and a nurturing, family-run feel. Popular with younger campers across Morris County.

name: Somerset Hills YMCA Camp
description: The Somerset Hills Y's outdoorsy day camp in Basking Ridge, with swimming, nature, and traditional camp skills at a community price. Financial aid and inclusion support round out the program.

name: Brookside Day Camp
description: A neighborhood day camp serving Bergen County families with pools, sports, and arts on a leafy Oradell campus. Warm, straightforward, and a reliable first-camp choice.

name: Shongum Day Camp
description: A long-established Morris County day camp on a wooded Randolph campus with a private lake, pools, and a full traditional program. Multi-generational families keep coming back for the spirit.

name: Camp Roanoke
description: A century-old camp in the hills above the Roanoke Valley, now run by the city's parks department with affordable day and overnight adventure weeks on historic stone-lodge grounds.

name: Camp Robbinswold
description: A Girl Scout camp with a mile of private Hood Canal beachfront, where girls harvest oysters, sail, and sleep in platform tents under the Olympics. Saltwater camp life at its most authentic.

name: Camp Robin Hood
description: A polished full-season coed camp in Freedom with nearly a century of history, strong athletics and waterskiing, and a warm 'camp family' culture that draws many Canadian and international families.

name: Camp Robindel
description: A classic full-summer girls camp on Lake Winnipesaukee with strong waterskiing, dance, and arts programs and a tight sisterhood culture. Many families send multiple generations.

name: Camp Rockmont for Boys
description: A large Christian boys' camp on the historic Black Mountain College site, mixing lake jumps and blacksmithing with a thoughtful philosophy of boy development.

name: Camp Rodney
description: A century-old camp on the headwaters of the Chesapeake Bay, where sailing, motorboating, and bay ecology give Scouts a genuine maritime summer within sight of the Elk Neck bluffs.

name: Camp Roganunda
description: Camp Fire Yakima's forest camp in the Cascades off the White Pass highway west of Naches. A simple, affordable week of creek stomping, crafts, and campfire songs for central Washington kids.

name: Camp Roger
description: A Christian camp near Grand Rapids with a creation-focused outdoor program — kids catch frogs, ride horses, and learn to see nature as sacred — in a gentle, unhurried week.

name: Camp Romimu
description: A well-known Orthodox boys' camp near Monticello balancing morning Torah learning with a full sports and waterfront program, a Catskills fixture for yeshiva families for decades.

name: Camp Ronald McDonald for Good Times
description: A free camp in the San Jacinto Mountains for children with cancer and their families, run with medical backing from Southern California's pediatric oncology community. Sessions cover patients, siblings, and whole families year-round.

name: Camp Roosevelt Firebird
description: A century-old private camp near Lake Erie east of Cleveland, revived as a coed camp that leans into camper choice, sailing, and a nurturing, low-pressure culture.

name: Camp Roosevelt
description: The Katahdin Area Council's scout camp on Fitts Pond near Bangor, offering merit-badge summer camp, climbing, and waterfront weeks with easy access to Katahdin-region adventures.

name: Camp Roosevelt (Central Wyoming)
description: A rustic mountain camp in the Wind River Range foothills near Lander, offering affordable weeks of hiking, fishing, and outdoor skills for central Wyoming youth. Verify current session offerings before enrolling.

name: Camp Royall
description: The Autism Society of North Carolina's camp, the oldest and largest autism-specific camp in the country, with a near one-to-one staffing model, sensory-aware routines, and campers spanning early childhood through adulthood.

name: Camp Royaneh
description: A century-old Scout camp in the redwoods near the Russian River, run by the San Francisco Bay Area Council. Redwood-shaded campsites and a long list of troops who've come back for generations.

name: Camp Runoia
description: A warm, low-key girls' camp on Great Pond that has been run with the same unhurried spirit since 1907. Strong riding and waterfront programs without the pressure of the flashier Maine camps.

name: Camp Sabra
description: The St. Louis JCC's storied overnight camp on a Lake of the Ozarks peninsula, mixing a serious waterfront — ski boats, sailing, the famous trans-lake swim — with warm Jewish culture and Shabbat under the trees. The Midwest Jewish camp for many families.

name: Camp Sacajawea
description: South Jersey's Girl Scout camp in the pines near Vineland, an easygoing camp where Philly-area and shore-county girls get their first taste of sleepaway life.

name: Camp Sacajawea
description: The Girl Scouts' rustic camp on Casper Mountain, giving Wyoming girls affordable weeks of forest exploration, crafts, and campfire traditions above the plains.

name: Camp Saginaw
description: A family-owned coed camp in Chester County farm country that has served Philadelphia and Baltimore families since the 1930s, with classic traditions, pools, ballfields, and a homey, unpretentious feel.

name: Woodside Day Camp
description: A traditional day camp on a wooded Montgomery County campus, with heated pools, sports, and arts for Philadelphia-area families. Known for strong swim instruction and a friendly, spirited culture.

name: Camp San Pedro
description: The Catholic Diocese of Orlando's lakeside camp hidden in the oaks north of Winter Park, mixing sacraments and s'mores in weeklong sessions that Orlando Catholic families rely on every summer.

name: Camp Sandy Cove
description: A Christian camp in the West Virginia mountains west of Winchester, with horses, mountain boarding, and intentional discipleship in a small, family feel.

name: Camp Sangamon
description: Betsey Cox's brother camp, a noncompetitive boys camp on a Vermont farm where the schedule is whatever a boy signs up for — forge, farm, mountain bikes, or the pond.

name: Camp Santa Maria
description: A historic Catholic camp in the Platte Canyon run through Catholic Charities, famous for the giant Christ of the Rockies statue above camp and for keeping tuition low so every kid can come.

name: Camp Sawtooth
description: A Presbyterian camp deep in the Soldier Mountains near Fairfield, known for a rustic, off-grid feel where kids unplug fully for a week of hiking, stargazing, and campfire community.

name: Camp Scatico
description: A hundred-year-old brother-sister camp on twin lakes in the Hudson Valley, run by the same family for generations, with old-school traditions, strong tennis and basketball, and no interest in glitz.

name: Camp Scherman
description: A large Girl Scout camp in the pine-and-boulder country of the San Jacinto Mountains, with pools, horses, and desert-mountain stargazing for Southern California girls.

name: Camp Scouthaven
description: A Cub and Scout camp in the rural hills of Cattaraugus County near Freedom, focused on younger scouts with a lake, nature program, and first-time-friendly outdoor adventures south of Buffalo.

name: Camp Scully
description: A century-old nonprofit camp on Snyder's Lake just outside Albany, long run by Catholic charities to give Capital Region kids an affordable, old-fashioned lakeside summer.

name: Camp Sea Gull
description: A huge YMCA seamanship camp on the Neuse River where boys earn ranks in sailing, motorboating, and navigation — one of the country's premier saltwater camp programs, paired with sister camp Seafarer.

name: Camp Sea-Gull
description: A friendly coed camp on Lake Charlevoix with a strong sailing and waterski program and a laid-back, sunscreen-and-sand northern Michigan feel.

name: YMCA Camp Sea Gull
description: A legendary boys' seamanship camp on the Neuse River run by the YMCA of the Triangle, where campers earn ranks in sailing, motorboating, and golf on a huge riverfront campus.

name: Camp SEA Lab
description: A Monterey Bay ocean-science camp affiliated with CSU Monterey Bay, running day and residential weeks of kayaking Elkhorn Slough, tide pooling, and touring world-class marine labs. Science-rich and scholarship-friendly.

name: Camp Seafarer
description: Camp Sea Gull's sister camp on the Neuse, where girls work through the same celebrated seamanship ranks — sailing, waterskiing, powerboating — with a spirited all-girls culture.

name: YMCA Camp Seafarer
description: Camp Sea Gull's sister camp on the Neuse River, where girls work through the same storied seamanship ranks — sailing, skiing, and motorboating — with fierce Seafarer pride.

name: Camp Sealth
description: Camp Fire's flagship Puget Sound camp, spread across 400 acres of Vashon Island forest and beach since 1920. Known for its inclusive, unpretentious culture, horse program, and a ferry-plus-boat arrival that feels like an adventure in itself.

name: Camp Seawood
description: A Girl Scout day camp in the woods outside Portsmouth offering seacoast girls a low-key summer of trails, crafts, and outdoor skills close to home.

name: Camp Sebago
description: The Salvation Army's camp on Sebago Lake, giving Maine kids - many from families who couldn't otherwise afford camp - a free or near-free week of swimming, sports, and campfires.

name: Camp Seneca Lake
description: The Rochester JCC's beloved camp on the shore of Seneca Lake, where sailing, Shabbat on the hill, and a fiercely loyal CSL community have anchored upstate Jewish summers for generations.

name: Camp Sentinel
description: A small, affordable Christian camp in the hills of Tuftonboro with a pond, mountain views, and a gentle faith-and-fun rhythm. One-week sessions make it an easy first sleepaway.

name: Camp Sequassen
description: The Connecticut Yankee Council's big Scout reservation on West Hill Pond, with shooting sports, sailing, and merit badge programs that draw troops from across southern New England.

name: Camp Sequoyah
description: Alabama's flagship Scout camp beside Cheaha Mountain, the state's highest point, mixing ridge hikes with a solid lake and the Greater Alabama Council's summer traditions.

name: Camp Settoga
description: The Marlene Meyerson JCC Manhattan's day camp on a modern 21-acre campus in Pomona, busing city kids to pools, ropes courses, and woods each morning. Joyfully Jewish in a big-tent, cultural way.

name: Camp Settoga (Marlene Meyerson JCC Manhattan)
description: The JCC Manhattan's purpose-built day camp on 21 wooded acres in Rockland County, busing city kids to pools, treehouses, and a ropes course wrapped in warm, pluralistic Jewish culture.

name: Camp Seven Hills
description: A Girl Scout camp in the hills south of Buffalo where girls have canoed the pond, sung rounds at the dining hall, and grown into camp leaders for nearly a century.

name: Camp Sewataro
description: A big, polished day camp on 60 wooded acres in Sudbury with ponies, ropes courses, and swim lessons twice a day. A MetroWest favorite for younger kids, with bus service across the suburbs.

name: Camp Shady Brook
description: The YMCA of the Pikes Peak Region's overnight camp in the South Platte canyon country, offering affordable weeks of horses, archery, and creek stomping with generous financial aid. A solid, unpretentious first camp.

name: Camp Shai (JCC Denver)
description: The JCC Denver's day camp, mixing swim, sports, arts, and Israeli culture with a warm Jewish community feel — open to all families, with strong inclusion staffing.

name: TA Ranch Youth Adventure Camp
description: A small ranch-based youth adventure program near Buffalo at the foot of the Bighorn Mountains, focused on horsemanship, cattle work, and Wyoming history. Verify current session offerings before enrolling.

name: Camp Shalom (Levis JCC)
description: The Levis JCC's big Boca Raton day camp, with pools, sports leagues, arts, and Jewish culture woven through a polished, air-conditioned program - plus a respected inclusion track. A South Florida summer institution.

name: Camp Shamineau
description: A large evangelical camp on Lake Shamineau near Motley with horses, a huge waterfront, paintball, and decades of central-Minnesota families sending kids for a week of faith and fun.

name: Camp Shand
description: One of America's oldest YMCA camps, run by the Lancaster Y in the wooded hills near Cornwall, with a mission of getting every kid to camp regardless of family income. Simple, joyful, heavily scholarshipped weeks.

name: Camp Shands
description: North Florida's council camp in the sandhills and live oaks east of Gainesville, a straightforward, affordable week of Scouting fundamentals.

name: Rockland Camps
description: A community-oriented Rockland day camp in New City with pools, sports fields, and nature programming at a moderate price. Known for friendly staff and inclusive programming for a range of campers.

name: Camp Shankitunk
description: Delaware County's 4-H camp outside Delhi, where Catskill-country kids get an old-fashioned, affordable week of creek stomping, crafts, and dining-hall singalongs.

name: Camp Shaw-Waw-Nas-See
description: A 4-H-rooted camp along Rock Creek near Kankakee, an hour from Chicago, where kids do creek stomps, archery, and campfires under giant oaks.

name: Camp Shenandoah
description: A Shenandoah Valley camp under the Allegheny foothills near Staunton, small enough to feel personal and framed by some of Virginia's prettiest farm-and-mountain country.

name: Camp Shohola
description: A small boys camp on Lake Greeley known for letting campers build their own schedule, from sailing and riflery to a real ham radio shack. Brothers often overlap with sisters at nearby Camp Netimus.

name: Camp Shomria
description: Hashomer Hatzair's youth-led kibbutz-style camp near Liberty, where campers share chores and decision-making in a socialist-Zionist tradition that's scrappy, idealistic, and fiercely loved.

name: Camp Shomria (Hashomer Hatzair)
description: The Hashomer Hatzair movement's Catskills camp, a century-old socialist-Zionist youth community where campers share responsibilities, sleep simply, and learn the movement's scouting and egalitarian traditions.

name: Camp Sierra Vista for Girls
description: The girls' side of the historic Vista Camps on Johnson Creek, sharing the century-old property and traditions with Rio Vista while running its own program of riding, river fun, and arts. A good pick for families wanting brother-sister camps on one site.

name: Camp Siloam
description: A century-old Arkansas Baptist camp where church groups bring kids for classic week-long sessions of creek swimming, rec games, and revival-style evening worship.

name: Camp Simcha
description: Chai Lifeline's free camp in Glen Spey for children with cancer and serious illness, running separate boys' and girls' sessions with hospital-grade medical care hidden inside nonstop celebration.

name: Camp Skimino
description: A Girl Scout camp in the woods near Williamsburg with a pool, archery ranges, and platform-tent units, running summer weeks for Scouts and non-Scouts across the Tidewater region.

name: Camp Skyline Ranch
description: One of Mentone's classic girls camps atop Lookout Mountain, with the Little River running through camp, big sisterly traditions, and a Christian foundation. Alabama and Georgia girls grow up counting summers here.

name: Camp Sloane YMCA
description: A tented YMCA camp in Connecticut's northwest corner where campers sleep on platform tents around Indian Lake and the community skews wonderfully international. Rustic sleeping, rich programming.

name: Wilton Family YMCA Camp
description: The Wilton Y's day camp, mixing swim, sports, and outdoor exploration on Fairfield County grounds at a community price. Financial aid and inclusion support make it broadly accessible.

name: Camp Small Valley
description: Girl Scouts in the Heart of Pennsylvania's camp in a quiet mountain valley north of Harrisburg, with a pool, ponds, and one of the council's stronger horse programs.

name: Camp Solomon Schechter
description: The Pacific Northwest's Conservative-affiliated Jewish camp, on a private lake south of Olympia, blending Shabbat ruach and Hebrew songs with lake swims and gaga. Draws campers from Seattle, Portland, and Vancouver BC.

name: Camp Somerset for Girls
description: A revived historic girls' camp on North Pond in the Belgrade Lakes, relaunched with modern cabins, upscale amenities, and a big activity menu while keeping a classic camp rhythm.

name: Camp Southern Ground
description: Zac Brown's state-of-the-art inclusive camp south of Atlanta, purpose-built to serve neurotypical and neurodiverse kids side by side, with an amazing farm-to-table kitchen and top-tier facilities. Scholarships keep it accessible.

name: Camp Southwoods
description: An upbeat, well-run coed camp on Paradox Lake known for flexible session lengths, attentive staff communication, and a kindness-first culture that suits first-time campers.

name: Camp Spalding
description: A Presbyterian camp on Davis Lake between Spokane and Newport, known regionally for polished programming, wakeboarding, and strong counselor mentorship. One of the Inland Northwest's most respected church camps.

name: Camp Speers YMCA
description: A 1,100-acre Pocono camp with a 45-acre private lake, strong equestrian program, and a long-standing commitment to inclusion, including programs for campers with autism.

name: Camp Spofford
description: A Christian camp and conference center on Spofford Lake with a classic waterfront, tube runs, and week-long youth camps that many New England church families treat as an annual fixture.

name: Camp Spring Creek
description: A specialized camp for children with dyslexia that pairs daily one-on-one Orton-Gillingham tutoring with woodshop, swimming, and mountain adventure — academics without the shame.

name: Camp Sprout Lake
description: Young Judaea's junior camp in Dutchess County, a gentle first sleepaway where Israeli counselors, song sessions, and lake days build joyful Jewish identity for younger kids.

name: Camp Squanto
description: The Mayflower Council's Scout camp deep in Myles Standish State Forest, running merit badges, rifle ranges, and waterfront weeks for Scouts BSA troops. Classic scouting on a pine-barrens pond.

name: Camp St. Albans
description: A Girl Scout camp in the woods along Case Inlet near Belfair, mixing saltwater beach time with archery, boating, and classic unit-camping traditions. A gentle first overnight camp for many western Washington girls.

name: Camp St. Charles
description: A Catholic camp on the Wicomico River in Southern Maryland, long run in the Redemptorist tradition with swimming, sailing, and Sunday Mass by the water.

name: Camp St. Christopher
description: An Episcopal-heritage barrier-island camp at the tip of Seabrook Island, where campers seine the surf, watch dolphins, and worship with sandy feet.

name: Camp St. Francis
description: A Franciscan Catholic camp perched above the beach in Aptos, where kids split their days between redwood shade and Monterey Bay sand. One of the few sleepaway camps in California with its own beach access.

name: Camp Stand By Me
description: Easterseals Washington's fully accessible camp on the Key Peninsula, serving campers with physical and developmental disabilities alongside sibling and inclusion weeks. Adaptive waterfront and high ropes with one-to-one support available.

name: Camp Starfish
description: A one-to-one ratio camp on Hubbard Pond for kids with emotional, behavioral, and learning challenges — highly structured days, tiny groups, and staff trained to help every child succeed.

name: Camp Steiner
description: A Scouts BSA camp on a lake at 10,400 feet in the Uinta Mountains — one of the highest-elevation scout camps in America — where troops earn badges between alpine lakes and granite peaks.

name: Camp Stella Maris
description: A Catholic camp on Conesus Lake south of Rochester that's been a rite of passage for western New York families since 1926 — big waterfront, big spirit, and Mass under the trees.

name: Camp Sternberg
description: A large Orthodox girls' camp near Narrowsburg known for its horseback riding, expansive grounds, and lively torah-infused programming that draws frum families from across the tri-state area.

name: Camp Stevens
description: An Episcopal camp in the oak-and-pine mountains near Julian, with organic gardens, art barns, and an intentionally inclusive, questioning-friendly spirituality.

name: Camp Stewart for Boys
description: One of the oldest boys' camps in Texas, Stewart spreads across 500-plus acres on the Guadalupe with riflery, horses, sports, and river swimming at its core. It's the classic Hill Country boyhood experience, run with old-school structure and spirit.

name: Camp Stone
description: Bnei Akiva's camp in northwest Pennsylvania with a distinctive chalutz (pioneer) theme - farming, horses, and wilderness built into a religious Zionist program that Midwest and East Coast Orthodox families treasure.

name: Camp Stoney
description: The Episcopal Diocese of the Rio Grande's small camp in the hills outside Santa Fe, offering gentle, inclusive weeks of hiking, crafts, and evening chapel under big New Mexico skies.

name: Camp Strake
description: The Sam Houston Area Council's rebuilt flagship near Sam Houston National Forest — a brand-new campus from 2020 with a big lake, modern lodges, and program built for Houston's huge Scouting community.

name: Camp Strawderman
description: A wonderfully old-fashioned girls camp in a Shenandoah Valley hollow, centered on horseback riding, mountain trail rides, and theatrical productions — little changed since 1929, by design.

name: Camp Sumatanga
description: The North Alabama United Methodist camp beneath Chandler Mountain, with a lake, mountaintop chapel hikes, and simple weeklong sessions that have anchored Alabama Methodist childhoods since 1948.

name: Camp Summit
description: A barrier-free camp northwest of Fort Worth serving campers with disabilities of all ages, where activities from horseback to the pool are adapted rather than watered down. Staff ratios are high and the culture is joyfully capable.

name: Camp Sunnyside
description: Easterseals Iowa's camp on Des Moines' north side, where kids of all abilities camp side by side with adaptive equipment, a pool, and decades of inclusion expertise.

name: Camp Sunshine
description: A one-of-a-kind retreat on Sebago Lake where entire families of children with life-threatening illnesses attend together for free — kids get camp, parents get support groups and rest, siblings get seen. Volunteers essentially run the place.

name: Camp Susan Curtis
description: A tuition-free camp on Trout Lake serving Maine children from economically disadvantaged families, pairing classic camp fun with year-round mentoring and leadership development.

name: Camp Susque
description: A small nondenominational Christian camp in a remote hollow of the Pennsylvania Wilds north of Williamsport, big on creek exploration, wilderness skills, and unhurried discipleship. Wonderfully off the grid.

name: Camp Susquehannock
description: One of the oldest camps in Pennsylvania, running separate boys and girls programs on Tripp Lake in the Endless Mountains since 1905. Known for sportsmanship-first athletics and a proudly unplugged, old-school feel.

name: Camp Swatara
description: A Church of the Brethren camp in the mountains of eastern Pennsylvania with a swimming lake, nature center, and a century of peaceable outdoor ministry.

name: Camp Sweeney
description: The storied Texas camp for children with type 1 diabetes, running three-week sessions with a full medical team, its own research pedigree, and big traditions like musical productions and lake olympics. One of the largest diabetes camps anywhere.

name: Camp Sweyolakan
description: Camp Fire's boat-access-only camp on Mica Bay of Lake Coeur d'Alene, running since 1922 with open-air 'kiwanis' sleeping shelters and deep song traditions. Arriving by boat sets the tone for an unplugged week.

name: Camp Sycamore Hills
description: Girl Scouts of Middle Tennessee's resident camp in the wooded hills along Sycamore Creek, with a pool, horses, and progression-based outdoor skills. Open to all girls, scout or not.

name: Camp T. Brady Saunders
description: The Heart of Virginia Council's camp west of Richmond, a dependable full-program operation with lake aquatics and one of the state's better COPE courses.

name: Camp Ta-Kum-Ta
description: A free Lake Champlain camp for Vermont-area children who have or have had cancer, run largely by volunteers with medical support so kids in and out of treatment can swim, tube, and just be kids again.

name: Camp Taconic
description: A family-owned coed camp on a hundred Berkshires acres that balances strong athletics with real arts and waterfront programs. It has a polished feel but a warm, multi-generation community.

name: Camp Tahosa
description: A small high-altitude camp at 9,000 feet on Tumblesome Lake near the Indian Peaks, focused on backcountry and older-Scout adventure rather than a traditional merit badge mill.

name: Camp Tall Timbers
description: A coed camp in the eastern West Virginia mountains near the Virginia line, popular with DC-area families for its huge fully elective activity menu, from waterskiing and paintball to horseback riding.

name: Camp Talooli
description: A Camp Fire council camp on a quiet lake north of Syracuse with 75 years of small-scale, inclusive tradition — canoes, crafts, and council fires under tall pines.

name: Camp Tamarack
description: An independent camp on little Dark Lake near Sisters, running since the 1930s with canoes, horses, and high-desert pine forest all around. Small sessions and an easygoing central Oregon character.

name: Tanah Keeta Scout Reservation
description: South Florida's Scout camp on the Loxahatchee River near Jupiter, where canoeing past cypress and the occasional manatee makes for a distinctly subtropical camp week.

name: Camp Tanako
description: A United Methodist camp on Lake Catherine near Hot Springs, offering gentle week-long sessions of swimming, canoeing, and campfire worship in the pine hills. A steady, affordable Arkansas church-camp classic.

name: Camp Tanasi
description: The Girl Scout Council of the Southern Appalachians' camp on Norris Lake north of Knoxville, with a beautiful clear-water lakefront, kayaking, and classic unit-based camp life. A budget-friendly East Tennessee staple.

name: Camp Tanuga
description: A family-owned coed camp in the woods near Kalkaska with a devoted metro-Detroit following, mixing waterfront and sports with up-north dirt-under-the-nails camp culture.

name: Camp Tapawingo
description: A century-old girls' camp on Keyes Pond in Sweden, Maine, cherished for its singing traditions, unpretentious warmth, and generations of returning families.

name: Camp Tapawingo
description: Deerfoot Lodge's sister camp near Speculator, giving girls the same Adirondack wilderness diet — canoe trips, campcraft, and Christian community — in a warm, unhurried setting.

name: Camp Taum Sauk
description: An adventure camp at the foot of the Ozarks' highest country near Johnson's Shut-Ins, sending campers rappelling, caving, and floating the Black River from a rustic base camp. For kids who want real wilderness over manicured facilities.

name: Camp Tavor
description: The Midwest's Habonim Dror camp near Three Rivers, a youth-led kibbutz-style community where campers share chores, debate ideas, and build fierce lifelong bonds — progressive Jewish camping at its most idealistic.

name: Camp Tavor (Habonim Dror)
description: The Midwest's Habonim Dror camp in southwest Michigan, a small kibbutz-inspired community where Chicago and Detroit kids garden, debate, sing in Hebrew, and practice youth-led democracy on a quiet lake.

name: Camp Tawonga
description: A beloved Bay Area Jewish camp on the Tuolumne River just outside Yosemite, where progressive Jewish community-building meets serious wilderness backpacking. Generations of families swear by its warm, inclusive culture.

name: Camp Tecumseh
description: A large YMCA camp on the Tippecanoe River in northwest Indiana, blending a strong horse and adventure program with the Y's Christian character values.

name: Camp Tecumseh
description: The Salvation Army's camp in Hunterdon County farmland, giving thousands of kids from New Jersey cities low-cost or sponsored weeks of pool time, sports, and wholesome community.

name: Camp Tecumseh YMCA
description: Indiana's most beloved YMCA camp, on the Tippecanoe River near Lafayette, famous for its counselor culture, values talks, and waitlists that form the day registration opens.

name: Camp Tejas
description: A well-kept Christian camp and retreat center between Austin and Houston near Giddings, hosting summer youth weeks with lakes, ropes courses, and a service-hearted staff culture. Popular with central Texas churches.

name: Camp Teko (Temple Israel Minneapolis)
description: Temple Israel of Minneapolis's lakeside day camp on Lake Minnetonka's shores, a Reform-rooted summer spot where Twin Cities kids swim, sail, and sing their way through Jewish summer.

name: Camp Tekoa
description: The United Methodist camp of Western North Carolina, set on a small lake near Hendersonville. Affordable one-week sessions blend classic camp fun with gentle faith formation and a notably inclusive spirit.

name: Camp Tel Noar
description: The Cohen Camps' lakeside community on Sunset Lake in Hampstead, a warmer, smaller cousin to the big Maine Jewish camps, with strong swim instruction and a family-like feel.

name: Camp Tel Yehudah
description: Young Judaea's national teen camp on the Delaware River in Barryville, where Jewish teens from across the country mix leadership seminars and Israel education with river swims and camp mischief.

name: Camp Ten Trees
description: A Seattle-based nonprofit running residential sessions at rented forest sites for LGBTQ+ youth and children of LGBTQ+ families. Sliding-scale pricing and a fiercely affirming, arts-forward community.

name: Camp Tesomas
description: The Samoset Council's flagship Scout camp north of Rhinelander, widely regarded as one of the best Scout summer camps in the country, with a huge waterfront and high-adventure add-ons.

name: Camp Tevya
description: A Cohen Camps sibling on Lake Potanipo where Jewish culture is woven into classic camp life — waterfront mornings, musical theater, and Shabbat traditions families pass down.

name: Camp Texlake
description: Central Texas's Girl Scout camp on Lake Travis in the Hill Country, where swimming, paddleboarding, and limestone-canyon hikes fill long Texas summer days.

name: Camp Thoreau
description: A Concord day camp in the Thoreau tradition of woods, ponds, and unhurried childhood, run by the same family ethos for generations. Strong swim instruction and a gentle, nature-first culture.

name: Camp Thorpe
description: A Green Mountain camp in Goshen serving campers with special needs for nearly a century, with adaptive activities, talent shows, and a staff famous for patience and joy.

name: Camp Three Falls
description: The Ventura County Council's Scout camp in Lockwood Valley beneath Mt. Pinos, with horse programs, shooting sports, and high-desert-meets-pine-forest scenery. An unpretentious troop-week classic.

name: Camp Thunderbird
description: Twin camps for boys and for girls on opposite shores near Bemidji, running strong athletics, waterskiing, and wilderness trips with a spirited, largely Minneapolis-and-Chicago camper base since 1946.

name: Camp Thurman
description: A wooded Christian day camp hidden in the middle of Arlington-Pantego, famous locally for its zipline, spirited counselors, and themed weeks that kids quote all school year. A Metroplex summer staple for over 50 years.

name: Camp Tiak
description: The Pine Burr Area Council's Scout camp in the longleaf pines near Wiggins, giving south Mississippi Scouts their merit-badge summers with a lake, shooting sports, and thick camp lore.

name: Camp Timanous
description: A small, quietly traditional boys' camp on Panther Pond founded on Luther Gulick's mind-body-spirit philosophy. Barefoot mornings, wooden war canoes, and a community where every boy is known.

name: Camp Timber-lee
description: A big Christian camp on 570 acres near East Troy with horses, a nature center full of animals, and specialty weeks, drawing kids from Milwaukee and Chicago suburbs alike.

name: Camp Timber Ridge
description: Girl Scouts of Greater Atlanta's close-in camp in Mableton, offering day and resident weeks with a pool, ponies, and classic badge-style adventure just outside the Perimeter. You don't have to be a Girl Scout to come.

name: Camp Timber Tops
description: A spirited girls camp in the Poconos run by the same family as Pine Forest and Lake Owego, big on sisterhood, singing, and lake days. Draws heavily from the Philadelphia area with a culturally Jewish feel.

name: Camp Timbercrest
description: Girl Scouts of Western New York's overnight camp on a private lake near Randolph, with a full riding stable, archery, and badge-rich weeks in deep Allegheny woods.

name: Timberlake (Farm & Wilderness)
description: Farm & Wilderness's original boys camp in Plymouth, with Quaker roots, farm chores, mud, axes, and mountain trips — rugged simplicity as a deliberate philosophy.

name: Camp Timberlake
description: Merri-Mac's brother camp for boys, offering mountain biking, kayaking, and tribal games in a tight-knit Christian community in the Black Mountains.

name: Camp Timberlane for Boys
description: A boys' camp on Towanda Lake known for letting campers choose their own daily activities and for an unusually warm, goofy culture — the emphasis is friendship first, competition second.

name: Camp Timberline
description: A Christian sports and adventure camp near Rocky Mountain National Park where energetic college-age staff lead climbing, mountain biking, and summit hikes alongside evening worship. High energy and heavy on fun.

name: Camp Timpoochee
description: Florida's oldest 4-H camp, sitting right on Choctawhatchee Bay in Niceville, where Panhandle kids sail, seine for marine critters, and learn coastal ecology during structured county weeks.

name: Camp Tioga
description: A modern coed camp on a private lake in the northern Poconos with a friendly, spirit-heavy culture and a strong mix of sports, water, and creative arts. Known for warm owner-directors who know every camper.

name: Camp Togowoods
description: The Girl Scouts of Alaska's wilderness camp near Wasilla, where girls canoe, build fires, and hike under the midnight sun. Genuine Alaskan camp skills with a supportive all-girls culture.

name: Camp Tohkomeupog
description: A rustic boys camp on Purity Lake run by the same family since 1932, where wilderness trips into the Whites anchor a program of canoeing, riflery, and honest hard play.

name: Camp Tom Hale
description: An Oklahoma camp tucked into the Ouachita Mountains near the Talimena Scenic Drive, giving Tulsa-area Scouts genuine mountain hiking without leaving the state.

name: Camp Tommy
description: A free Fresh Air Fund camp for teen boys at Sharpe Reservation, backed by Tommy Hilfiger, focusing on leadership and mentorship alongside lake swims and ridge hikes.

name: Camp Ton-A-Wandah
description: A traditional girls camp tucked into a wooded cove outside Hendersonville, big on singing, spirit, and classic activities from riflery to riding. Generations of Southern families rotate through its short sessions.

name: Camp TPS
description: The Philadelphia School's city day camp, blending creative arts, science, and outdoor play with weekly enrollment for center-city and West Philly families. Progressive, hands-on, and warm.

name: Camp Treetops
description: A progressive farm-and-wilderness camp at North Country School near Lake Placid, where kids do real barn chores, ride, garden, and take serious mountain trips over a single seven-week season. No screens, no competition.

name: Camp Trinity
description: The Episcopal camp at Trinity Center on Bogue Banks, uniquely set between ocean and sound so campers surf, seine the marsh, and worship barefoot in the same day.

name: Camp Tuckabatchee
description: A scrappy, independent nonprofit camp in the bluffs near Starved Rock country, keeping traditional camping affordable for Illinois Valley families since 1926.

name: Camp Turk
description: A Masonic-supported nonprofit camp on Round Lake near the southwestern Adirondacks, offering a full traditional program — swimming, archery, theater — at heavily subsidized rates.

name: Camp Turner
description: The Diocese of Buffalo's camp inside Allegany State Park, where campers hike park trails, swim, and celebrate outdoor Mass surrounded by some of the wildest country in western New York.

name: Camp Tuscarora
description: The Baden-Powell Council's Scout reservation on a lake near Windsor in the Southern Tier, a longtime summer base for merit badges, aquatics, and outdoor skills for Binghamton-area troops.

name: Camp Tuttle
description: The Episcopal Diocese of Utah's small camp at the top of Big Cottonwood Canyon near Brighton, known for a gentle, radically welcoming culture and alpine hikes right out the back door.

name: Camp Twin Creeks
description: A coed camp in the Allegheny Mountains near the Greenbrier River, offering a broad, choice-driven program from whitewater and climbing to theater and arts, with a friendly, international staff.

name: Camp Twin Lakes
description: A fully adaptive Georgia camp serving children with serious illnesses, disabilities, and other life challenges, hosting dozens of partner-organization weeks each summer at little or no cost to families. Medical staff on site and every activity is accessible.

name: Camp Unalayee
description: A rugged, nonprofit wilderness camp at 6,200 feet in the Trinity Alps, reachable only by a long dirt road, where backpacking trips and lake swims replace electricity and cell service entirely. Founded on intercultural community ideals in 1949.

name: Camp Union
description: A small coed camp on Otter Lake in Greenfield with a family-run feel, a mix of American and international campers, and a flexible, try-everything program.

name: Camp Utmost
description: A Christian camp in the mountains outside Bozeman offering classic summer weeks of hiking, games, and campfire teaching for youth from the Gallatin Valley and beyond.

name: Camp Vacamas
description: A century-old nonprofit camp in the Highlands of northern New Jersey with a mission of serving kids across the income spectrum, mixing lake days and mountain trails with generous scholarship support.

name: Camp Victory
description: A special-purpose camp facility in rural Columbia County that hosts week-long medically supervised camps for kids with diabetes, cancer, autism, and other health needs. Full camp fun with nurses and specialists on site.

name: Camp Virgil Tate
description: The Kanawha County 4-H camp in the hills north of Charleston, a longtime home for affordable summer 4-H weeks with a pool, hiking trails, and the traditional West Virginia tribe camp structure.

name: Camp Virginia
description: A traditional boys camp in the Goshen Pass area of the Alleghenies, keeping alive an old Virginia camp formula of sports, river swims, riflery, and gentlemanly manners.

name: Camp Voyageur
description: A small boys' wilderness camp on the edge of the Boundary Waters near Ely, where every session builds toward canoe trips — older boys venture into Quetico and beyond. Screens don't stand a chance here.

name: Camp Wa-Klo
description: A traditional girls camp beneath Mount Monadnock on Thorndike Pond, where riding, sailing, and decades-old honor traditions build a quietly confident sisterhood.

name: Camp WaBak
description: South Carolina's Girl Scout camp in the Blue Ridge foothills near Marietta, with a mountain creek, pool, and badge-friendly adventure weeks open to Scouts and newcomers alike.

name: Camp Wabanna
description: A nondenominational Christian camp on the Chesapeake shore near Mayo, with a busy waterfront, blob, and high-energy chapel program that draws church youth groups from around the region.

name: Camp Wah-Nee
description: A spirited coed camp in the Litchfield hills with a big tri-state following, mixing competitive leagues, a strong social culture, and Friday night traditions. Teens especially rave about the older-camper program.

name: Wah-Tut-Ca Scout Reservation
description: A Boston-area council camp on Northwood Lake in southern New Hampshire known for its patrol-cooking tradition — troops cook nearly every meal in their own sites, the old way.

name: Camp Wakpominee
description: The Twin Rivers Council's Scout camp in the hills near Fort Ann, offering classic merit-badge summers, a strong waterfront, and shooting sports on a lake in the southeastern Adirondack foothills.

name: Camp Waldemar
description: Famous for its stone architecture, tribe competitions, and legendary dining hall etiquette, Waldemar has polished generations of Texas girls on the Guadalupe since 1926. Expect deep traditions, strong horsemanship, and a waitlist that starts at birth.

name: Camp Walden
description: One of the country's oldest girls' camps, on Walden Pond in Denmark since 1916. A spirited, sisterhood-forward community that balances classic activities with real leadership development.

name: Camp Walden
description: A coed camp on Long Lake in northern Michigan known for its inclusive, arts-friendly culture and camper-chosen schedules — a haven for kids who want camp without cliques.

name: Camp Walden
description: A classic private camp on Trout Lake near Lake George with polished facilities, strong athletics and dance, and a spirited color-war tradition drawing heavily from the NYC metro area.

name: Camp Waluhili
description: Camp Fire Green Country's rustic camp near Fort Gibson Lake, known for its horse program and unfussy outdoor traditions that have served eastern Oklahoma kids since the 1940s. Small, sweet, and affordable.

name: Camp Wamatochick
description: A Camp Fire camp in the pines near Prescott with generations of history, keeping traditional camp alive — songs, crafts, hikes, and council fires — at a family-friendly price.

name: Wanakee United Methodist Center
description: A gentle United Methodist camp in the hills above Meredith where week-long sessions mix pond time, crafts, and inclusive, open-hearted faith conversation.

name: Camp Wanocksett
description: A Scout summer camp on Thorndike Pond in the shadow of Mount Monadnock, serving troops with merit-badge programs, a strong waterfront, and mountain hikes right from camp.

name: Camp Wapogasset (Camp Wapo)
description: A beloved Lutheran Bible camp on Lake Wapogasset in western Wisconsin — 'Wapo' to thousands of Minnesota and Wisconsin kids — famous for exuberant worship, lake days, and counselors kids idolize.

name: Camp War Eagle
description: A Northwest Arkansas camp on Beaver Lake with sliding-scale pricing so kids from every income level share the same cabins, sports, and Christian community.

name: Camp Wartburg
description: A Lutheran (LCMS) camp south of St. Louis with a pool, ropes course, and welcoming weeks for campers with disabilities alongside traditional cabin camps.

name: Camp Washington
description: The Episcopal Church in Connecticut's camp in the Litchfield hills, mixing classic camp weeks with quiet chapel time and a strongly inclusive, come-as-you-are ethos.

name: Camp Watchaug
description: The Ocean Community YMCA's day camp on Watchaug Pond beside Burlingame State Park, where South County kids spend summers swimming, boating, and hiking the campfire-scented woods.

name: Camp Watonka
description: A rare thing: a traditional lakeside boys' camp in the Poconos built around real hands-on science, with labs in chemistry, rocketry, robotics, and ham radio alongside sailing and campfires. Kids do serious experiments in the morning and cannonball off the dock in the afternoon.

name: Camp Waukeela
description: A century-old girls camp on Crystal Lake in the foothills of the Whites, known for its sisterhood rituals, strong arts program, and a lake so clear it looks painted.

name: Camp Wawbeek
description: Easterseals Wisconsin's fully accessible camp in the Dells, giving campers with physical disabilities the whole camp experience — climbing wall, campouts, and mud hikes included — since 1938.

name: Camp Wawbeek (Easterseals Wisconsin)
description: Easterseals Wisconsin's flagship camp in the Dells, hosting campers with physical disabilities since the 1930s on a wooded, fully accessible campus with adapted climbing, hayrides, and a beloved staff culture.

name: Camp Wawenock
description: A full-season girls' camp on Sebago Lake that has quietly perfected its craft since 1910 - one seven-week session, no cliques tolerated, and a community so close that nearly every camper returns.

name: Camp Wawona
description: A Seventh-day Adventist camp actually inside Yosemite National Park at Wawona, running horse, rock-climbing, and adventure weeks with the park as its backyard. One of the most extraordinary locations of any camp in America.

name: Camp Wayfarer
description: A family-owned coed camp at the site of a historic Flat Rock camp, mixing brother-sister style traditions with polished programming in sports, riding, and mountain adventure.

name: Camp Waziyatah
description: The coed camp on McWain Pond made famous by the 'Bug Juice' documentary series, now known for flexible sessions, a choose-your-own-adventure schedule, and a refreshingly unpretentious camper mix.

name: Camp Weed
description: The Episcopal Diocese of Florida's camp near Live Oak, gathering kids from across north Florida for lakefront weeks of worship, arts, and unhurried friendship on a big piney campus.

name: Camp Wekeela
description: A polished coed camp on the Bear Ponds in Hartford with a huge elective menu, strong bunk spirit, and an inclusive vibe that draws campers from across the US and abroad.

name: Camp Wesley Pines
description: A United Methodist camp in the piney woods of south-central Mississippi with a small lake, low ropes, and warm weeklong sessions at church-camp prices. Unassuming and kind.

name: Camp Wesley Woods
description: The Holston Conference United Methodist camp in Walland/Townsend on the doorstep of the Great Smoky Mountains, with mountain streams, hiking, and gentle faith formation. Simple, affordable, and deeply rooted in East Tennessee.

name: Camp Westminster
description: A Presbyterian camp on Higgins Lake — one of Michigan's clearest — serving Detroit-area congregations and beyond with classic cabin weeks and a welcoming, service-minded ethos.

name: Camp Westminster
description: A Presbyterian camp on the Yellow River just east of Atlanta offering day and overnight sessions with horses, a lake, and Reformed faith teaching. Close enough to the city for anxious first-time parents.

name: Camp Westmont
description: A large, polished coed camp in Wayne County with strong facilities across sports, arts, and waterfront, drawing many families from the New York metro area. Energetic, social, and well staffed.

name: Camp Westwind
description: A YWCA-founded camp on 500 wild acres where the Salmon River meets the Pacific, reachable only by boat across the estuary. Ocean beach hikes, mudflat ecology, and a deeply loyal Portland following.

name: Camp Whippoorwill
description: The girls' side of North Country Camps, sharing Augur Lake with brother camp Lincoln. Riding, canoe trips, and a gentle, screen-free culture where girls get genuinely comfortable outdoors.

name: Camp Whispering Pines
description: Louisiana East's Girl Scout camp in the tall pines north of New Orleans, giving generations of Louisiana girls their week of horses, canoes, and cicada-song nights.

name: Camp Whispering Winds
description: The Girl Scouts of Oregon and SW Washington's camp in the Coast Range foothills west of Corvallis, with a popular horse program, a pool, and cabin-and-tent units in the firs. Steady, confidence-building camp classic.

name: Camp Whitman on Seneca Lake
description: A Presbyterian camp on Seneca Lake near Dresden offering gentle week-long sessions of swimming, campfires, and faith exploration, including dedicated weeks for campers with disabilities.

name: Camp Whitsett
description: A classic Sierra Nevada Scout camp among the sequoias of the Kern Plateau, with a lake for sailing and rowing plus treks into the Golden Trout Wilderness. Old-school mountain Scouting done well.

name: Camp Wightman
description: A Baptist camp on Billings Lake in the quiet woods of southeastern Connecticut, offering affordable faith-based weeks with swimming, campfires, and evening worship under the pines.

name: Camp Wigwam
description: One of Maine's oldest boys' camps, tucked on Bear Lake in Waterford since 1910. A balanced sports-and-arts program with a famous golf pitch-and-putt and an easygoing, big-brotherly culture.

name: Camp Wilderness
description: The Northern Lights Council's northwoods camp near Itasca headwaters country, a true lakes-and-loons camp serving troops from the Dakotas and northern Minnesota.

name: Camp Wildwood
description: A classic boys' sports camp on Woods Pond in Bridgton with strong instruction across athletics and a spirited color-war tradition. Energetic but warmer and less cutthroat than its reputation-heavy neighbors.

name: Camp William B. Snyder
description: The National Capital Area Council's modern Cub Scout camp near Haymarket, running day and short resident sessions with BB ranges, pools, and pirate-ship-level enthusiasm for younger scouts.

name: Camp William Hinds
description: The Pine Tree Council's Boy Scout camp on Panther Pond in Raymond, hosting Scouts BSA troops for merit-badge weeks, waterfront fun, and high-adventure programming each summer.

name: Camp Willow Run
description: A Christian camp on Lake Gaston where campers famously sleep in converted train boxcars. Big-lake watersports — skiing, tubing, blobbing — meet energetic evangelical programming.

name: Camp Wilmot
description: A small Presbyterian camp in the Kearsarge foothills with a pay-what-you-can heart, garden-to-table meals, and an unhurried, faith-rooted community feel.

name: Camp Winaukee
description: A big, spirited private boys camp on Lake Winnipesaukee with a mainland campus for younger boys and an island campus for older ones. Strong athletics and waterskiing with a polished program.

name: Camp Wing
description: A nonprofit camp in the Duxbury pines run by Crossroads, serving a deliberately diverse mix of kids - many on full scholarship - with classic camp activities and strong social-emotional support.

name: Camp Wingate*Kirkland
description: A small all-gender camp tucked into the Cape Cod pines where kids build their own schedules and kindness is the loudest value. Consistently praised for making every kind of kid feel at home.

name: Camp Wingmann
description: The Episcopal camp on Lake Byrd in Avon Park serving central and south Florida dioceses, with old-Florida oak hammocks, lake swims, and warm liturgical camp life.

name: Camp Winnarainbow
description: The circus and performing arts camp founded by Woodstock legend Wavy Gravy, where kids sleep in tipis and learn juggling, stilt-walking, trapeze, and clowning in the Mendocino hills. Counterculture warmth is the whole point.

name: Camp Winnataska
description: An interdenominational Christian camp east of Birmingham running since 1918, with creek swimming under a waterfall, Indian-lore pageantry, and a fiercely devoted volunteer alumni base. Old Alabama camp magic at a modest price.

name: Camp Winnekeag
description: A Seventh-day Adventist camp on Lake Winnekeag in north-central Massachusetts offering horses, waterskiing, and worship in a wholesome, affordable package.

name: Camp Winton
description: A small, boat-access Scout camp on the Bear River Reservoir in the El Dorado National Forest, known for its intimate size and strong waterfront. The kind of camp where staff know every Scout's name by Tuesday.

name: Camp Wise
description: Cleveland's historic JCC overnight camp, running since 1907, where Jewish culture, Shabbat traditions, and classic lake camp life mix with a famously spirited community.

name: Camp Wiyaka
description: A tiny, wonderfully unpolished YMCA camp on Sandy Pond run by the Athol Area Y since the 1890s — one of the oldest continuously operating camps anywhere, and one of the cheapest.

name: Camp Wojtyla
description: A rugged Catholic wilderness camp in the mountains above Boulder, inspired by St. John Paul II's love of the outdoors — backpacking, climbing, and the sacraments at altitude.

name: Camp Wolfeboro
description: A rustic Sierra camp on the Stanislaus River where troops still cross a footbridge into camp and cook some meals in their own sites. Proudly old-fashioned, river-swimming Scouting.

name: Camp Wonderland
description: The Salvation Army's camp on Lake Massapoag, giving Boston-area kids a nearly free week of classic camp - swimming, campfires, and chapel included. A quiet engine of access for families who could never afford camp otherwise.

name: Camp Wood YMCA
description: Kansas' grand old YMCA camp in the Flint Hills tallgrass prairie, where sunset horse rides, a spring-fed lake, and big-sky campfires give kids a genuinely Kansan summer.

name: Camp Woodland
description: The girls' half of a brother-sister pair with Towering Pines, Camp Woodland offers riding, sailing, and gentle Northwoods traditions with shared events across the road at the boys' camp.

name: Camp Woodmont
description: A small family-run camp on Lookout Mountain that specializes in younger and first-time campers, with horses, a farm-like feel, and lots of individual attention. Values-based but not religious.

name: Woodruff Scout Camp
description: The Atlanta Area Council's flagship Scout camp in the mountains near Blairsville, considered one of the best BSA summer camps in the country for its whitewater, climbing, and merit badge program. Troops book it a year out.

name: Camp Woolman
description: A small Quaker-rooted camp on the Sierra Friends campus near Nevada City, blending swimming-pond afternoons and farm-and-garden mornings with peace-centered community practices like silent reflection. Gentle, values-forward, and unhurried.

name: Camp Workcoeman
description: One of the oldest Scout camps in the country, on West Hill Pond since 1924, running merit badge weeks with a famously clear-water lakefront. Scouting tradition thick enough to cut.

name: Camp Wright
description: An Episcopal camp on the tip of Kent Island where the Chesapeake meets the Chester River, offering sailing, crabbing, and gentle faith community in a stunning bayfront setting.

name: Camp Wyandot
description: A small nonprofit camp in the Hocking Hills with Camp Fire roots, beloved for hiking among the sandstone gorges, council fires, and an earthy, inclusive spirit.

name: Camp Wyldewood
description: A Church of Christ camp in the wooded hills outside Searcy, staffed heavily by Harding University students who bring big-sibling energy to a week of swimming, sports, and Bible classes. Modest cost, earnest heart.

name: Camp Wyman
description: One of America's oldest camps, founded in 1898 in the wooded hills of Eureka outside St. Louis, now run by the Wyman Center with a mission focus on kids from low-income families. High ropes, hiking, and a century-deep commitment to access.

name: Camp Wyoming
description: A Presbyterian camp in the rolling hills of eastern Iowa with a farm-like feel — gardens, animals, creek hikes — and grounded, low-key faith programming.

name: Camp Yavneh
description: A pluralistic but seriously Jewish camp on Lucas Pond in Northwood, where kosher kitchens, Hebrew learning, and Shabbat observance sit comfortably alongside color war and lake swims.

name: Camp Yawgoog
description: Rhode Island's grand old Scout reservation, running since 1916 with three sub-camps around Yawgoog Pond and some of the deepest traditions in Northeast Scouting.

name: West Suburban YMCA Camp Chickami
description: The West Suburban Y's traditional day camp busing greater Boston kids to a pond-and-woods property for swimming, nature, and camp skills. Affordable, community-minded, and outdoorsy.

name: Camp Yomi (92NY)
description: The 92nd Street Y's classic Manhattan day camp, busing kids to a leafy Rockland County campus for swim, sports, and arts with a lightly Jewish, very New York sensibility - running for generations.

name: Camp Young Judaea Midwest
description: A close-knit Zionist youth movement camp in Waupaca where Israeli culture, Hebrew songs, and lake time combine in a smaller setting that first-time campers find easy to join.

name: Camp Young Judaea (New England)
description: One of the oldest Zionist camps in America, on Lake Baboosic in southern New Hampshire, where kosher food, Shabbat, and Israeli culture come wrapped in a classic New England lake-camp package. Small and deeply traditional.

name: Camp Young Judaea
description: A spirited Zionist youth camp on Otter Lake in Amherst where Israeli culture, Hebrew songs, and lake-camp classics blend into a famously tight-knit community.

name: Camp Young Judaea Sprout Lake
description: Young Judaea's junior camp in the Hudson Valley, a pluralistic Zionist camp with kosher food, Shabbat observance, and a big-hearted Israeli culture program for younger campers. Feeds into Tel Yehudah for teens.

name: Camp Young Judaea Texas
description: The Southwest's flagship Zionist youth camp, gathering Jewish kids from Houston, Dallas, and beyond to a Wimberley hilltop for Israeli culture, ruach-filled song sessions, and classic camp fun. Strong community feel with kosher-style dining.

name: Camp Zanika Lache
description: A rustic Camp Fire camp on the shore of Lake Wenatchee in the eastern Cascades near Leavenworth. Mountain lake swimming, canoeing, and starry-night campfires with a small, tight-knit camper community.

name: Camp Zeke
description: A wellness-centered Jewish camp in the Poconos built around fitness, culinary arts, yoga, and body-positive health, with farm-to-table kosher food and a gentle, judgment-free culture.

name: Camp Zephyr
description: South Texas' longtime Baptist encampment on Lake Corpus Christi, where church groups from the Valley to San Antonio gather for lake swimming, rec tournaments, and revival-style worship weeks. A rite of passage for generations of South Texas kids.

name: Camps Equinunk & Blue Ridge
description: Century-old brother-sister camps facing each other in the far northern Poconos, with deep traditions, strong athletics, and a fiercely loyal multi-generation family base. The full-summer program builds tight bunk bonds.

name: Camps Kenwood & Evergreen
description: A brother-sister camp on Eagle Pond in Wilmot pairing excellent facilities with an intentional friendship-skills culture — separate boys' and girls' camps that share special events.

name: Camps Kenwood & Kenmont
description: Brother-sister camps sharing a lakefront campus in Kent, with polished facilities, strong sports leagues, and shared evening events that keep siblings connected. A Litchfield County staple for tri-state families.

name: Camps 'R' Us
description: A multi-site Long Island day camp with roughly a dozen campuses across Nassau and Suffolk, pitched as the affordable neighborhood alternative to the big private camps. Flexible weeks and lots of locations make logistics easy.

name: Campus by the Sea
description: InterVarsity's boat-access-only camp in a private cove on Catalina Island, hosting family and youth weeks with snorkeling, kayaking, and beachfront worship. A rustic slice of island paradise with a ministry heart.

name: Canoe Island French Camp
description: A one-of-a-kind camp occupying its own 47-acre island in the San Juans, where French language and culture mix with kayaking, marine science, and fencing. Tiny by design — about 50 campers sleeping in teepees.

name: Canonicus Camp
description: An American Baptist camp and conference center on 300 wooded acres in Exeter, running classic overnight and day weeks with a pond waterfront and low-key Christian programming.

name: Canyon Camp
description: A surprisingly rugged camp in the unglaciated hill country of northwest Illinois, with limestone canyon hikes and a tight-knit feel that keeps Rockford-area troops loyal.

name: Canyon Creek Sports Camp
description: A sports-forward sleepaway camp in the canyons north of Santa Clarita, an hour from LA, where kids rotate through dozens of sports plus a pool, ropes course, and classic campfires. Popular for short first-time sessions.

name: Canyonlands Field Institute Youth Camps
description: A Moab-based nonprofit that runs small river and canyon camps for youth — rafting the Colorado, exploring redrock side canyons, and learning desert ecology from professional guides.

name: Capital Camps
description: The Jewish community camp for the Washington-Baltimore area, set in the Blue Ridge foothills of south-central Pennsylvania with modern facilities, a strong inclusion department, and joyful, pluralistic Jewish life.

name: Carol Joy Holling Camp
description: Nebraska's largest Lutheran camp, spread over rolling prairie ridge land between Lincoln and Omaha, with horse programs, tree houses, and warm ELCA-style community.

name: Carolina Creek Christian Camp
description: A lakeside Christian camp near Huntsville with waterslides, wake sports, and themed weeks that feel more like a festival than a retreat. Its energetic gospel programming has made it a favorite for Houston-area families and churches.

name: Carolina Creek
description: A lakefront Christian camp near Sam Houston National Forest with waterslides, wake sports, and themed weeks that keep the energy high and the gospel central.

name: Caroline Furnace Lutheran Camp
description: An ELCA camp tucked in the George Washington National Forest of Virginia's Fort Valley, with creek swimming, backpacking, and quiet mountain faith formation.

name: Cascades Camp and Conference Center
description: The Evangelical Covenant Church's large camp on the Nisqually River near Yelm, with a strong horse program, lake toys, and polished facilities. Runs day and overnight camps for kids across the south Sound.

name: Casowasco Camp & Retreat Center
description: A United Methodist camp spread along the wooded shore of Owasco Lake, running classic week sessions from sailing to high ropes with warm, low-pressure faith formation.

name: Castaway Club (Young Life)
description: Young Life's resort-quality camp on Pelican Lake near Detroit Lakes, where high schoolers get a week of wakeboarding, ridiculous games, and evening talks about faith — polished, loud, and joyful.

name: Catalina Sea Camp
description: A residential marine-biology camp at Toyon Bay on Catalina Island, reachable only by boat, where campers snorkel kelp forests, drive ROVs, and study the animals they just swam with. Older teens can add scuba certification.

name: The Cathedral Domain
description: The Episcopal Diocese of Lexington's mountain camp near the Red River Gorge, centered on a striking A-frame cathedral in the woods, with hiking, music, and famously affirming community weeks.

name: Catholic Youth Camp
description: Minnesota's Catholic Youth Camp on Big Sandy Lake has offered pontoons, campfires, and lakeside Mass since the 1940s, with a warm, come-as-you-are Catholic identity.

name: Cazadero Performing Arts Camp
description: A beloved Bay Area music camp in a redwood grove along Austin Creek, where band, orchestra, and jazz kids rehearse under the trees. Generations of Northern California musicians got their start at Caz.

name: Cedar Lake Camp
description: The teen-oriented flagship of NJY Camps in Milford, with strong leagues, a busy lake, and a Jewish cultural core that stays fun rather than preachy. Many campers arrive with years of Nah-Jee-Wah behind them.

name: Cedar Lake Camp (NJY Camps)
description: The teen village of NJY Camps in Milford, where middle and high schoolers get electives, lake sports, and a big social scene within a kosher, pluralistic Jewish framework.

name: Cedar Lakes Conference Center
description: A large state-owned conference and camp center near Ripley, West Virginia, best known for hosting FFA, 4-H, and youth leadership camps around its lakes, with swimming, fishing, and hands-on agricultural programs.

name: Cedarmore Camp
description: The Kentucky Baptist Convention's camp between Louisville and Frankfort, hosting church youth weeks with a lake, rec fields, and revival-style worship.

name: The Center for Courageous Kids
description: A SeriousFun-affiliated camp in south-central Kentucky serving children with serious illnesses at no cost, with disease-specific weeks, a full medical center, and a whimsical campus built around a giant barn. Families from across the region attend free.

name: Johns Hopkins CTY Summer Programs
