description: The Johns Hopkins Center for Talented Youth's residential academic camps, held on college campuses nationwide, where bright kids study everything from cryptology to philosophy for three intensive weeks. A defining experience for a certain kind of intellectually hungry teen.

name: Chanco on the James
description: The Episcopal camp of southern Virginia, set on bluffs above the James River with a riverfront pool, ropes course, and an open-hearted chapel life.

name: Channel 3 Kids Camp
description: A century-old nonprofit camp on the Skungamaug River that makes sure Connecticut kids of every income and ability get a real camp summer, with heavy scholarship support. Day and overnight programs, zero pretension.

name: Chapel Rock Camp
description: An Episcopal camp among the granite boulders and pines of Prescott, offering welcoming week-long sessions of swimming, crafts, hiking, and chapel in a cool mountain setting.

name: Chelsea Piers Summer Sports Camp
description: Manhattan's mega sports-complex day camp on the Hudson piers, where kids rotate through gymnastics, ice skating, rock climbing, soccer, and swimming in one facility. As convenient — and as costly — as NYC camps get.

name: Chewonki Camp for Boys
description: A nature-immersion camp on a saltwater peninsula, run by the well-regarded Chewonki Foundation. Boys tend farm animals, study tide pools, and head out on canoe and backpacking trips across Maine.

name: Chewonki Camp for Girls
description: Chewonki's girls' camp sits on remote Fourth Debsconeag Lake in the shadow of Katahdin - true North Woods wilderness with loons, paddling, and star-filled nights. Small, rustic, and deeply intentional.

name: Chicago Park District Day Camp
description: The city's massive six-week day camp program run at parks in nearly every neighborhood — beloved, chaotic in the summer-in-the-city way, and one of the best bargains in Chicago.

name: Children's Beach House
description: A nonprofit on the Delaware Bay near Lewes that runs supportive residential summer sessions for children with speech, language, and learning challenges, blending beach and bay activities with skill-building.

name: Chimney Corners Camp
description: The sister camp to Camp Becket, a YMCA girls camp in the Berkshires centered on leadership, tradition, and unplugged lakeside living. Alumnae loyalty here is famously intense.

name: Chippewa Ranch Camp
description: A girls' camp in Eagle River with a standout horseback riding program — campers can ride daily — alongside waterskiing and a big-sisterly cabin culture that keeps families coming back.

name: Choate Rosemary Hall Summer Programs
description: Summer academics on Choate's celebrated boarding-school campus, from writing workshops to a well-known young-scholars track, with dorm life and global classmates. Rigorous and resume-friendly.

name: Chop Point Camp
description: A small Christian coed camp on a peninsula where the Kennebec River meets Merrymeeting Bay, known for salt-water sailing, big wilderness trips, and a gentle, non-pushy faith culture.

name: Christikon
description: A small Lutheran camp deep in Montana's Boulder River valley near the Absaroka-Beartooth Wilderness, centered on backpacking trips, mountain worship, and unplugged simplicity.

name: Circle C Ranch
description: A western-themed Christian ranch camp south of Buffalo where every camper rides, the wranglers double as mentors, and chuckwagon cookouts end in gospel campfires.

name: Circle F Dude Ranch Camp
description: A western-style dude ranch camp improbably set among the lakes of central Florida, running since 1952 with daily riding, wake sports, and international campers who give it a worldly flavor.

name: Circle V Ranch Camp
description: A Catholic camp run by St. Vincent de Paul in the mountains above Lake Cachuma, giving Central Coast and LA kids a classic ranch-style week with a pool, archery, and hearty traditions. Generous scholarships keep it accessible.

name: Circus Smirkus Summer Camp
description: The training camp of Vermont's famous touring youth circus, where kids of all levels learn aerials, acrobatics, wire-walking, and clowning from professional coaches. Strong campers can aspire to the Smirkus Big Top Tour.

name: Joseph A. Citta Scout Reservation
description: A Pine Barrens camp near the Jersey Shore with a cedar lake, sandy trails, and easy trips to Barnegat Bay for the Jersey Shore Council's troops.

name: Clara Barton Camp
description: A camp for girls with type 1 diabetes, run by the Barton Center on Clara Barton's birthplace, with full medical staff so campers can just be kids. For many families it is the first place their daughter feels normal.

name: Clearwater Camp for Girls
description: A small, deliberately low-key girls' camp on Tomahawk Lake, with a beloved island unit for older campers and a strong sailing and canoeing program. The intimate size means every girl is known by name.

name: Clearview Day Camp
description: A large traditional day camp on wooded Monmouth County acreage, with multiple pools, sports, and adventure activities. High-energy camp spirit for central Jersey Shore-area families.

name: Clemson Outdoor Lab (Camp Sertoma and Camp Hope)
description: Clemson University's lakeside camp property, long home to Camp Sertoma for kids with hearing and communication differences and Camp Hope for campers with developmental disabilities — inclusive camping run with university backing.

name: Clover Patch Camp
description: A fully accessible camp near Schenectady run by the Center for Disability Services, giving campers with physical and developmental disabilities a real sleepaway week with adaptive everything and 24-hour nursing.

name: CM Ranch Youth Program
description: One of Wyoming's oldest dude ranches, near Dubois, whose summer youth program lets kids ride daily, fish mountain streams, and experience genuine ranch life at the edge of the Wind River wilderness.

name: Coastal Camp at NatureBridge
description: A nature day camp based at NatureBridge's historic Fort Cronkhite campus in the Marin Headlands, where campers tidepool, hike coastal bluffs, and study the Golden Gate's wild backyard with professional environmental educators. National-park summer, daily.

name: Cole Canoe Base
description: A Michigan camp on the Rifle River that doubles as a canoe base — troops mix standard merit badge weeks with multi-day paddle treks downriver. Water is the whole point here.

name: Coleman Country Day Camp
description: A big-hearted Merrick day camp with a western ranch theme, horseback riding, canoeing, and serious swim instruction. Known for its 'kindness counts' culture and slick parent communication.

name: Colorado Lions Camp
description: The Lions Clubs' Colorado camp near Woodland Park, serving campers who are blind, deaf, or have developmental disabilities with heavily subsidized weeks of classic camp fun and high staff support.

name: The Colorado Mountain Ranch
description: A family-owned ranch camp in the hills above Boulder at Gold Hill, running day and overnight sessions built on horses, western spirit, and a big-hearted, all-welcome philosophy.

name: Colvig Silver Camps
description: A family-run camp in the San Juans outside Durango where wilderness trips into the mountains and desert canyon country are the heart of the program. Kids progress from base-camp fun to real multi-day expeditions.

name: Comer Scout Reservation
description: The Greater Alabama Council's Scout camp atop Lookout Mountain near Mentone, sharing the same cool ridge air as the famous private camps at a fraction of the price. Solid merit-badge summers with mountain views.

name: Concordia Language Villages
description: The famous language-immersion camps in the Minnesota north woods where each 'village' recreates a country — campers get a passport, use foreign currency, and speak only the target language from Spanish to Norwegian to Arabic. Full immersion that actually sticks.

name: Congressional Camp
description: A long-running day camp on the wooded Congressional School campus in Falls Church, with pools, sports, and specialty tracks for inner-suburb DC families. Weekly flexibility and a polished program.

name: Cooperstown Baseball Camp
description: A traditional overnight baseball camp minutes from the Hall of Fame, where mornings are fundamentals and afternoons are games, with a pilgrimage to the museum built into every session. Baseball-obsessed kids are in heaven.

name: Copper Cannon Camp
description: A completely free camp in the White Mountains serving New Hampshire kids from low-income families — a week of mountain trails, pond swims, and belonging, at no cost to anyone.

name: Coppercreek Camp
description: A warm, choose-your-own-adventure camp in the Lost Sierra with a strong horse program, crafts barn, and creek swimming, run by the same family for decades. The community showed remarkable grit rebuilding spirit after the 2021 Dixie Fire devastated its hometown.

name: Corbin's Crusaders Day Camp
description: A sports-forward day camp on backcountry Greenwich acreage that has bused Manhattan and Connecticut kids to its fields, pond, and pool for decades. Old-school coaching energy with lots of game play.

name: Cottonwood Gulch Expeditions
description: A century-old expedition camp based near the Zuni Mountains that takes small treks across the Southwest — archaeology digs, canyon backpacking, and cultural exchange — from a rustic base camp. For kids who'd rather explore than compete.

name: Covenant Harbor
description: An Evangelical Covenant camp right on Geneva Lake with a premium waterfront — wakeboarding, the blob, sailing — and week-long sessions that fill up almost the moment registration opens.

name: Covenant Heights Camp
description: An Evangelical Covenant camp bordering Rocky Mountain National Park near Estes Park, offering classic cabin weeks with alpine hikes and campfire worship in a pine-forest setting.

name: Covenant Hills Christian Camp
description: A small Evangelical Covenant camp in the hills above Cabot where week-long sessions blend swimming, campfires, and earnest, friendly faith formation.

name: Coyote Central
description: A Central District Seattle nonprofit where middle schoolers take hands-on intensives — welding, filmmaking, culinary, carpentry — taught by professionals in real studios. Sliding-scale pricing keeps it accessible.

name: Cragged Mountain Farm
description: A small, intentionally rustic coed camp on a hilltop farm in Freedom where campers help run the community, swim in a spring-fed pond, and take real wilderness trips. Progressive roots, timeless feel.

name: Crestwood Country Day Camp
description: A big traditional day camp on a wooded Melville campus with pools, go-karts, and a packed activity schedule. A Long Island staple that generations of Huntington-area kids have grown up at.

name: Crooked Creek Ranch (Young Life)
description: A big, polished Young Life resort-camp in the Fraser Valley near Winter Park, built to wow high schoolers with ropes courses, a lake, and top-tier food alongside evening club talks.

name: Cross Point Camp
description: A United Methodist camp on a bluff over Lake Texoma near Kingston, filling summers with tubing, cliff-top sunsets, and campfire worship for Oklahoma youth groups. The lakefront location does a lot of the magic.

name: Cross Roads Camp & Retreat Center
description: A joint Lutheran-Episcopal camp in the Musconetcong hills of western New Jersey, offering small, warm one-week sessions with pond time, ropes elements, and openhearted faith formation.

name: Crossings at Cedarmore
description: Kentucky Baptists' big camp near Bagdad, run by Crossings Ministries with high-production worship, rec tournaments, and lake time for church groups across the state. Loud, joyful, and gospel-centered.

name: Crossings at Jonathan Creek
description: The western Kentucky sister camp to Cedarmore, set on Kentucky Lake with a huge waterfront, tournament-style rec, and worship nights that pack the tabernacle. A staple for Baptist youth groups from three states.

name: Imago Dei Village
description: An ELCA Crossways camp on a lake in east-central Wisconsin, offering inclusive, joy-filled Lutheran outdoor ministry with swimming, canoeing, and singing.

name: Red Willow Bible Camp
description: A Lutheran Bible camp on Red Willow Lake in eastern North Dakota, a summer touchstone for prairie church families with swimming, campfires, and singing.

name: Crystal Springs Baptist Camp
description: A Baptist camp on a spring-fed prairie lake along I-94 in central North Dakota, where generations of church kids have spent revival-style weeks swimming and singing.

name: Culver Summer Schools & Camps
description: The century-old summer program at Culver Academies on Lake Maxinkuckee, famous for its Black Horse Troop cavalry tradition, naval program, and leadership-heavy structure. It feels like a cross between camp, boarding school, and a very polite military academy.

name: Curtis S. Read Scout Reservation
description: The Greater New York Councils' Adirondack scout reservation on Summit Lake near Brant Lake, hosting troops from the five boroughs for classic merit-badge summers in the mountains.

name: CYO Camp Rancho Framasa Adventure
description: The teen adventure track at the Archdiocese of Indianapolis' CYO camp in Brown County, adding climbing and wilderness challenge to its inclusive Catholic program.

name: CYO Camp Howard
description: The Catholic Youth Organization camp near Mount Hood serving Portland-area families for generations, with forest trails, sports, and Mass woven into a classic camp week.

name: CYO Camp
description: Catholic Charities' big-hearted camp in the redwoods of western Sonoma County, serving a wide cross-section of Bay Area kids with a pool, creek, and generations of counselors who grew up there. Faith is present but gentle.

name: D-bar-A Scout Ranch
description: Michigan's Scout ranch in the horse country north of Detroit, where western riding and ranch life share the schedule with lakes, ranges, and traditional camp program.

name: Darrow Wilderness Trip Camp
description: A canoe-tripping camp based in the legendary paddling village of Grand Lake Stream, running small-group wilderness expeditions on the lakes and rivers of far Downeast Maine and beyond.

name: Edlavitch DCJCC Camp
description: The Edlavitch DCJCC's downtown day camp, offering swim, sports, and arts with a warm Jewish-cultural community for District families. Convenient, inclusive, and enrichment-rich.

name: DEC Camp Colby
description: New York State's DEC environmental education camp on Lake Colby, where teens spend a $350 week learning field ecology, fishing, and outdoor skills from conservation educators.

name: DEC Camp DeBruce
description: The state DEC's Catskills conservation camp near Livingston Manor, where teens learn fly fishing on legendary trout water, wildlife ecology, and outdoor skills for a token fee.

name: DEC Camp Pack Forest
description: A DEC conservation camp in a working demonstration forest near Warrensburg, giving teens a hands-on week of forestry, wildlife study, and camping basics led by environmental educators.

name: DEC Camp Rushford
description: The state DEC's western New York conservation camp near Rushford Lake, where teens spend an inexpensive week on fishing skills, wildlife ecology, and orienteering with environmental educators.

name: Decoma Day Camp
description: A family-owned Northbrook day camp running since the late 1950s, with pools, sports, and long-tenured staff that give it a personal feel among the North Shore giants.

name: deCordova Summer Art Camp
description: An art-focused day camp on the wooded sculpture-park grounds of the former deCordova museum in Lincoln, where kids make art outdoors amid the sculptures. A creative, nature-rich week for artistic kids near Boston.

name: Deer Crossing Camp
description: A wilderness camp on the far shore of Loon Lake accessible only by boat, teaching serious skills - sailing, kayaking, climbing, wilderness leadership - in tiny cohorts at the edge of Desolation Wilderness. For independent kids ready for real backcountry.

name: Deer Hill Expeditions
description: An expedition program based in the Four Corners that combines river trips and backpacking with meaningful service projects alongside Native communities. For teens ready to go deeper than a normal camp.

name: Deer Run Camps
description: A Christian camp and retreat center south of Nashville offering day and overnight camps with zip lines, fishing ponds, and clear gospel focus for Middle Tennessee families.

name: Deer Run Camps & Retreats
description: A family-founded Christian camp in the hills south of Franklin offering day and overnight weeks with paintball, ziplines, a lake, and intentional mentoring. Popular with Williamson County families.

name: Deerfoot Lodge
description: A rugged Christian wilderness camp for boys on Whitaker Lake near Speculator, built around canoe trips, axemanship, and cabin brotherhood, with daily chapel woven into the woodcraft.

name: Des Moines Y Camp
description: The century-old YMCA camp in the Des Moines River valley near Boone, with river bluffs, horses, and the four core values drilled into generations of central Iowa kids.

name: Des Moines YMCA Camp
description: Known simply as 'Y Camp' to generations of central Iowans, this Des Moines Y camp above the Des Moines River valley near Boone runs horses, paintball, and river adventures.

name: Destination Science
description: A California-based science day camp at church and school sites across LA, Orange County, and the Bay Area, where younger kids build take-home robots, rockets, and roller coasters in themed weeks. Cheerful, structured, and aimed squarely at the elementary set.

name: Digital Media Academy
description: A tech-camp brand born at Stanford that leans creative — filmmaking, 3D design, AI, and music production alongside straight coding. Courses feel like a taste of a college seminar compressed into a week.

name: Doe River Gorge
description: A Christian adventure camp built in a dramatic East Tennessee river gorge along an old narrow-gauge railroad, where campers ride a vintage train into camp and spend the week climbing, rafting, and ziplining. The setting is unforgettable.

name: Double H Ranch
description: Co-founded by Paul Newman and Charles Wood, this Adirondack SeriousFun camp gives children with serious illnesses a completely free week of riding, fishing, and big-hearted fun with full medical support on site.

name: Douglas Ranch Camps
description: A hundred-year-old riding camp in sunny Carmel Valley where every camper rides daily and traditions like gymkhana carry across generations. Genteel, horse-centered, and steeped in old-California charm.

name: Drumlin Farm Camp
description: Mass Audubon's camp on a working wildlife sanctuary and farm in Lincoln, where kids collect eggs, track turtles, and get properly muddy. The gold standard for nature-loving kids near Boston.

name: Dry Creek Baptist Camp
description: A long-running Baptist camp in the piney woods of southwest Louisiana, hosting large church youth weeks with a tabernacle, pool, and revival-style worship.

name: Dry Gulch U.S.A.
description: A Wild West-themed church camp northeast of Tulsa, complete with an old-west town, train rides, and big-production evening services run by Rhema Bible Church. Kids remember the theatrics as much as the waterslides.

name: Summer Enrichment at Duke
description: Residential academic summer courses for academically motivated middle and high schoolers on Duke's campus, offering deep dives into subjects from genetics to constitutional law. A taste of college academics and dorm life for gifted kids.

name: Durland Scout Reservation
description: The Westchester-Putnam Council's Scout reservation on Clear Lake in Putnam Valley, a large, long-running summer camp offering the full range of merit badges, aquatics, and shooting sports.

name: Eagle Eyrie
description: A Virginia Baptist conference center on a mountaintop above Lynchburg, hosting church youth camps with worship in a mountaintop tabernacle, rec fields, and pool.

name: Eagle Island Camp
description: A historic Great Camp on its own island in Upper Saranac Lake, saved by alumnae and reopened as an affordable girls' camp — campers arrive by boat and live among century-old Adirondack architecture.

name: Eagle Lake Camps
description: The Navigators' camp above Colorado Springs, with a lake, a huge ropes complex, and discipleship woven through a classic overnight program. Also runs well-regarded day camps on the Front Range.

name: Eagle's Nest Camp
description: A progressive coed camp near Brevard blending wilderness trips, organic gardening, and a strong arts program with an earthy, inclusive community. Also runs the Hante Adventures teen expeditions.

name: East Bay Camp
description: A historic church camp on the shore of Lake Bloomington that has hosted central Illinois youth weeks for generations, with a big waterfront and interdenominational spirit.

name: Eastern 4-H Environmental Education Center
description: A 4-H center on the Albemarle Sound in far eastern North Carolina where campers kayak blackwater swamps, study estuary wildlife, and swim off the sound-front beach.

name: Eastern Nebraska 4-H Center
description: The 4-H day and resident camp in the Platte River hills near Gretna, an easy nature fix for Omaha and Lincoln families with ropes courses, fishing, and STEM weeks.

name: Eastern U.S. Music Camp
description: A residential music camp held on Colgate University's campus in Hamilton, offering ensembles, theory, jazz, and voice with college facilities and nightly recreation on the quad.

name: Easterseals Camp Harmon
description: Easterseals' camp among the Santa Cruz Mountains redwoods for children and adults with physical and developmental disabilities, with fully accessible trails, pool, and cabins and one-to-one staffing where needed.

name: Echo Hill Camp
description: A family-owned camp on the Chesapeake Bay in Kent County, known for its choice-driven schedule, tidal-marsh nature program, and a gentle, non-competitive culture right on the water.

name: Echo Hill Ranch
description: A quirky, beloved family ranch camp near Medina founded by the Friedman family in 1953 — yes, that Friedman family — where creek swims, nature hikes, and gentle eccentricity rule. Small, informal, and full of character.

name: Echo Ranch Bible Camp
description: A remote Bible camp at the end of the road north of Juneau on Berners Bay tide flats, with horses on the beach, kayaks, and Southeast Alaska rainforest all around. Getting there is half the story.

name: Eden Village Camp
description: A Jewish organic-farm camp an hour from NYC where campers herd goats, ferment pickles, practice wilderness skills, and celebrate an earthy, inclusive Judaism that draws crunchy families from every denomination.

name: Eden Village West
description: A Jewish organic-farm camp on the Russian River where kids harvest vegetables, tend goats, and practice earth-based Judaism. The West Coast sibling of New York's Eden Village, with the same gentle, kindness-first culture.

name: El Lago del Bosque (Concordia Language Villages)
description: The Spanish immersion village at Concordia Language Villages, alive with salsa dancing, fútbol, and mercado days — one of the network's largest and liveliest villages.

name: Elks Camp Barrett
description: A camp run by the Elks in the woods near Annapolis, offering affordable, scholarship-supported weeks of fishing, archery, swimming, and outdoor skills for Anne Arundel-area kids.

name: Elmwood Day Camp
description: A warm, mid-sized Westchester camp that leans into community and social-emotional growth rather than flash, with heated pools and electives from cooking to photography. A favorite for kids who need a gentler landing.

name: Emagination STEM Camps
description: One of the longest-running computer camps in the country, hosted on college campuses with its flagship at Fitchburg State. Half the day is coding and game design, the other half is swimming and traditional camp fun — a good fit for kids who want both.

name: Emma Kaufmann Camp
description: Pittsburgh's JCC overnight camp on Cheat Lake in West Virginia, over a century old, with lake swims, mountain views, and a famously tight Pittsburgh Jewish community feel.

name: Wilton YMCA Camp Mohackemo
description: The Wilton Y's traditional outdoor day camp, with swim, nature, and camp skills on wooded Fairfield County grounds at a community price. Financial aid and a friendly, unpretentious culture.

name: Endless Summer Surf Camp
description: A long-running overnight surf camp at San Onofre State Beach founded by a former pro, with campers sleeping steps from the sand and surfing multiple sessions a day. A friendly, safety-first introduction to real surf culture.

name: Epworth Forest Camp
description: A century-old United Methodist camp on Lake Webster run by Impact 2818, where generations of Indiana youth groups have mixed lake days with high-energy worship.

name: YMCA of the Rockies Day Camp
description: The day-camp program at YMCA of the Rockies' Estes Park Center, giving kids hikes into Rocky Mountain National Park, crafts, swimming, and archery on a sprawling mountain campus.

name: Esther K. Newman Camp
description: The Omaha Jewish community's overnight camp near the Platte River, giving Great Plains Jewish kids — many from small communities — a warm cultural home each summer.

name: Evert Tennis Academy Summer Camp
description: The Boca Raton academy founded by the Evert family, offering intensive summer tennis with the structured, fundamentals-first coaching the Evert name implies. Boarding campers live on site and train twice a day.

name: Everwood Day Camp
description: A choice-based day camp in the Sharon woods where kids pick their own daily adventures, from woodworking to zip lines. Popular south-of-Boston option with unusually flexible scheduling.

name: EXPLO
description: A big, buzzing academic-enrichment program on a college campus where kids sample everything from forensics to improv without grades or pressure. Global student body and a curiosity-first culture.

name: Fair Haven Camps
description: A small, rustic Christian camp in the hills of Waldo County running affordable week-long sessions of swimming, sports, and campfire worship for boys and girls.

name: Camp Playland
description: A long-running traditional day camp on wooded acreage in Stamford, with heated pools, sports, and arts serving lower Fairfield County. Family-owned since the 1940s with a warm, homey culture.

name: Fairview Lake YMCA Camp
description: A big, historic YMCA camp on a mile-long private lake at the base of the Kittatinny Ridge, with sailing, horses, and a century of traditions at accessible prices. One of New Jersey's classic sleepaway experiences.

name: Falcon Camp
description: One of Ohio's few classic private sleepaway camps, tucked on Leesville Lake with sailing, riding, and a big-choice elective program. Families like that it feels traditional without being rigid.

name: Falls Creek Baptist Conference Center
description: An Oklahoma institution in the Arbuckle Mountains, where thousands of Baptist church youth groups converge weekly for worship in a giant tabernacle plus swimming, hikes, and cabin life.

name: Ferncliff Camp and Conference Center
description: A progressive Presbyterian camp just outside Little Rock known for its eco-focus, solar buildings, and gentle, inclusive take on Christian community.

name: Fernwood Cove
description: Camp Fernwood's four-week sister camp in Harrison, created for girls who want the classic Maine experience in a half-season format. Polished, nurturing, and popular with first-time campers.

name: Fitness by the Sea
description: A beach day camp running on the sand in Santa Monica and Pacific Palisades, mixing surfing, beach sports, and roaming 'adventure' stations with a flexible any-day enrollment model. A fixture of westside beach summers for nearly three decades.

name: Flat Rock River YMCA Camp
description: The Indianapolis YMCA's traditional resident camp on the Flatrock River southeast of the city, a century-old standby for canoe trips, horses, and first overnights.

name: Flip Fest Gymnastics Camp
description: A weeklong gymnastics camp on a Tennessee lake co-founded by Olympian John Macready, mixing top-flight coaching with blob launches, ziplines, and genuine summer-camp silliness. The South's answer to the big Pocono gymnastics camps.

name: Florida Elks Youth Camp
description: The Florida Elks' service-project camp near Umatilla with a huge pool complex, horses, and go-karts, offered free or nearly free to Florida kids sponsored by local lodges. Astonishing value with real facilities.

name: Florida National High Adventure Sea Base
description: Scouting's ocean high-adventure base in the Florida Keys, sending crews out on live-aboard sailboats, scuba certifications, and coral-reef fishing adventures. A dream trip for water-loving older Scouts.

name: Flying Cloud (Farm & Wilderness)
description: The most primitive of the Farm & Wilderness camps: boys live in a forest village without electricity, learning fire-building, wildcrafting, and quiet self-reliance.

name: Flying G Ranch
description: A Girl Scouts of Colorado ranch camp in the Pike National Forest near Deckers, with a strong horse program, backpacking for older girls, and generations of camp tradition.

name: Flying Horse Farms
description: Ohio's SeriousFun camp on a transformed farm north of Columbus, where campers with serious illnesses fish, swim, and fly down the zipline at no charge, with medical staff woven invisibly into camp life.

name: Flying Horseshoe Ranch Camp
description: A small family-run horse ranch camp near Cle Elum on the sunny east side of the Cascades, where kids ride daily, help with barn chores, and sleep in bunkhouses. For kids who want horses first and everything else second.

name: Flying Moose Lodge
description: A tiny Downeast wilderness camp on Craig Pond where the trips ARE the program - boys are out canoeing and hiking Maine's backcountry most of the summer, returning to a rustic lodge with no electricity.

name: Forest Acres Camp for Girls
description: The sister camp to Indian Acres in Fryeburg, offering girls a classic Maine summer with dance, riding, arts, and lake time. Family-owned with an emphasis on confidence over competition.

name: Forest Home
description: A historic Southern California Christian conference center in the San Bernardino Mountains, running age-graded summer camps with creek hikes, zip lines, and evangelical teaching.

name: Forest Lake Camp
description: A traditional Adirondack camp on 800 wooded acres with separate boys' and girls' campuses that come together for evening events. Strong all-around program without a hyper-competitive edge.

name: Fort Wilderness
description: A frontier-themed Christian camp in the woods north of Rhinelander with log buildings, horses, and a strong Bible focus — a longtime favorite of Midwest evangelical families.

name: Fortune Lake Lutheran Camp
description: The Upper Peninsula's beloved Lutheran camp near Crystal Falls, where UP kids get week-long Bible camp with lake swims, sauna nights, and sincere northwoods hospitality.

name: 4-H Camp Ohio
description: A classic 4-H camp in the Licking County hills where county clubs converge each week for canoeing, shooting sports, line dances, and the kind of traditions 4-H does best.

name: Four Winds * Westward Ho
description: A classic 1920s camp on Orcas Island where campers wear traditional uniforms, sail wooden boats, and ride horses along saltwater meadows. Unhurried, screen-free, and steeped in nearly a century of ritual.

name: Camp Hidden Valley
description: The Fresh Air Fund's camp for younger children at Sharpe Reservation in Fishkill, a free first-sleepaway experience that welcomes NYC kids of all abilities into an inclusive, gentle woodland community.

name: Friendly Pines Camp
description: Arizona's grande dame of sleepaway camps, family-run in the Bradshaw Mountains above Prescott since 1941, with horses, a lake, and a famously warm culture built for first-time campers.

name: Friends Academy Summer Camps
description: Summer camps on the Quaker school's gracious Locust Valley campus, mixing traditional day camp with weekly sports and arts specialties. Draws Gold Coast families who like the small-group, values-forward feel.

name: Friends' Central Summer Programs
description: Summer camps on Friends' Central's leafy Main Line campus in Wynnewood, spanning traditional day camp, sports, arts, and academics under the Quaker school's teachers. A polished, values-forward option.

name: Frontier Camp
description: A Christian camp in the East Texas pines near Davy Crockett National Forest, mixing lake activities, horses, and riflery with clear evangelical teaching. Long-tenured staff give it a steady, trustworthy feel.

name: Frontier Ranch (Young Life)
description: Young Life's flagship-caliber camp on the slopes of Mount Princeton, where high schoolers come with their hometown YL groups for a fast-paced week of ridge hikes, pool antics, and nightly gospel talks.

name: Frost Valley YMCA
description: A sprawling YMCA camp on thousands of Catskill acres in Claryville, with everything from classic villages to a farm camp and adventure trips, plus dialysis-supported sessions and strong scholarship access.

name: Future Stars Summer Camps
description: A weekly-enrollment specialty camp operator based at SUNY Purchase with campuses across Westchester and Long Island, offering focused programs in tennis, soccer, basketball, STEAM, dance, and more. Great for kids who want to go deep on one interest at a time.

name: Gander Brook Christian Camp
description: A Church of Christ camp in Raymond running affordable week-long sessions of swimming, sports, singing, and Bible study in the Sebago Lakes region.

name: Gardner Dam Scout Camp
description: A Bay-Lakes Council camp on the wild Wolf River in northern Wisconsin, where whitewater rafting and trout fishing set it apart from the usual flat-lake camp.

name: GGGA Summer Golf Camp
description: Summer camps at Gary Gilchrist's Florida golf academy, where the coach behind several major champions applies his holistic method — technique, fitness, mental game — to junior golfers on a resort course. Small and serious.

name: Genesee Valley Outdoor Learning Center
description: An adventure-focused outdoor education center in northern Baltimore County with a big challenge course, climbing tower, and mountain biking, offering both day and overnight programs on a scenic farm campus.

name: Geneva Glen Camp
description: A century-old camp in the foothills just outside Denver, famous for immersive themed sessions like Knighthood and American Heritage that wrap values and pageantry into camp life. Multi-generational families are the norm here.

name: Ghost Ranch Youth Camps
description: The famed Ghost Ranch education and retreat center in the red-rock country near Abiquiu runs youth camps blending hiking, paleontology, arts, and desert exploration on the landscape Georgia O'Keeffe made famous.

name: Camp Gilmont
description: A Presbyterian camp in the East Texas piney woods near Gilmer, offering a pond, low ropes, and gentle Reformed faith formation for church families.

name: Camp Amahami
description: A century-old Girl Scout camp on a lake in the Southern Tier hills near Deposit, where girls build outdoor skills, ride horses, and paddle canoes in a proudly rustic, empowering setting.

name: Camp Coleman (Girl Scouts)
description: Girl Scouts of North-Central Alabama's historic camp near Trussville, with a lake, pool, and badge-driven adventure weeks just outside Birmingham. Practical, affordable girl-power summers.

name: Girls Rock Math
description: Seattle day camps that wrap math in art, games, and themed projects — think fractals through weaving and geometry through architecture — to build girls' math confidence. Runs at several school sites around the city.

name: Glacier Camp
description: An Evangelical Covenant camp above Flathead Lake near Lakeside, running high-energy youth weeks with waterfront toys, a giant swing, and mountain hikes toward Glacier National Park.

name: Glorieta Adventure Camps
description: A large Christian adventure camp on a historic conference-center campus near Santa Fe, with mountain biking, ziplines, ropes courses, and lake activities alongside high-energy worship. Draws families from across Texas and the Southwest.

name: Glorieta Camps
description: A sprawling former Baptist conference ground near Santa Fe reborn as an adventure camp, with mountain biking, zip lines, and skate features at 7,500 feet.

name: Golden Slipper Camp
description: A nonprofit Poconos camp founded by the Golden Slipper charity to make sleepaway summers affordable for Philadelphia-area kids, with generous scholarships and a classic pool-sports-campfire program.

name: Goldman Union Camp Institute (GUCI)
description: The Reform movement's storied Midwest camp outside Indianapolis, famous for song sessions, Hebrew woven into daily life, and alumni networks that span the region's synagogues.

name: Gorham Scout Ranch
description: Northern New Mexico's council camp in the foothills of the Sangre de Cristos near Chimayo — horses, high-desert hikes, and a taste of Philmont country for younger Scouts.

name: Goshen Scout Reservation
description: The National Capital Area Council's 4,000-acre reservation around Lake Merriweather in the Alleghenies, running several distinct camps at once for Washington-area Scouts — a small Scouting city each July.

name: Green Lake Conference Center Kids Camp
description: The American Baptist conference center on Wisconsin's deep, clear Green Lake runs summer youth camps with sailing, hikes, and Bible study on its sprawling grounds.

name: Green Mountain Camp for Girls
description: A little hilltop camp near Brattleboro that has kept sleepaway camp affordable for southern Vermont girls for over a century — gardens, crafts, and old-fashioned camp songs.

name: Green River Preserve
description: A nature-immersion camp on a 3,400-acre private preserve where mentored hikes, fly fishing, and creek stomping replace color wars. Draws curious, outdoorsy kids — the camp calls them 'bright, creative' and means it.

name: URJ Greene Family Camp
description: The Reform movement's camp for Texas and the Southwest, sitting between Waco and Temple with song sessions, Israeli culture, and joyful Shabbat traditions at its heart. Generations of Texas Reform Jewish families call it their summer home.

name: Greenwich Recreation Camp Simmons
description: Greenwich's town recreation day camp on a wooded property with a pond, swimming, and outdoor activities at a resident-friendly price. An accessible, outdoorsy hometown option in a pricey town.

name: Greenwich Family YMCA Day Camp
description: The Greenwich Y's day camp, offering swim, sports, and outdoor fun at a community price with financial aid and inclusion support. An accessible, unpretentious option in a pricey town.

name: Greenwood Music Camp
description: A tiny, old-soul chamber music camp in the Berkshire hills where string players and a few winds play quartets, hike, and do barn dances — no ranking, no competition, fierce alumni loyalty. Admission is competitive because it's so small.

name: Greenwoods Camp for Boys
description: The brother camp to Lake of the Woods on a shared private lake in Decatur, where boys bounce between waterfront, go-karts, and sports in a high-energy but warm program.

name: Gretna Glen Camp
description: A United Methodist camp in the woods of Mount Gretna offering classic one-week sessions - creek play, campfires, low ropes - with warm faith mentoring. A gentle, affordable central-PA favorite.

name: Griffith Park Boys' Camp
description: LA's own city-run boys' sleepaway camp tucked into Griffith Park since the 1920s, with a pool, archery, and hiking trails minutes from Hollywood at recreation-department prices. A rare public sleepaway option in a major city.

name: Griswold Scout Reservation (Hidden Valley)
description: The Daniel Webster Council's reservation in New Hampshire's Lakes Region, where Hidden Valley's twin ponds, horse program, and mountain-view hikes anchor Granite State Scouting.

name: Grotonwood Camp
description: An American Baptist camp on Lost Lake in Groton with classic waterfront weeks plus dedicated sessions for campers with special needs. Faith-forward but welcoming to all comers.

name: Great Smoky Mountains Institute at Tremont
description: A residential field-science camp inside Great Smoky Mountains National Park where kids backpack, catch salamanders, and learn from real naturalists. For the kid who'd rather flip rocks in a stream than play capture the flag.

name: Gwynn Valley Camp
description: A working-farm camp where young campers milk cows, gather eggs, and eat food grown on site. Famously gentle and noncompetitive — one of the region's best first sleepaway experiences for younger kids.

name: Gymtime Summer Camp
description: An Upper East Side day camp for the youngest set, built around a big gymnastics gym plus art, cooking, and science by the week. A gentle, indoor-outdoor first camp for Manhattan toddlers and early graders.

name: H. Roe Bartle Scout Reservation
description: Kansas City's massive camp on the Lake of the Ozarks' upper reaches, home of the century-old Tribe of Mic-O-Say honor tradition that binds generations of Missouri Scouts together.

name: Hale Scout Reservation
description: The Indian Nations Council's Scout camp deep in the Ouachita Mountains near Talihina, with a mountain lake for sailing weeks of merit badges and high-adventure treks. Remote, wooded, and satisfyingly rugged.

name: Hale Day Camp
description: Day camp on Hale's 1,100-acre reservation of ponds and trails just outside Boston, with pond beaches, mountain biking, and real woods minutes from the city. Huge but well-organized, with strong financial aid.

name: Hampshire College Summer Studies in Mathematics
description: A legendary six-week immersion in pure mathematics for talented high schoolers, focused on the beauty of proofs rather than competition or acceleration. Small, intense, and famously formative for future mathematicians.

name: Hampton Country Day Camp
description: The Hamptons' premier private day camp, serving East End summer families with heated pools, mini golf, and resort-level grounds in East Hampton. Peak-season weeks book out with the summer-share crowd.

name: Happiness Is Camping
description: A free residential camp in northwest New Jersey for children with cancer and their siblings, with onsite medical care that lets kids mid-treatment swim, ride, and roast marshmallows like everyone else.

name: Happy Hollow Camp
description: A Brown County camp founded to serve Indianapolis kids of limited means, still keeping camp affordable across 700 hilly acres with a lake, horses, and old-growth woods.

name: Hartland Christian Camp
description: A Sierra foothills camp near Kings Canyon that has served Central Valley churches for generations with paintball, pools, and straightforward Bible teaching.

name: Hawaii Nature Center Summer Camp
description: Environmental education day camps based in Makiki Valley on Oahu (with a Maui branch), where kids hike rainforest trails, hunt stream critters, and learn Hawaiian natural history. Muddy boots and native forest knowledge guaranteed.

name: Hawk Mountain Scout Reservation
description: A ridge-country reservation near the famous raptor sanctuary of the same name, where Appalachian Trail country and hawk flights shape the nature program.

name: Heart O' the Hills Camp
description: A smaller, warmer girls' camp on the North Fork of the Guadalupe between Stewart and Waldemar, known for personal attention, strong riding, and a family feel. Its intimate size means the directors actually know every camper.

name: Heartland Hockey Camp
description: An Olympian-founded hockey camp in the Minnesota lake country with its own year-round arena, where campers skate twice daily and then hit the lake between ice sessions. A pure northern-hockey summer.

name: Henderson Scout Reservation
description: The Revolutionary Trails Council's Scout reservation on a lake in the southwestern Adirondacks near Cold Brook, a longtime merit-badge and outdoor-skills base for central New York troops.

name: Heritage Reservation
description: The Laurel Highlands Council's flagship Scout reservation on Lake Courage near Ohiopyle, with sailing, climbing, and multiple sub-camps that make it one of the premier Scouting summer destinations in the region.

name: Heritage Scout Reservation
description: Pittsburgh's mountain reservation in the Laurel Highlands near Ohiopyle, pairing a large lake with whitewater rafting trips on the Youghiogheny.

name: Herzl Camp
description: The beloved Zionist camp of the Upper Midwest, on Devil's Lake in Webster, Wisconsin, where generations of Twin Cities and Omaha kids — including a young Bob Dylan — have sung, danced, and grown up together.

name: Hidden Acres Christian Center
description: A large evangelical camp along the Des Moines River valley with horses, go-karts, and lake fun, serving church groups from across central Iowa.

name: Hidden Valley 4-H Camp
description: A 4-H camp in the wooded hills outside Watkins Glen where farm kids and town kids alike spend affordable weeks doing archery, pond study, crafts, and campfire cooking.

name: Hidden Valley Camp
description: An artsy, animal-loving coed camp in the hills of Waldo County where kids can care for llamas, blow glass, ride horses, and swim - all with minimal competition and maximum individuality.

name: Hidden Valley Camp
description: A private, family-run camp in the Cascade foothills near Granite Falls, with a private lake, big lawn games, and a warm all-camp culture. A favorite first sleepaway camp for Seattle-area kids since the 1940s.

name: Hidden Valley Scout Reservation (Griswold)
description: New Hampshire Scouting's flagship summer camp at Griswold Scout Reservation, with merit-badge programs, a big waterfront, and high-adventure options in the Belknap hills.

name: Hidden Villa Summer Camp
description: Day and wilderness camps on a historic organic farm in Los Altos Hills, founded by the Duveneck family who ran one of the nation's first intentionally multiracial summer camps in 1945. Farm animals, redwood hikes, and social-justice roots that still shape the culture.

name: High Cascade Snowboard Camp
description: The legendary summer snowboard camp on Mt. Hood's Palmer snowfield, where kids ride a private park with pro coaches in the morning and skate, swim, and make videos all afternoon. Most of snowboarding's biggest names have passed through.

name: High Meadows Camp
description: A woodsy, old-fashioned day camp on the High Meadows School's 40 rolling acres in Roswell, with horses, farm animals, creek stomping, and barely a screen in sight. Progressive, choice-driven, and much loved.

name: Highland Lakes Camp and Conference Center
description: A Baptist camp on Lake Travis near Spicewood hosting church youth groups and kids' camps all summer, with lake recreation, rec fields, and nightly worship. Most campers come with their church, which builds instant community.

name: Highlands Presbyterian Camp
description: A welcoming Presbyterian camp near the Indian Peaks with week-long sessions of hiking, crafts, and campfire worship, known for an intentionally inclusive, low-pressure culture.

name: Hiram House Camp
description: One of the oldest camps in the country, founded as a settlement-house camp in 1896, now a woodsy east-side Cleveland day camp with horses, ravines, and mud in the best way.

name: Hofstra Summer Camps
description: University-run day camps on Hofstra's Hempstead campus, spanning traditional rec, dozens of sports, and academic and arts specialties with college facilities like pools and turf fields. Weekly enrollment keeps it flexible.

name: The Hole in the Wall Gang Camp
description: Paul Newman's camp for children with serious illnesses, an entirely free Wild West-themed village in the Connecticut woods with hospital-grade medical support behind the scenes. Pure, carefully engineered joy.

name: Holiday Lake 4-H Educational Center
description: A Virginia 4-H center inside the Appomattox-Buckingham State Forest, where county 4-H weeks fill the summer with canoeing, riflery, and forestry education around a quiet lake.

name: HoneyRock Camp
description: Wheaton College's Northwoods center in Three Lakes, where a serious Christian formation mission meets a real wilderness program — horsemanship, tripping, and leadership tracks for teens.

name: HoneyRock
description: Wheaton College's Northwoods camp in Wisconsin, blending classic lake camp with serious leadership development and wilderness trips run by thoughtfully trained college staff.

name: Hood Scout Reservation
description: Mississippi's principal Scout camp south of Jackson, with a sizable lake, shooting sports, and the unhurried feel of deep-South summer camping.

name: Hoop Group Elite Basketball Camps
description: One of the oldest basketball camp operations in America, running skills and exposure camps from the Jersey Shore with overnight sessions on college campuses in the Poconos and beyond. Serious hoopers go for reps and to get seen.

name: Horace A. Moses Scout Reservation
description: Western Massachusetts' Scout camp in the Berkshire foothills above Russell, a traditional lake-and-woods week for Pioneer Valley troops.

name: Horseshoe Scout Reservation
description: Home of Camp Horseshoe on a horseshoe bend of the Octoraro Creek at the Mason-Dixon line, where Chester County troops have camped for nearly a century.

name: Hosmer Point Camp
description: A newer camp on Great Hosmer Pond beside the Craftsbury Outdoor Center, mixing sculling, swimming, and farm-to-table meals with Northeast Kingdom calm.

name: Hume Lake Christian Camps
description: A massive, iconic evangelical camp beside a lake in the Sequoia high country, hosting thousands of church-group kids weekly with slick programming, a famous snack shop, and big-tent worship.

name: Hummingbird Music Camp
description: A family-run institution in the Jemez Mountains where New Mexico kids have rehearsed band and orchestra music between river swims and mountain hikes for generations. Homey, affordable, and full of heart.

name: Hurricane Island Outward Bound School
description: Outward Bound's legendary Maine sea program, sending teen crews out on open pulling boats to sail and row the island-studded coast - a rite of passage, not a rest cure.

name: Camp Id-Ra-Ha-Je South
description: The southern campus of Camp Id-Ra-Ha-Je in the mountains near Cotopaxi, offering the same budget-friendly Bible-camp formula of games, waterfront, and camp meetings for youth in southern Colorado.

name: iD Tech Camps
description: The biggest name in tech camps, running weeklong coding, game-dev, and robotics programs on university campuses nationwide from its Silicon Valley home base. Small groups of about eight kids per instructor keep things hands-on.

name: Idyllwild Arts Summer Program
description: The summer arm of the famed Idyllwild Arts Academy, offering intensive workshops in everything from film scoring to Native American arts on a pine-forest campus a mile above Palm Springs. Serious instruction with a mellow mountain-town feel.

name: Idyllwild Pines Camp
description: A century-old Christian camp in the heart of Idyllwild's cedar forest, hosting church summer camps beneath Tahquitz Rock. Historic, affordable, and pleasantly old-fashioned.

name: Illinois 4-H Memorial Camp
description: The state 4-H camp beside Allerton Park in central Illinois, built as a WWII memorial, where farm kids and city kids alike do watercraft, crafts, and classic 4-H traditions.

name: Imagination Stage Summer Camp
description: A leading theater-arts day camp in Bethesda, where kids act, sing, and stage musicals under professional teaching artists, culminating in real performances. A DC-metro standout for stage-struck kids.

name: IMG Academy Camps
description: The famous Bradenton sports-training campus (born as Bollettieri's tennis academy) running intensive boarding camps in tennis, golf, soccer, football, and more, with pro-level facilities and coaching. For serious athletes chasing the next level.

name: Incarnation Camp
description: Arguably the oldest coed camp in America, spread across 700 acres and a mile-long private lake near the Connecticut shoreline. Episcopal roots, a deliberately diverse camper community, and gloriously unfancy traditions.

name: Indian Acres Camp for Boys
description: A traditional boys' camp in Fryeburg near the Saco River, paired with Forest Acres for girls across the way. Broad sports program, flexible sessions, and a proud family-run history since the 1920s.

name: Indian Brook (Farm & Wilderness)
description: Farm & Wilderness's camp for girls and gender-expansive youth, where empowerment looks like carrying firewood, leading hikes, and singing in the garden. Quaker-rooted and fiercely warm.

name: Indian Creek Camp
description: The Seventh-day Adventist camp on Center Hill Lake between Nashville and Cookeville, with waterskiing, horses, and a big vegetarian dining hall, plus dedicated weeks for campers with special needs. High energy and very affordable.

name: Indian Head Camp
description: A well-established coed camp on its own lake in the upper Delaware region, mixing serious athletics and arts with big all-camp traditions. Draws a loyal, largely NY/NJ crowd summer after summer.

name: Ingersoll Scout Reservation
description: West-central Illinois's council camp along the Spoon River valley, a steady, affordable week of ranges, lake time, and prairie-edge woods for downstate troops.

name: Ingham Okoboji Lutheran Bible Camp
description: A large, spirited ELCA Bible camp on Lake Okoboji, famous among Iowa and Minnesota Lutherans for its energetic worship, lakefront, and decades of returning families.

name: International Gymnastics Camp
description: The premier dedicated gymnastics camp in the country, a Pocono Mountains campus with world-class training pavilions where Olympians regularly appear as guest clinicians. Gymnasts of all levels train hard and still get lake time.

name: International Music Camp
description: A storied fine-arts camp straddling the US-Canada border at the International Peace Garden, drawing young musicians, dancers, and artists from both countries since 1956.

name: Iowa 4-H Center
description: The statewide 4-H camping center on 1,100 acres above the Des Moines River, where county 4-H'ers do conservation projects, ropes courses, and classic camp weeks.

name: Iowa Young Writers' Studio
description: A selective residential writing program tied to the legendary Iowa Writers' Workshop, giving high schoolers a two-week taste of the famous University of Iowa writing culture through workshops in fiction, poetry, and creative nonfiction.

name: Iroquois Springs
description: A polished coed camp on a private lake in Rock Hill with excellent facilities, a strong arts building, and a warm family-run feel despite its size. Popular with first-time sleepaway families from the metro area.

name: Camp Kinneret NJ
description: A long-running Bergen County day camp on a green Franklin Lakes campus, offering swim instruction, sports, and arts with a warm Jewish-community feel. A North Jersey staple for generations of families.

name: Island Lake Camp
description: A CRISTA Camps property on a private lake near Poulsbo, loaded with skate park, wakeboarding, paintball, and go-karts alongside evangelical chapel programming. Built for kids who want action-sport energy.

name: Ivy League Day Camp
description: A full-service Smithtown day camp and school campus with pools, athletic fields, and a broad elective menu serving central Suffolk families. Known for hot lunch and strong swim instruction.

name: J Robinson Intensive Wrestling Camps
description: The legendarily grueling wrestling camps founded by former Minnesota coach J Robinson, where 14- and 28-day intensives on college campuses test kids with dawn runs, triple sessions, and a finisher's jacket that means something in wrestling rooms nationwide.

name: URJ Henry S. Jacobs Camp
description: The Reform movement's Deep South camp in Utica, Mississippi - the summer heartbeat of small-town Southern Jewry, where kids from Gulf Coast and Delta communities find their people. Song sessions and Color War are famous.

name: Jameson Camp
description: A 100-acre woodland camp surprisingly inside Indianapolis city limits, with a long history of serving kids who need extra support alongside traditional camp weeks.

name: Jamestown 4-H Educational Center
description: A 4-H camp on the James River near historic Jamestown, giving Tidewater kids a week of riverfront swimming, archery, and campfire skits at a public-program price.

name: JCC Camp Grossman
description: JCC Greater Boston's flagship day camp on a wooded Westwood campus, blending pools, sports, and Shabbat sing-alongs with a strong inclusion program. A warm first camp for many Jewish families south of Boston.

name: JCC Camp Hoover
description: The Columbus JCC's day camp on Hoover Reservoir, mixing swimming, boating, and arts with warm Jewish cultural flavor — and open to families of all backgrounds.

name: Leventhal-Sidman JCC Day Camp
description: The JCC's day camp on its Newton campus, offering swim, sports, and specialty tracks with a warm Jewish-cultural community and strong inclusion services. A cornerstone for greater Boston Jewish families.

name: JCC on the Palisades Camp
description: The Kaplen JCC on the Palisades' large day camps in Tenafly, spanning traditional, sports, and specialty tracks with a warm Jewish-cultural community and robust inclusion services. A Bergen County cornerstone.

name: Camp Grossman
description: The JCC Greater Boston's storied day camp on a wooded lake property in Dover, busing kids from across metro Boston for swimming, sports, and Jewish-cultural community since the 1930s. Warm, outdoorsy, and inclusive.

name: Kaiserman JCC Day Camp
description: The Kaiserman JCC's day camp on the Main Line in Wynnewood, offering swim, sports, and arts with a warm Jewish-cultural community and inclusion support. A neighborhood hub for suburban Philadelphia families.

name: Abrams Camps at JCC
description: The Abrams JCC's day camps near Princeton, offering swim, sports, nature, and arts with a big-tent Jewish community and inclusion support. A central Jersey favorite for Mercer County families.

name: JCC MetroWest Day Camps
description: The JCC MetroWest's family of day camps in West Orange, spanning traditional, sports, and specialty tracks with a warm Jewish-cultural community and strong inclusion services. A cornerstone of Essex County Jewish life.

name: JCC Ranch Camp
description: Denver's Jewish community ranch camp on the Black Forest prairie, blending horses, a farm-and-garden program, and joyful Shabbat traditions welcoming to all levels of observance. Kosher-style kitchen and a strong inclusion ethos.

name: JCC Rockland Day Camp
description: The JCC of Rockland's day camp in West Nyack, offering swim, sports, and arts with a big-tent Jewish-cultural feel and strong inclusion programming. A community hub for Rockland families.

name: JCC of Greater New Haven Day Camp
description: The JCC of Greater New Haven's day camp in Woodbridge, offering swim, sports, and arts with a warm Jewish-cultural community and inclusion support on a green campus. A hub for south-central Connecticut families.

name: JCYS Camp Henry Horner
description: A century-old Jewish Council for Youth Services camp on Wooster Lake north of Chicago, blending classic overnight camping with a warm, culturally Jewish, all-welcome community.

name: Jeff Lake Day Camp
description: A historic lakefront day camp on Jefferson Lake in Sussex County, with real swimming, boating, and fishing on the water since the 1920s. A rustic, nature-first summer for northwest Jersey kids.

name: Jenness Park Christian Camp
description: A high-Sierra Christian camp off Highway 108 near Pinecrest, hosting church youth weeks with a pool, lake activities, and tall-pine cabin villages. A longtime favorite of Central Valley and Bay Area congregations.

name: JH Ranch
description: A leadership-focused Christian ranch in far Northern California's Scott Valley, known for parent-teen sessions, ropes challenges, and intentional character coaching.

name: John Knox Center
description: A Presbyterian camp and retreat center on Watts Bar Lake south of Knoxville, running weeklong sessions heavy on sailing, swimming, and small-group faith community. Kind-hearted and unpretentious.

name: John Knox Ranch
description: A rustic Presbyterian camp on the spring-fed Blanco River between Wimberley and Canyon Lake, where kids swim in crystal water, muck around in the creek, and sing at vespers. Proudly low-tech and inclusive.

name: John Newcombe Tennis Ranch Camps
description: The Texas Hill Country ranch of Wimbledon champion John Newcombe, running summer tennis camps with an Aussie team-spirit culture — hard drilling by day, river floats and camp antics after. More fun-loving than the Florida academies.

name: Jr. 76ers Basketball Camp
description: The Philadelphia 76ers' branded basketball day camps at various suburban gyms, teaching fundamentals and running games by the week. A focused pick for hoops-obsessed kids around Philly.

name: Julian Krinsky Camps & Programs
description: A Philadelphia Main Line operation founded by a South African tennis pro that grew from tennis and golf camps into a whole menu of sports, cooking, business, and enrichment programs on college campuses. Day and overnight options for nearly any interest.

name: Jumonville
description: A United Methodist camp atop Chestnut Ridge in the Laurel Highlands, famous for its giant hilltop Great Cross and sweeping views, with sports, adventure, and arts weeks that draw kids from across southwestern PA.

name: June Norcross Webster Scout Reservation
description: A quiet camp in Connecticut's wooded northeast corner, a smaller and friendlier alternative for troops who prefer their summer weeks unhurried.

name: Kaleidoscope Day Camp
description: A traditional Westchester day camp on a wooded hillside in Ossining, with heated pools, a big sports and arts menu, and door-to-door busing across the county. Family-run with a warm, unpretentious feel.

name: Kamp Kiwanis
description: The New York Kiwanis Foundation's camp near Taberg, where sponsored weeks give kids who couldn't otherwise afford camp — including children with special needs — a classic summer of swimming and crafts.

name: Kanakuk K-1
description: Flagship of the huge Kanakuk network in the Ozarks, mixing high-energy sports and lake time with unapologetically evangelical discipleship. Sessions are short, loud, and packed with tradition.

name: Kanakuk K-2
description: Kanakuk's teen camp on Table Rock Lake leans into serious sports competition and leadership training for older campers. Expect big tribal rivalries, ski boats, and nightly worship.

name: Kanakuk K-Kountry
description: Kanakuk's camp for the youngest kids, with costumed characters, pee-wee sports, and a gentle first-sleepaway feel while keeping the network's evangelical core.

name: Kanakuk K-West
description: Kanakuk's all-girls camp near Table Rock Lake blends sports, lake activities, and sisterhood traditions with intentional Christian mentoring from college-age counselors.

name: Kanakuk Kamps
description: The giant of Christian sports camping, running multiple single-gender and coed camps around Branson and Table Rock Lake for nearly a century, with tens of thousands of alumni across the South. Its legacy is complicated by well-publicized past abuse scandals, which families should research alongside its passionate following.

name: Kaufmann Camp
description: A family-run Bergen County day camp on a shady Wyckoff campus, offering swim, sports, and arts with a warm, personal touch. A neighborhood mainstay serving northern NJ towns.

name: Keewaydin Dunmore
description: One of the great canoe-tripping camps of the Northeast, on Lake Dunmore since 1910 — wooden canoes, wanigans, and expeditions that grow from overnight paddles to Canadian rivers.

name: Kennolyn Camps
description: A polished family-owned camp in the redwoods above Santa Cruz, running since 1946 with horses, a climbing wall, and beach outings to Monterey Bay. Classic camp traditions delivered with noticeable attention to detail.

name: Kents Hill Sports Camp
description: A girls' sports camp on the Kents Hill School campus offering serious multi-sport training - turf fields, ice rink, lake waterfront - wrapped in a supportive, empowerment-first culture.

name: Kenwal Day Camp
description: A longtime family-owned day camp in Melville with big pools, ballfields, and a loyal multi-generation following. A slightly more affordable classic among Long Island's private day camps.

name: Kenyon Review Young Writers Workshop
description: An intensive creative-writing workshop for teens at Kenyon College, home of the venerable Kenyon Review, where students write daily in small workshops led by working writers. A rite of passage for literary high schoolers.

name: Keystone Camp
description: One of the oldest girls camps in the Southeast, family-run and intentionally small. Keystone leans into old-fashioned charm — riding, crafts, banquets, and a gentle, nurturing culture for first-time campers.

name: Keystone Science School Camp
description: A science-forward camp at 9,200 feet in Keystone where kids do field ecology, alpine hikes, and river study taught by educator-counselors. Great for curious kids who want their adventure with data.

name: Kickapoo Kamp for Girls
description: A small, proudly old-fashioned girls' camp outside Kerrville that has been run by the same family for generations since 1925. Riding, river swimming, and simple cabin life — no bells and whistles, lots of heart.

name: Kidder Creek
description: A Christian adventure camp in the remote Marble Mountains of far Northern California, famous for real ranch horsemanship, whitewater rafting on the Klamath, and backcountry pack trips. For kids who want genuine wilderness with their faith.

name: Kids Across America
description: A Kanakuk-born camp serving urban youth through sponsored, low-cost weeks of sports, lake time, and gospel-centered mentoring on Table Rock Lake.

name: Kilmer Day Camp
description: A big, well-established Bergen County day camp on wooded Mahwah acreage, with pools, a lake, ziplines, and a huge traditional program. A North Jersey powerhouse with serious camp spirit.

name: Kings Bay Y Summer Camp
description: A neighborhood Brooklyn Y camp serving southern Brooklyn families for generations, with swim, sports, trips, and a strong Russian-speaking Jewish community presence. Affordable and close to home.

name: Kingsley Pines Camp
description: A fully elective coed camp on Panther Pond where campers rebuild their schedule every few days - a good match for kids who want variety and autonomy without a hyper-competitive edge.

name: Kingswood Camp for Boys
description: A sports-forward traditional boys camp on stunning, undeveloped Lake Tarleton, where daily team drafts, lake swims, and mountain views define an unfussy classic-camp summer.

name: Kinhaven Music School
description: A storied summer music school on a Weston hillside where serious young musicians rehearse orchestras and chamber groups, do communal chores, and dance in the barn on Saturdays.

name: Kroka Expeditions
description: A farm-based wilderness expedition school in Marlow where programs leave base camp for real backcountry travel — whitewater, backpacking, and semester-style rigor with a homesteading soul.

name: Kutsher's Sports Academy
description: The storied sports camp born at the Catskills' Kutsher's resort — where Wilt Chamberlain once bellhopped — now on a Berkshires lakefront campus with an elective, multi-sport program. Sporty but not cutthroat.

name: La Foret Camp
description: A United Church of Christ camp in the Black Forest north of Colorado Springs, known for an inclusive, progressive spirit and classic ponderosa-pine camp weeks of hiking, arts, and worship.

name: Lac du Bois (Concordia Language Villages)
description: The French immersion village of Concordia Language Villages, where campers adopt French names, eat croissants they helped bake, and earn high-school credit in four-week sessions on Turtle River Lake.

name: Laity Lodge Youth Camp
description: A Frio River canyon camp run by the H.E. Butt Foundation, prized for its gentle, grace-centered culture, canyon swimming holes, and long waitlists.

name: Lake Ann Camp
description: A Baptist-heritage camp near Traverse City on a clear northern Michigan lake, with wake sports, a strong teen program, and unapologetic Bible teaching.

name: Lake Cumberland 4-H Camp
description: Kentucky 4-H's camp on Lake Cumberland where county extension groups bring kids for tightly scheduled, wildly affordable weeks of canoeing, riflery, and nature classes. Many rural Kentucky kids' first night away from home.

name: Reston Association Camp Fairfax
description: A community day camp in Reston built around nature, swimming, and outdoor exploration at a resident-friendly price. An accessible, outdoorsy option for Fairfax County families.

name: Lake Forest Ranch
description: An east Mississippi lakeside camp with skiing, bass fishing, and earnest evangelical discipleship that draws families from Mississippi and Alabama.

name: Lake Geneva Youth Camp
description: An evangelical camp on the shore of Lake Geneva in Wisconsin, drawing Chicago-area church groups for waterfront fun, tournaments, and gospel-centered programming.

name: Lake of the Woods Camp for Girls
description: A polished girls' camp in southwest Michigan paired with Greenwoods for boys across the lake, offering 50-plus activities from waterski to riding with a large Chicago-area following.

name: Lake Owego Camp
description: The brother camp to Timber Tops, a boys camp on its own Pocono lake with strong sports, big traditions, and a brotherhood-first culture. Part of the long-running Pine Forest family of camps.

name: Lake Retreat Camp
description: A Baptist-rooted camp on a private lake near Ravensdale in southeast King County, running week-long youth camps with swimming, banana-boat rides, and evening chapel. Family-feel staff and reasonable prices.

name: Lakeshore United Methodist Assembly
description: A United Methodist camp on Kentucky Lake in west Tennessee, with lakefront swimming, canoeing, and campfire worship for church families across the region.

name: Laketrails Base Camp
description: A tiny tripping camp on Oak Island in Lake of the Woods — nearly at the Canadian border — where teens crew sailboats and canoes on multi-day journeys across big, wild water.

name: Fairview Lake YMCA Day Camp
description: A YMCA lakefront day camp in Sussex County with canoeing, hiking, and real outdoor skills on a beautiful natural property. Rustic and affordable, a nature-first option for northwest Jersey families.

name: Laurelville Retreat Center
description: A Mennonite-rooted retreat center in the Laurel Highlands whose summer camp weeks emphasize peace, creativity, music, and nature over competition. Gentle and welcoming to kids outside its tradition too.

name: Lawrence Hall of Science Summer Camps
description: UC Berkeley's public science center runs summer camps in its hilltop labs, from animal behavior to engineering design, with the Bay panorama out the window and real museum specimens in hand. Nerd heaven with a view.

name: Lazy F Camp and Retreat Center
description: A United Methodist camp in the Manastash Canyon outside Ellensburg, with horses, canyon hikes, and a warm, inclusive brand of church camp. A quiet favorite for central Washington families.

name: Leoni Meadows
description: The Northern California Seventh-day Adventist conference camp in the El Dorado forest, with a strong horse program, wagon rides, and its own little train. It notably survived the 2021 Caldor Fire that burned around it.

name: Ligonier Camp & Conference Center
description: A century-old Christian camp on the slopes above Ligonier with a serious adventure program - high ropes, climbing, mountain biking - woven into Christ-centered weeks. A Laurel Highlands favorite for Pittsburgh families.

name: Lindley G. Cook 4-H Camp
description: New Jersey's statewide 4-H camp near Stokes State Forest, run through Rutgers Cooperative Extension, mixing lake swims and archery with hands-on nature and animal programs at a public-friendly price.

name: LINX Camps
description: A modern multi-camp operation on the Dana Hall campus in Wellesley, running everything from sports academies to coding and theater weeks. Week-by-week flexibility that busy families love.

name: Lions Camp Merrick
description: A Lions Club camp on the Potomac in southern Maryland providing free summer sessions for children who are deaf, hard of hearing, blind, or have diabetes, with fully adapted waterfront and outdoor programs.

name: Lions Camp Tatiyee
description: Arizona's Lions Clubs camp in the White Mountains, providing completely free, fully adapted summer weeks for campers with disabilities — from fishing to talent shows — with remarkable staff care.

name: Little Grassy United Methodist Camp
description: A United Methodist camp on Little Grassy Lake at the edge of the Shawnee National Forest, serving southern Illinois congregations with lake weeks and quiet faith formation.

name: Little Sioux Scout Ranch
description: A camp in the strange and beautiful Loess Hills of western Iowa, rebuilt with strong community support after the 2008 tornado and known today for its resilient, close-knit spirit.

name: Camp Onomia
description: A Lutheran camp on Shakopee Lake in central Minnesota, offering canoe trips, campfires, and gentle Bible-centered community for church families up north.

name: Lochearn Camp for Girls
description: A century-old girls camp on Lake Fairlee built around 'poise and grace' traditions, clan competitions, and a famously polished waterfront and gymnastics program.

name: Lutheran Outdoor Ministries Center
description: An ELCA camp along the Rock River in northern Illinois with a horse program, farm animals, and gentle, welcoming Lutheran faith formation.

name: Long Beach Recreation Day Camp
description: Long Beach's city recreation day camp, built around the boardwalk and beach with swimming, sports, and ocean days at a resident-friendly price. A quintessential South Shore hometown summer.

name: Long Point Camp
description: The Salvation Army's camp on a Seneca Lake point near Penn Yan, giving heavily subsidized weeks of tubing, sports, and gospel-centered fun to kids across upstate New York.

name: Longacre Leadership Camp
description: A working-farm leadership camp in central Pennsylvania where teens run daily community meetings, care for animals, and take real responsibility for camp life. Small, thoughtful, and big on personal growth.

name: Longshore Sailing School Camp
description: A specialty sailing and windsurfing day camp on Long Island Sound at Westport's Longshore park, teaching real seamanship by the week. A rite of passage for Fairfield County water kids.

name: Look Up Lodge
description: An Upstate South Carolina Christian camp in the Blue Ridge foothills where church groups and individual campers get high-energy rec, mountain views, and clear gospel teaching.

name: Lost Canyon (Young Life)
description: Young Life's Southwest showpiece near Williams, where high schoolers come with their local groups for a resort-quality week — ziplines, pools, mountain biking — wrapped around nightly gospel club talks.

name: Lost Pines Scout Reservation
description: The Capitol Area Council's camp in the strange loblolly pine island near Bastrop, a friendly central-Texas camp that has regrown gracefully since the 2011 fires.

name: Lost Valley Scout Reservation
description: A remote Scout reservation in a high valley of the Cleveland National Forest east of Palomar Mountain, offering merit-badge weeks, ATV and climbing programs, and dark-sky stargazing. Feels genuinely away from everything.

name: Lotus Lake Camp
description: A central Vermont day camp on its own private lake near Barre and Montpelier, where busloads of kids spend summers swimming, boating, and doing classic camp crafts.

name: Louisiana Lions Camp
description: Free residential camp near Leesville run by Louisiana Lions Clubs for children with disabilities, diabetes, and other health challenges, with medical staff and fully adapted activities. A quiet point of pride for the state's Lions.

name: Lourdes Camp
description: The Syracuse Catholic diocese's camp on the crystal-clear southern end of Skaneateles Lake, where central New York kids have swum, sailed, and sung grace before meals for 75 years.

name: Luccock Park Camp
description: A United Methodist camp tucked in Paradise Valley on the flank of the Absaroka Range near Livingston, offering small, unhurried weeks of hiking, crafts, and worship close to Yellowstone.

name: Luther Crest Bible Camp
description: An ELCA Bible camp on Lake Carlos near Alexandria serving western Minnesota congregations with cabin weeks, canoe trips, and campfire worship in the lakes-country sunset.

name: Luther Heights Bible Camp
description: A high-altitude ELCA Lutheran camp in the Sawtooths near Galena Summit, where campers backpack, hike to alpine lakes, and share faith and song in one of Idaho's most striking mountain settings.

name: Lutheran Lakeside Camp
description: An ELCA camp right on Big Spirit Lake in the Iowa Great Lakes, pairing beach afternoons with energetic Lutheran worship and counselor-led faith conversations.

name: Lutherdale
description: An ELCA Bible camp on Lauderdale Lakes in southern Wisconsin, offering pontoon rides, gardens, and gentle Lutheran faith formation with a strong day-camp outreach.

name: Lutheridge Camp and Conference Center
description: A beloved Lutheran camp on a wooded ridge south of Asheville, running week-long sessions of songs, crafts, and faith community — including a well-known music week and inclusive programs.

name: Lutherock
description: The adventure-focused ELCA camp high in the North Carolina mountains near Grandfather Mountain, with climbing, caving, and backpacking wrapped in Lutheran community.

name: Lutheridge
description: An ELCA camp on a wooded mountain just south of Asheville, treasured across the Southeast for its music weeks, warm staff, and generations of Lutheran campers.

name: Luzerne Music Center
description: A serious classical-music camp near Lake George founded by Philadelphia Orchestra players, pairing daily lessons, chamber groups, and faculty concerts with a normal camp afternoon of swimming and sports.

name: Maccabi Sports Camp
