description: A Jewish overnight sports camp on a Peninsula boarding-school campus, offering serious coaching in soccer, basketball, tennis, and more, wrapped in ruach-filled Shabbat weekends. Great for sporty kids who want Jewish community without a traditional camp vibe.

name: Maine Golf & Tennis Academy
description: A specialty sleepaway academy in the Belgrade Lakes with its own golf course and tennis courts, where kids train seriously by day and still get lake swims and camp fun in the evenings.

name: Maine High Adventure
description: A BSA high-adventure base on Grand Lake Matagamon at the edge of Baxter State Park, sending crews of teens on custom canoe treks through Maine's genuinely wild northern waterways.

name: Maine Teen Camp
description: The rare camp built exclusively for teenagers - recording studios, band shows, waterfront, and an elective schedule designed to feel grown-up. Draws an international crowd of 13-17 year olds.

name: Malibu Makos Surf Camp
description: A surf-focused day camp at Zuma and other Malibu breaks where kids get real wave coaching, ocean-awareness training, and plenty of beach downtime between sets. Run by longtime Malibu watermen and women.

name: Manidokan Camp & Retreat Center
description: A United Methodist camp on a bluff above the Potomac near Harpers Ferry, with climbing, caving, whitewater trips, and hiking on the nearby Appalachian Trail woven into faith-centered weeks.

name: Many Point Scout Camp
description: Northern Star Scouting's massive camp on Many Point Lake, hosting thousands of Scouts each summer across themed sub-camps with sailing, fishing, and a famous voyageur history program.

name: Manzano Mountain Retreat
description: A small Christian camp in the wooded Manzano Mountains southeast of Albuquerque, offering affordable, unplugged weeks of hiking, crafts, and campfire faith for central New Mexico families.

name: Breezemont's Maplebrook
description: A traditional day camp on shady grounds in Armonk offering swim, sports, and nature programming for northern Westchester families. Small-group attention and a classic, no-frills summer.

name: Mar-Lu-Ridge
description: A Lutheran camp and retreat center on a ridge above the Middletown Valley with sweeping views, offering week-long sessions of hiking, crafts, swimming, and welcoming faith community.

name: Maranatha Bible Camp
description: A big evangelical camp on a sandhill lake along I-80 near North Platte, serving western Nebraska for generations with lake blobs, go-karts, and revival-style chapel nights.

name: Maritime Aquarium Summer Camp
description: A marine-science day camp at Norwalk's Maritime Aquarium, where kids explore tide pools, animals, and Long Island Sound ecology by the week. Perfect for young naturalists and future marine biologists.

name: Frogbridge Day Camp
description: A large, amenity-rich day camp on 84 acres in Millstone, central NJ, with a private lake, water slides, ziplines, and a full traditional program. High-energy and facilities-forward for Monmouth and Middlesex families.

name: Marmon Valley Farm
description: A Christian horse camp on a working farm in the Logan County hills, where campers ride daily, do barn chores, and square dance in the hay barn on family night.

name: Camp Massanetta
description: Youth camp programs at the historic Presbyterian Massanetta Springs conference grounds in the Shenandoah Valley, with a spring-fed lake and gentle Reformed faith formation.

name: Massawepie Scout Camps
description: A vast Scouting reservation on its own lake-dotted tract near Tupper Lake, famous for merit-badge weeks and true wilderness treks across thousands of Adirondack acres.

name: Mataguay Scout Ranch
description: San Diego Scouting's ranch camp in the golden hills near Julian, running summer merit-badge weeks with shooting sports, a pool, and ranch-country character. A dependable local council camp with deep alumni loyalty.

name: McCallie Sports Camp
description: A boys' overnight sports camp on the McCallie School campus in Chattanooga, where campers rotate through a dozen sports with varsity-level coaching and dorm living. Competitive but character-focused, in the McCallie tradition.

name: Meadow Mountain Ranch
description: A Girl Scout ranch camp below Longs Peak near Allenspark, offering horses, backpacking, and rock climbing in a stunning high-country setting on the edge of Rocky Mountain National Park.

name: Meadowbrook Day Camp
description: A country day camp on rolling acreage in Morris County's Long Valley, with pools, horseback riding, and a farm-and-nature bent. A rural alternative to the busier suburban camps.

name: Meadowmount School of Music
description: A legendary summer school for classical string and piano students in the Adirondacks near Westport, where serious young musicians practice hours a day under master faculty. Alumni include Itzhak Perlman and Yo-Yo Ma.

name: Medicine Mountain Scout Ranch
description: A Black Hills Scout ranch outside Custer with granite spires, ponderosa forest, and side trips to Crazy Horse and Mount Rushmore woven into the week. Destination camping for plains-state troops.

name: Melita Island Scout Camp
description: The Montana Council's private 64-acre island on Flathead Lake — Scouts boat in and spend the week sailing, kayaking, and swimming in some of the clearest big-lake water in America.

name: Michigan Tech Summer Youth Programs
description: Week-long residential explorations at Michigan Tech in the Keweenaw — robotics, engineering, wilderness medicine — where teens live in dorms and get real lab time, with Lake Superior out the window.

name: Mid-Island Y JCC Camp Kadima
description: The Mid-Island Y JCC's Camp Kadima in Plainview, offering swim, sports, and arts with a warm Jewish-cultural community and inclusion services. A neighborhood favorite for central Nassau families.

name: Middlebury Monterey Language Academy
description: A four-week teen language-immersion program under the Middlebury language-pledge model, where students commit to speaking only their chosen language — Spanish, French, Chinese, Arabic — the entire session. Serious linguistic gains in one summer.

name: Mile High Pines Camp
description: A Christian camp and retreat center in the mile-high forest between Redlands and Big Bear, hosting summer youth weeks with horses, a ropes course, and mountain-town charm. Smaller and more personal than SoCal's mega-camps.

name: Mill Basin Day Camp
description: A big, energetic South Brooklyn day camp with its own facilities plus pools and trip days, serving Mill Basin, Marine Park, and beyond. Known for high energy, theme weeks, and door-to-door busing.

name: Millstone 4-H Camp
description: A rustic Sandhills 4-H camp near Ellerbe with a spring-fed lake and CCC-era cabins, giving kids a screen-free week of paddling, riflery, and pine-woods exploring.

name: Miracle Ranch
description: CRISTA's western-themed horse camp on Horseshoe Lake near Port Orchard, where every camper can ride daily and advanced wranglers help run the barn. Cowboy church and campfires round out the ranch feel.

name: Mission Springs Camps
description: A redwood-canyon Christian conference center between Santa Cruz and Silicon Valley whose Frontier Ranch summer camps give kids a week of creek stomping, campfires, and gentle faith formation. Close enough to the Bay for easy drop-off.

name: Mo-Ranch Summer Camps
description: A Presbyterian conference center and camp on a gorgeous stretch of the North Fork of the Guadalupe, known for its historic slide into the river and thoughtful, open-hearted faith programming.

name: Mohawk Day Camp
description: A big, classic Westchester day camp on a sprawling wooded campus off Route 22, with pools, ropes courses, and a huge activity menu. Known for warm staff and a something-for-everyone program.

name: Montana Wilderness School
description: A Bozeman-based nonprofit running small-group backpacking, packrafting, and mountaineering expeditions for teens across Montana's wild ranges, with an emphasis on leadership and self-reliance.

name: Montlure Camp
description: A Presbyterian camp in the White Mountains near Greer, at 8,500 feet among spruce and aspens, offering small, community-centered weeks of hiking, fishing, and campfire worship.

name: Moose River Outpost
description: A Christian adventure camp in the remote border town of Jackman, using Maine's wildest corner - whitewater, mountains, moose country - as the backdrop for faith-and-grit summer weeks.

name: Mount Cross
description: A Lutheran camp in the Santa Cruz Mountains redwoods offering gentle, song-filled cabin weeks, day camps, and a welcoming, all-are-beloved theology. Small enough that the director knows every camper's name by Tuesday.

name: Mount Hermon (Redwood Camp & Ponderosa)
description: A century-old Christian conference center among the Santa Cruz Mountains redwoods, whose Redwood Camp and Ponderosa teen camp deliver classic camp weeks with a redwood canopy zip line course overhead. Deeply rooted in Bay Area church life.

name: Mount Norris Scout Reservation
description: Vermont's own Scout camp on Lake Eden in the Green Mountains — small, unpolished, and quietly beautiful, with Mount Norris itself to climb before breakfast.

name: Mount Tom Day Camp
description: A beloved old-fashioned day camp on a wooded mountainside in Pleasantville since the 1930s, emphasizing nature, free play, and a slower, gentler pace than the big flashy camps. A rare traditional gem in Westchester.

name: Mountain Camp
description: A choice-driven camp on Ice House Reservoir in the El Dorado National Forest near Tahoe, where kids pick their own daily schedule of sailing, wakeboarding, mountain biking, and arts. Bay Area families prize its confident, kid-led culture.

name: Mountain Meadow Ranch
description: A family-owned ranch camp on hundreds of acres in far northeastern California, where a small camper crew rides horses, tends animals, and roams ponderosa forest. Feels like being sent to a beloved relative's ranch for the summer.

name: The Mountaineers Summer Camps
description: Climbing and adventure day camps at the Mountaineers' Magnuson Park program center, with real rock walls, kayaking, and field trips into the Cascades run by the storied climbing club. A launchpad for future alpinists.

name: Mt. Hood Summer Ski Camps
description: The venerable race-and-freestyle ski training camp on Palmer Glacier, where gate training and mogul coaching draw serious junior skiers from race programs nationwide. Less party, more PSIA than the snowboard camps down the road.

name: Mystic Seaport Museum Sailing Camp
description: Overnight sailing camp at Mystic Seaport where kids bunk aboard the historic square-rigger Joseph Conrad and spend their days sailing the Mystic River. A one-of-a-kind week for nautical kids.

name: NaCoMe Camp & Retreat Center
description: A Presbyterian camp in a quiet Hickman County creek valley an hour west of Nashville, with creek wading, low-key traditions, and a famously welcoming staff culture. Small, sweet, and unhurried.

name: NaCoMe Camp
description: A Presbyterian camp in a quiet Middle Tennessee creek valley, serving church kids with creek play, campfires, and unhurried, grace-centered community.

name: National Computer Camps
description: Widely credited as America's first computer camp, running no-frills weeklong coding sessions on college campuses since the punch-card era. Less slick than the big brands, but beloved for its old-school, substance-over-marketing approach.

name: National Debate Forum
description: An intensive summer debate institute near Boston covering public forum, Lincoln-Douglas, and congressional debate, staffed by top high school and college coaches. Competitive debaters go to sharpen; beginners go to learn the ropes.

name: Michigan Debate Institutes
description: The University of Michigan's storied policy debate camp, a rite of passage for serious high school policy debaters who spend weeks cutting cards and drilling on campus. Widely regarded as one of the most rigorous debate institutes in the country.

name: National Student Leadership Conference
description: Career-focused summer programs for high schoolers on university campuses in DC, Chicago, and beyond, with simulations in medicine, law, engineering, and international affairs. Part academic immersion, part college preview.

name: Nature Camp
description: A beloved natural-history camp in the Big Mary's Creek valley where campers take actual classes — geology, herpetology, botany — then swim in the creek and sing at vespers. Nerdy in the most joyful way.

name: NatureBridge Olympic Summer Camps
description: Field-science summer camps run from a historic campus on Lake Crescent inside Olympic National Park, with backpacking, canoeing, and hands-on ecology led by naturalist educators. For kids who want a national park as their camp.

name: NBC Basketball Camps
description: A Northwest institution running intense overnight basketball camps across the region from its Spokane base, known for demanding fundamentals and a strong character-building message. Many Gonzaga-country players grew up on NBC camps.

name: Nebraska State 4-H Camp
description: The state 4-H camp set improbably inside the hand-planted Nebraska National Forest at Halsey, where Sandhills kids hike forest dunes and do classic 4-H weeks.

name: Charles River Center Camp
description: A specialized day camp in Needham for children and teens with developmental disabilities, offering adapted recreation, arts, and life skills with high staff ratios. A trusted resource for greater Boston families needing dedicated support.

name: NeSoDak Bible Camp
description: A Lutherans Outdoors camp on Enemy Swim Lake in northeastern South Dakota, offering classic lake weeks, canoeing, and warm ELCA community for prairie families.

name: New England Science & Sailing (NESS) Camp
description: An ocean-adventure nonprofit in Stonington Borough running sailing, marine science, and paddleboarding camps right on Fishers Island Sound. STEM credentials with salt spray.

name: Nevada State 4-H Camp
description: The University of Nevada Extension's historic 4-H camp near Lake Tahoe's south shore, hosting affordable weeks where kids from across Nevada do waterfront, forestry, and leadership projects.

name: New Country Day Camp (14th Street Y)
description: The 14th Street Y's outdoorsy day camp in Staten Island's Greenbelt, where downtown Manhattan and Brooklyn kids get creeks, gardens, and arts with a progressive, culturally Jewish frame.

name: New England Frontier Camp
description: A Christian camp on Horseshoe Pond in Lovell with deep Christian Service Brigade roots, mixing classic waterfront and adventure programming with intentional discipleship in separate boys' and girls' weeks.

name: New England Music Camp
description: A venerable conservatory-style music camp on Messalonskee Lake (the 'Snow Pond' campus), where serious young musicians rehearse with top faculty and perform weekly at the lakeside Bowl in the Pines.

name: New Life Ranch
description: A well-loved Oklahoma ranch camp on Flint Creek offering horses, creek swimming, and solid Bible teaching at a price that keeps families coming back for generations.

name: Nike Sports Camp at Babson
description: A branded sports-skills day camp on Babson College fields near Boston, focusing on a single sport per week with college coaches and facilities. A serious option for athletic greater-Boston kids.

name: Night Eagle Wilderness Adventures
description: A tiny primitive-skills camp in the Green Mountains where boys live in tipis, cook over fire, track animals, and go days without anything that plugs in.

name: Nike Sports Camps (US Sports Camps)
description: The nation's largest network of branded sports camps, run by US Sports Camps from its California headquarters with weeklong day and overnight programs in nearly every sport at hundreds of colleges. Quality tracks the local directors, who are usually college or high school coaches.

name: NMH Summer Session
description: Northfield Mount Hermon's summer school above the Connecticut River, mixing college-prep academics and ESL with afternoon sports and arts on a gorgeous hilltop campus. Gentler in tone than some peer programs.

name: Nobles Day Camp
description: The Noble and Greenough School's long-running day camp, with pool complexes, turf fields, and arts studios most camps can only dream about. A well-oiled classic for Boston's southwest suburbs.

name: NOLS Wyoming Wilderness Courses
description: The National Outdoor Leadership School's flagship base in Lander runs backpacking and mountaineering expeditions for teens deep in the Wind River Range, teaching serious wilderness and leadership skills.

name: North Shore Day Camp
description: A polished Glen Cove day camp with heated pools, athletics, and specialty programs on a leafy North Shore campus. Draws families from across Nassau's Gold Coast towns.

name: NorthBay
description: A modern experiential-education camp on the upper Chesapeake Bay blending outdoor adventure, character education, and media arts. Best known for weeklong programs that mix ziplines and kayaking with reflection and teamwork.

name: Northern Tier High Adventure Base
description: Scouting's oldest high-adventure program, launching canoe treks into the Boundary Waters and Canada's Quetico from the edge of Ely. Portages, loons, and utter silence — wilderness canoeing at its purest.

name: Northern Virginia 4-H Educational Center
description: The 4-H center at the northern gateway to Skyline Drive, where week-long county camps mix pool time, riflery, and mountain hikes for kids from across northern Virginia.

name: Occohannock on the Bay
description: A United Methodist camp on a quiet Chesapeake creek on the Eastern Shore, where kids sail, kayak, and watch herons between chapel songs — humble, salty, and sweet.

name: Ocean Institute Summer Camps
description: Marine-science day camps at Dana Point Harbor's teaching institute, where kids run plankton labs, board a tall ship, and take research-vessel cruises past whales and dolphins. Science-serious but joyful.

name: OMSI Camp Hancock Field Station
description: The Oregon Museum of Science and Industry's high-desert field station near the John Day Fossil Beds, where campers dig real fossils, map geology, and stargaze in some of Oregon's darkest skies. Science camp with legitimate scientific pedigree.

name: Onteora Scout Reservation
description: Long Island troops' Catskills getaway near Livingston Manor, with a mountain lake, trout streams, and the cool forest air that made the Catskills famous.

name: Orme Summer Camp
description: A working-ranch summer camp on the historic Orme School campus in central Arizona, where campers ride daily on a genuine cattle ranch and cool off in the pool between rodeo games and art.

name: OSRUI (Olin-Sang-Ruby Union Institute)
description: The Reform movement's first camp, on Lac La Belle in Oconomowoc since 1952, organized into distinct units from rustic tents to Hebrew-immersion — music and creative Jewish expression are the camp's signature.

name: Oswegatchie Educational Center
description: Run by New York FFA on lakes near Croghan, this camp blends traditional summer fun with agriculture, forestry, and leadership programming — a favorite of rural and 4-H/FFA families.

name: Outlaw Ranch
description: A Lutherans Outdoors camp in the Black Hills near Custer, mixing trail rides, Harney Peak-country hikes, and family weeks with easygoing Lutheran warmth.

name: Outpost Summer Camps
description: A nature-forward San Diego day camp where kids build forts, learn archery and outdoor skills, and get properly dirty on inland-county acreage. A refreshing screen-free counterweight to activity-mill day camps.

name: Owasippe Scout Reservation
description: The oldest continuously operating Scout camp in America, founded 1911 and run by Chicago-area Scouting on nearly 5,000 acres near Twin Lake, Michigan — a summer pilgrimage for generations of Chicago Scouts.

name: Pacific Science Center Camps for Curious Minds
description: Science-themed day camps run by Pacific Science Center at Seattle Center and satellite locations, covering robotics, chemistry, dinosaurs, and more. Museum access and real educators give it substance beyond babysitting.

name: Pali Adventures
description: A specialty-track camp in the San Bernardino mountains where kids major in everything from Hollywood stunts and DJ-ing to secret agent training, with afternoons of full elective freedom. Flashy, fun, and unlike any traditional camp.

name: Park Shore Country Day Camp
description: A long-running Suffolk County day camp on 15 acres in Dix Hills, with multiple heated pools, a water park feel, and add-ons like travel and teen programs. Big, energetic, and activity-packed.

name: Park Slope Day Camp
description: A long-running brownstone-Brooklyn camp using Prospect Park as its backyard, with sports, trips, swim outings, and flexible weekly signup. A neighborhood institution for Park Slope and Windsor Terrace kids.

name: Paskowitz Surf Camp
description: The original American surf camp, founded by the famous surfing Paskowitz family, running weeklong sessions at San Onofre's mellow breaks. Small groups, family lore, and a pure old-California soul-surfing ethos.

name: Peaceful Valley Scout Ranch
description: The Denver-area council's sprawling scout ranch on the Palmer Divide, with horse programs, shooting sports, and ATV and climbing options spread across thousands of prairie-and-pine acres.

name: Perry-Mansfield Performing Arts School & Camp
description: The oldest continuously operating performing arts camp in the country, training young dancers, actors, and musicians in aspen-shaded studios above Steamboat Springs since 1913. Serious training with a mountain-camp soul.

name: Peterkin Camp & Conference Center
description: The Episcopal camp of West Virginia, set on the South Branch of the Potomac near Romney, offering river swimming, hiking, and gentle faith community in a beautiful mountain valley.

name: Phantom Lake YMCA Camp
description: One of the oldest camps in continuous operation in America, running on Phantom Lake since 1896, offering old-fashioned coed camp weeks at genuinely accessible prices.

name: Philmont Scout Ranch
description: Scouting's legendary high-adventure base: 140,000 acres of Sangre de Cristo backcountry where crews backpack 50-100 miles between staffed camps offering everything from blacksmithing to spar-pole climbing. A bucket-list trek, not a cabin camp.

name: Pierce Country Day Camp
description: A century-old Long Island institution in Roslyn with immaculate grounds, pools everywhere, and legendary camp spirit. One of the priciest day camps around, and families still line up for it.

name: Pilgrim Lodge
description: The Maine Conference UCC's beloved camp on Cobbosseecontee Lake, known for progressive, affirming week-long sessions where theology is gentle and belonging comes first.

name: Pilgrim Center
description: A United Church of Christ camp on Green Lake in central Wisconsin, with an affirming, welcoming community, lakefront activities, and gentle faith formation.

name: Pilgrim Pines Camp
description: A progressive United Church of Christ camp in Oak Glen's apple country, where inclusive theology meets classic cabin weeks, campfires, and mountain meadows. Known for welcoming every kid exactly as they are.

name: Pine Cove Camps
description: A multi-camp Christian powerhouse headquartered near Tyler, running themed one-week camps (Towers, Shores, Ranch and more) plus Hill Country outposts, all known for wildly enthusiastic counselors with nicknames like Waffle and Tater. Gospel-centered and high-energy.

name: Pine Cove Crier Creek
description: Pine Cove's family camp campus between Houston and Austin, where parents and kids camp together with the same over-the-top staff energy and Christ-centered programming.

name: Pine Cove Camps (Tyler)
description: Pine Cove's original East Texas campus runs multiple themed camps like the Towers and Timbers, famous for zany costumed staff, lake blobs, and clear gospel teaching.

name: Pine Island Camp
description: A tiny island camp on Great Pond where boys sleep in tents, live without electricity, and head out on serious wilderness trips. One of the oldest and most deliberately old-fashioned camps in the country.

name: Pine Tree Camp
description: Pine Tree Society's fully accessible camp on North Pond in the Belgrade Lakes, purpose-built so children and adults with disabilities can swim, boat, and fish like at any classic Maine camp.

name: Pinebrook Bible Camp
description: A classic Bible camp in the Poconos running structured, worship-centered weeks with pool time, sports, and chapel twice a day. Generations of evangelical families from the Philadelphia region grew up on Pinebrook summers.

name: Pinemere Camp
description: A mid-sized nonprofit Jewish camp in the Poconos known for its inclusive, down-to-earth community and joyful Shabbat culture. Big enough for great facilities, small enough that directors know every camper.

name: Pinewood Christian Camp
description: A rustic Christian camp in the West Central Mountains near New Meadows and Payette Lake country, serving Idaho youth with classic camp weeks of games, hikes, and Bible study.

name: Pipsico Scout Reservation
description: Tidewater Virginia's camp on bluffs above the James River, where sea kayaking, sailing, and fossil-hunting on the beach give it a coastal-plain personality all its own.

name: Placerville Camp
description: A United Church of Christ camp along Rapid Creek in the Black Hills west of Rapid City, known for its inclusive, open-minded church camp weeks in a historic creekside canyon.

name: Planet Bravo Techno-Camp
description: An LA-grown tech day camp at private-school campuses around the city, mixing coding, game design, and digital filmmaking with goofy spirit days that keep it feeling like camp rather than class. A two-decade staple for LA tech kids.

name: Plantation Farm Camp
description: A working farm camp on the Sonoma coast ridge where kids milk cows, gather eggs, ride horses, and eat food they helped raise. Tiny, screen-free, and proudly old-fashioned since 1952.

name: Play-Well TEKnologies LEGO Camps
description: A Marin-born engineering camp that teaches physics and machine design through tens of thousands of LEGO pieces, at recreation centers all over California. Half-day formats and universal kid appeal make it an easy first camp.

name: Pocono Plateau Camp & Retreat Center
description: A United Methodist camp spread across high Pocono woodland with a private lake, offering faith-filled one-week sessions and a quieter alternative to the region's big private camps.

name: Point CounterPoint
description: A chamber music camp on the shore of Lake Dunmore where serious young string players and pianists rehearse quartets in lakeside cabins, then jump in the water between coachings.

name: Poly Summer Day Camp
description: Poly Prep's summer camp on its 25-acre Dyker Heights campus — rare green space in Brooklyn — with pools, fields, and academics-meets-camp programming. A polished option for southwest Brooklyn families.

name: Pozez JCC Camp
description: The Pozez JCC of Northern Virginia's day camps in Fairfax, spanning traditional, sports, and specialty tracks with a warm Jewish-cultural community and strong inclusion services. A DC-metro cornerstone.

name: Prescott Pines Camp
description: A busy Christian camp in the ponderosas south of Prescott hosting summer youth weeks with ziplines, gaga ball, and chapel — a longtime favorite of Arizona church groups escaping the desert heat.

name: Pro Ambitions Hockey Camps
description: A Boston-based hockey camp brand founded by a former pro, running high-tempo 'battle camps' at rinks across the Northeast and beyond, with some overnight college-campus weeks. Known for small-area compete drills over lazy scrimmages.

name: Advantage Tennis Camp
description: A tennis-focused day camp on Brooklyn's public courts, teaching stroke fundamentals and match play by the week for city kids. A focused pick for aspiring young players.

name: Prude Ranch Summer Camp
description: A working guest ranch camp in the cool Davis Mountains of far West Texas, where campers ride real ranch horses through high-desert country and stargaze near McDonald Observatory. The mile-high elevation beats the summer heat.

name: Pyoca Camp & Retreat Center
description: A Presbyterian camp in the wooded hills of southern Indiana with a spring-fed lake, warm small-camp culture, and an openhearted approach to faith and belonging.

name: Quaker Lake Camp
description: A gentle Quaker camp on a small lake south of Greensboro, where week-long sessions mix swimming, crafts, and quiet worship in the Friends tradition.

name: Quaker Ridge Camp
description: A Christian camp near Woodland Park on the slopes of Pikes Peak, running active youth weeks with a lake, sports, and Bible teaching for Front Range church families.

name: Suffolk Y JCC Day Camp
description: The Suffolk Y JCC's day camp in Commack, offering swim, sports, and arts with a warm Jewish-cultural community and inclusion support. A central Suffolk hub for Long Island families.

name: Rain City Rock Camp
description: A Seattle nonprofit where girls and gender-expansive youth form a band on Monday and play a real venue showcase by Saturday, coached by local musicians. Loud, affirming, and confidence-building by design.

name: Rainbow Trail Lutheran Camp
description: A beloved ELCA Lutheran camp in the Sangre de Cristos with joyful song-leading culture, small-group weeks, and backpacking options for teens. Alumni loyalty here runs deep across the region.

name: Ramah Day Camp in Chicago
description: Chicagoland's Ramah day camp in the northern suburbs, pairing pools and sports with Hebrew, song, and Shabbat celebration in the Conservative tradition. Many campers graduate to Ramah Wisconsin.

name: Ramah Day Camp in Nyack
description: A big, leafy Conservative day camp just over the Hudson from the city, with pools, Hebrew woven into the day, and buses running throughout the NYC metro area. For many families it's the on-ramp to overnight Ramah.

name: Ramah Day Camp in Philadelphia
description: The Philadelphia area's Ramah day camp, mixing swim, sports, and arts with daily Hebrew and Jewish living in a warm Conservative setting. A natural feeder to the overnight Ramah camps.

name: Ramah in the Rockies
description: The Ramah movement's Jewish outdoor adventure camp in the foothills southwest of Denver, sending every camper on masa backcountry trips and framing wilderness through Jewish learning. Kosher, Shabbat-observant, and refreshingly rugged.

name: Ramah Sports Academy
description: Ramah's sports-immersion camp on a Connecticut university campus, where campers major in a sport with high-level coaching while keeping kosher food, tefillah, and Shabbat at the center. Dorm living rather than cabins.

name: Ramaquois Day Camp
description: A big, spirited Rockland County day camp on 100 wooded acres in Pomona, with lakes, pools, ziplines, and a huge traditional program. A powerhouse camp that buses across Rockland and northern NJ.

name: Rancho del Chaparral
description: Girl Scouts of New Mexico Trails' mountain camp in the Jemez high country, with a well-loved horse program, mesa hikes, and affordable sessions that keep camp within reach for families statewide.

name: Ransburg Scout Reservation
description: Indiana's premier Scout camp on Lake Monroe, drawing troops from a dozen states for its enormous waterfront, sailing fleet, and famously energetic staff. One of the Midwest's big-league camps.

name: Raven Knob Scout Reservation
description: One of the largest Scout camps in the region, spread across a mountain bowl near Mount Airy with a storied Order of the Arrow tradition and merit-badge programming for visiting troops.

name: Rawhide Ranch
description: A Western-style horse camp in northern San Diego County where kids ride daily, work cattle, try vaulting, and learn ranch skills from a real working-ranch playbook. One of Southern California's classic horsey summers.

name: Recreation Unlimited
description: A fully accessible camp north of Columbus built for youth with physical and developmental disabilities, with adapted sports, a barrier-free waterfront, and expert staff.

name: Red Arrow Camp
description: A small, proudly rustic boys' camp on Trout Lake occupying a former logging camp, where axemanship, canoeing, and old-fashioned Northwoods skills still headline the program.

name: Red Pine Camp for Girls
description: A traditional girls' camp on Clear Lake near Minocqua that has been run with a steady hand since the 1930s, known for strong swimming instruction and multigenerational camper families.

name: Mount Hermon Redwood Camp
description: The kids' camp of the venerable Mount Hermon conference grounds in the Santa Cruz redwoods, mixing giant-tree wonder, zip lines, and warm gospel teaching.

name: Redwood Christian Park
description: A rustic Christian camp deep in the Boulder Creek redwoods hosting summer youth weeks and family camps under some of the tallest trees in the Santa Cruz Mountains. Simple, affordable, and steeped in old-camp atmosphere.

name: Renaissance Adventures
description: A one-of-a-kind Boulder day camp where kids solve mysteries and battle with foam swords in live-action roleplaying quests that sneak in teamwork, ethics, and improv. Catnip for imaginative kids.

name: Resica Falls Scout Reservation
description: Philadelphia's big Pocono camp built around its namesake waterfall on Bushkill Creek, with fly-fishing water, a 4,000-acre backcountry, and the Cradle of Liberty Council's summer traditions.

name: Ridge Haven
description: The conference and camp center of the Presbyterian Church in America, hidden in the mountains near Rosman. Week-long summer camps combine Reformed teaching with creeks, courts, and mountain air.

name: River Way Ranch Camp
description: A big, activity-packed ranch camp near Kings Canyon with over 70 options from jet skiing on Wonder Valley's lake to horseback and go-karts. A Central California fixture that draws campers from LA, the Bay, and abroad.

name: Riverdale YM-YWHA Day Camp
description: The Riverdale Y's day camp, busing Bronx and Westchester kids to swim, sports, and nature programming with a warm Jewish-cultural community. A neighborhood cornerstone in the northwest Bronx.

name: Riverside Lutheran Bible Camp
description: An ELCA Bible camp on the Skunk River in central Iowa, offering river tubing, campfires, and warm Lutheran community for church kids near Ames and Des Moines.

name: Riverview Camp for Girls
description: A Mentone girls camp on the West Fork of the Little River with a full riding program, tidy facilities, and shorter session options that make it a gentler entry point than its four-week neighbors. Warm, Christian, and mother-daughter famous.

name: Road's End Farm Horsemanship Camp
description: A small girls' horsemanship camp on a New Hampshire farm where each camper rides daily and shares responsibility for the herd's care — no showing, no ribbons, just deep horse knowledge. Families hand it down through generations.

name: Roaring Brook Camp for Boys
description: A small wilderness-skills camp in the hills above Bradford where boys live in cabins they help maintain, learn axe and paddle craft, and take real trips into the Greens and Whites.

name: Robin Hood Camp
description: An exclusive coed camp on Walker Pond near the Blue Hill peninsula, known for its country-club-caliber golf, tennis, and sailing offerings and an international, well-heeled camper base.

name: Rock Eagle 4-H Camp
description: Georgia 4-H's massive center near Eatonton, named for the ancient stone eagle effigy on its grounds, running affordable classic camp weeks with wildlife classes, a lake, and huge dining-hall energy. Many Georgia kids' very first sleepaway experience.

name: Rock Springs Ranch 4-H Camp
description: The Kansas 4-H flagship camp on a spring-fed Flint Hills ranch, hosting thousands of campers each summer for horseback, shooting sports, and classic 4-H camp weeks.

name: Rocky Mountain Mennonite Camp
description: A peaceful Mennonite camp on the flank of Pikes Peak where simple living, creation care, and community are the point — think hikes, crafts, and unhurried campfires rather than flashy amenities.

name: Rocky Mountain Village (Easterseals)
description: Easterseals Colorado's fully accessible camp near Empire, where campers with disabilities ride the adaptive zipline, fish, and do arts with one-on-one support in a joyful mountain setting.

name: Rocky River Ranch
description: A small girls' horse camp on Cypress Creek in Wimberley where every camper rides daily and the vibe is nurturing rather than competitive. Short sessions and a gentle culture make it a favorite first sleepaway camp.

name: Rolling River Day Camp
description: A family-run South Shore camp on a canal in East Rockaway, where kids kayak and paddleboard right off the property alongside pools, sports, and a big ropes course. Strong spirit and a devoted following in the Five Towns and beyond.

name: Ron Jon Surf School Summer Camp
description: Weeklong learn-to-surf day camps on Cocoa Beach run under the famous Ron Jon name, with small groups and warm, forgiving Atlantic waves. A classic Space Coast summer for Florida kids.

name: Rotary Sunshine Camp
description: Rochester Rotary's century-old camp in Rush, rebuilt with fully accessible modern facilities, giving children with disabilities a completely free week of camp with medical staff on site.

name: Roughing It Day Camp
description: An East Bay outdoor day camp at the Lafayette Reservoir where every camper rides horses, paddles, swims, and fishes in a deliberately old-fashioned, skill-building progression. Famously well-organized, with buses across the East Bay and beyond.

name: Camp Deeny Riback Overlook
description: A traditional day camp on wooded Morris County acreage in Flanders, with pools, sports, and a full activity program. A well-regarded option for families in the Route 206 corridor.

name: Roundup River Ranch
description: A SeriousFun Children's Network camp on the Colorado River near Gypsum, giving kids with serious illnesses a completely free, medically supported week of fishing, ziplining, and pure camp joy.

name: Rowe Camp
description: A tiny, deeply progressive camp in the hills of northwest Massachusetts with Unitarian roots, where teens talk about big ideas, make art, and roam the woods. Famously accepting of kids who never fit the sporty camp mold.

name: S bar F Scout Ranch
description: The Greater St. Louis Area Council's 5,200-acre ranch in the St. Francois Mountains, wrapped around Nims Lake with two full summer camps and a high-adventure base on one property.

name: Sabattis Adventure Camp
description: A Scouting high-adventure base deep in the central Adirondacks near Long Lake, where troops run multi-day canoe and backpacking treks across some of the wildest country in the Northeast.

name: Sacramento Methodist Assembly Youth Camp
description: A United Methodist camp and assembly in the Sacramento Mountains near Cloudcroft, hosting summer youth weeks with a pool, gym, hikes, and worship in the cool southern New Mexico high country.

name: Saddlebrook Tennis Academy Summer Camp
description: The resort-based Tampa-area academy long associated with the Harry Hopman training method, where juniors drill on dozens of courts in every surface. It has hosted future tour pros for decades.

name: Sail Sand Point Summer Camps
description: Community boating center camps at Magnuson Park on Lake Washington, teaching kids to sail Optis and 420s with a progression from first tiller to racing. Nonprofit pricing keeps sailing surprisingly attainable.

name: Salish Sea Expeditions
description: Live-aboard marine science voyages on Puget Sound where teens sail a tall-masted vessel, run plankton tows, and analyze real oceanographic data. Equal parts sailing crew and floating lab.

name: Saltash Mountain Camp (Farm & Wilderness)
description: A small hiking-centered Farm & Wilderness camp on Lake Ninevah where the cabin group is the whole community and multi-day treks through the Green Mountains anchor each session.

name: SAMBICA
description: A century-old Christian camp on the shore of Lake Sammamish in Bellevue, running packed summer day camps with wakeboarding, blobs, and waterslides plus some overnight weeks. An Eastside institution.

name: Sanborn Western Camps
description: Big Spring Ranch for boys and High Trails Ranch for girls share 6,000 acres of high country near Pikes Peak, with a signature mix of geology hikes, horse trips, and mountain summits. Campers choose their own trips, which older kids especially love.

name: Sandy Hill Camp
description: A camp on the Elk River at the head of the Chesapeake Bay, offering sailing, waterskiing, and a busy waterfront alongside a full land program. A longtime favorite for families across the mid-Atlantic.

name: Sanibel Sea School
description: A small marine-conservation day camp on Sanibel Island where kids surf, seine, and study mollusks with ocean educators, barefoot from morning to pickup. Beloved by locals and vacationing families alike.

name: Santa Catalina Summer Camp
description: A girls' residential camp on the elegant Santa Catalina School campus in Monterey, blending riding, marine science, arts, and pool time with Catholic-school polish. A refined alternative to rustic mountain camps.

name: Schoellkopf Scout Reservation
description: The Iroquois Trail Council's Scout camp in the rural countryside near Cowlesville, between Buffalo and Batavia, offering traditional merit-badge weeks and outdoor skills for western New York troops.

name: School of Rock Summer Camp
description: A rock-band day camp where kids learn songs as a group and play a live show at the end of the week, run out of the Montclair School of Rock. A focused pick for young musicians in North Jersey.

name: Scioto Hills Camp
description: A Baptist camp tucked into the Appalachian foothills of southern Ohio, with a lake blob, zipline, and paintball alongside energetic gospel-centered teaching.

name: Sea Camp at Texas A&M Galveston
description: A marine science camp on the Texas A&M Galveston campus where teens trawl the Gulf on research vessels, dissect sharks, and sleep in university dorms. The rare camp that shows up on college applications.

name: Seacamp
description: The pioneering marine-science camp in the Florida Keys, where teens scuba dive, sail, and study reef ecology with marine biologists on Newfound Harbor. Future oceanographers find their calling here.

name: SEACAMP San Diego
description: A residential marine-science camp on Mission Bay where teens snorkel kelp forests, take boat-based labs, and handle sharks and rays under instructor guidance. Ideal for future marine biologists who want field time, not just lectures.

name: Seattle Children's Theatre Summer Camps
description: Drama day camps at one of the country's premier children's theaters, at Seattle Center, taught by working teaching artists and ending in real performances. Serious craft delivered playfully.

name: Seeds of Peace Camp
description: The famous dialogue camp on Pleasant Lake that brings teens from conflict regions and the US together for facilitated dialogue sessions woven into a traditional camp day.

name: Sephardic Adventure Camp
description: One of the only Sephardic overnight camps in the country, run by Seattle's historic Sephardic community in the Cascades, keeping Ladino songs, Sephardic customs, and traditional observance alive around the campfire.

name: Camp Sequanota
description: A Lutheran camp in the Laurel Highlands of southwestern Pennsylvania, offering mountain hikes, crafts, and campfire worship for western PA families.

name: Seven Ranges Scout Reservation
description: The Buckeye Council's big eastern-Ohio reservation, with rolling hardwood hills, a solid lake program, and a reputation for spirited staff campfires.

name: Sewanee Summer Music Festival
description: An orchestral training festival on the University of the South's Gothic mountain campus, where students play in multiple orchestras with weekly seating auditions. A Southern classical institution with gorgeous surroundings.

name: Shadow Rim Ranch
description: A Girl Scout ranch camp under the Mogollon Rim near Payson, where the horse program is the star and girls build confidence through riding, hiking, and unplugged cabin life.

name: Shaffer's High Sierra Camp
description: A small adventure camp in the Lost Sierra north of Truckee where campers choose daily from mountain biking, climbing, rafting trips, and a high-alpine lake circuit. Run hands-on by its founding family with a big-adventure, small-community ethos.

name: Shelburne Farms Summer Camp
description: Day camps on a 1,400-acre working farm and National Historic Landmark on Lake Champlain, where kids collect eggs, make cheese, and roam pastures with Adirondack views.

name: Shepherd of the Ozarks
description: A Christ-centered adventure camp on the Buffalo River headwaters in the remote Arkansas Ozarks, hosting church groups for ziplining, caving, and horseback in a spectacular canyon setting. Groups rave about the scenery and hospitality.

name: Shepherd's Fold Ranch
description: A Christian camp on rolling Osage County ranchland north of Tulsa, mixing horsemanship, paintball, and creek play with earnest discipleship. Its small size and long-serving staff give it a family-reunion feel.

name: Sherman Lake YMCA Day Camp
description: A big, well-run YMCA day camp on Sherman Lake between Kalamazoo and Battle Creek, with a true waterfront, character-development focus, and busing that makes summer logistics easy for area families.

name: Sherwood Forest Camp
description: A nonprofit camp on the Black River in the Missouri Ozarks that brings St. Louis kids from under-resourced neighborhoods into long, skill-building summers of river swimming, backpacking, and mentorship — most on deep scholarship. Quietly one of the region's most mission-driven camps.

name: Shwayder Camp
description: Temple Emanuel Denver's Reform Jewish camp high on Mount Blue Sky, where song sessions, hikes above treeline, and warm Jewish community have anchored summers since the 1940s.

name: Sid Richardson Scout Ranch
description: A big lakefront ranch camp on Lake Bridgeport northwest of Fort Worth, mixing water sports and sailing with horse program on two thousand acres of cross-timbers country.

name: Silver Lake Conference Center
description: The United Church of Christ camp in Connecticut's northwest hills, known for its affirming, justice-minded community and lakeside worship for teens and tweens.

name: Sitka Fine Arts Camp
description: Alaska's premier arts camp, held on the historic Sheldon Jackson campus in Sitka, where serious young musicians, actors, dancers, and visual artists study with professional faculty between rainforest hikes and whale sightings. A national-caliber program in a temperate-rainforest island town.

name: Skogfjorden (Concordia Language Villages)
description: Concordia's Norwegian language village, a favorite of Minnesota's Scandinavian-heritage families, blending immersion with hikes, folk music, and a big dose of friluftsliv on Turtle River Lake.

name: Sky High Ranch
description: A Girl Scout camp on the north slope of Pikes Peak with a strong western riding program, dark-sky stargazing, and backcountry adventures for older girls.

name: Sky Lake
description: A Presbyterian camp on its own lake in the Southern Tier hills near Windsor, offering affordable, welcoming week sessions of canoeing, crafts, and lakeside worship for Broome County families.

name: Sky Lake Camp
description: A United Methodist camp on a lake in New York's Southern Tier near Binghamton, offering swimming, crafts, and gentle faith community for upstate families.

name: Whispering Hope Ranch
description: A ranch retreat under the Mogollon Rim near Payson that hosts low-cost and free camp weeks for children with disabilities and special medical needs, with an accessible animal and horse program.

name: Horn Creek
description: A Christian family camp and youth camp at the foot of the Sangre de Cristos near Westcliffe, offering horses, ropes courses, and fourteener hikes with worship in a dramatic mountain valley.

name: Sky Ranch Lutheran Camp
description: An ELCA camp at 9,000 feet in the Poudre Canyon high country west of Fort Collins, keeping things intentionally small and rustic with mountain hikes, faith conversations, and cool nights around the fire.

name: Sky Ranch Ute Trail
description: Sky Ranch's Colorado high-country outpost near Lake City, pairing the organization's polished Christian camping model with San Juan trail rides, mountain biking, and alpine adventure.

name: Sky Ranch
description: A big, well-resourced Christian camp east of Dallas with an extensive horse program, water park-style lakefront, and polished cabins that suit first-timers.

name: Camp Pinerock
description: A Methodist camp in the ponderosa pines near Prescott offering traditional youth camp weeks of hiking, crafts, and campfires in the cool high country. Verify current summer offerings before enrolling.

name: Skycroft Conference Center
description: A Baptist camp and conference center high on South Mountain in western Maryland, with a zip line, pool, and mountaintop views, running structured, worship-centered summer weeks.

name: Skye Farm Camp
description: A United Methodist camp on Sherman Lake near Warrensburg offering low-cost week sessions of swimming, campfires, and gentle faith formation in a quiet Adirondack valley.

name: Skylake Yosemite Camp
description: A classic lakefront camp on Bass Lake's quiet shore near Yosemite, beloved for sailing, wakeboarding, and long-standing traditions like lazy rest-hour reading on the deck. Multi-week sessions build famously tight cabin bonds.

name: Skylark Ranch
description: A Girl Scout horse ranch in the coastal hills near Año Nuevo, where fog-cooled riding, tide-pool trips, and redwood campfires make a distinctly Northern California summer.

name: Skymont Scout Reservation
description: A Cumberland Plateau camp atop the mountain near Altamont, Tennessee, with sandstone bluffs, cave country, and cooler plateau air for Chattanooga-area troops.

name: Skyview Ranch
description: A Christian ranch camp in Ohio's Amish country hills, with horses, a lake, and energetic chapel times serving church families across northeast Ohio.

name: Slide Ranch Summer Camp
description: A teaching farm perched on coastal cliffs between Muir Beach and Stinson, where day campers milk goats, harvest kale, meet chickens, and explore tidepools below the pasture. Farm-to-fog childhood at its most vivid.

name: Slippery Falls Scout Ranch
description: A southern Oklahoma ranch camp named for the granite waterslide falls on its creek — swimming holes, ranges, and unpolished cowboy-country Scouting.

name: Smirkus Camp
description: Circus Smirkus's training camp in the Northeast Kingdom, where kids learn aerials, juggling, and clowning from professional coaches and end each session with a real show under the tent.

name: Snow Mountain Ranch Day Camp (YMCA of the Rockies)
description: YMCA of the Rockies' day camp at Snow Mountain Ranch near Granby, filling summer days with mountain biking, hikes, archery, and lake activities across 5,000 acres of high country.

name: SOAR
description: The pioneering adventure program for youth with ADHD and learning disabilities, running expedition-style courses — llama treks, whitewater, rock climbing — from its Blue Ridge base camp, with programs as far as Wyoming. Success is built on movement, not worksheets.

name: SOAR Wyoming
description: The Wyoming base of SOAR, the adventure program built specifically for kids with ADHD and learning differences, running llama treks, horse pack trips, and climbing with tiny staff ratios in the Winds.

name: SOCAPA: School of Creative and Performing Arts
description: Filmmaking, photography, acting, dance, and songwriting intensives for teens with campuses in New York, Burlington, and Los Angeles. Campers shoot real short films on city streets with pro gear and small crews.

name: Solid Rock Bible Camp
description: A nondenominational Bible camp on the Kenai Peninsula near Soldotna, with horses, lake canoeing, and wilderness trip options in the shadow of the Kenai Mountains. A fixture of Alaskan church-camp life for decades.

name: Songadeewin of Keewaydin
description: Keewaydin's sister camp on Lake Dunmore, giving girls the same serious canoe-tripping tradition — paddling, portaging, and singing back into camp sunburned and strong.

name: Sonlight Christian Camp
description: A small Christian camp in the San Juans near Pagosa Springs with a big reputation for depth over flash — small cabin groups, real wilderness, and staff who know every camper well.

name: Sound View Camp
description: A small Presbyterian camp and nature center on the Key Peninsula with beachfront on Drayton Passage, a farm garden, and goats. Earthy, ecumenical, and focused on creation care.

name: Southampton Camp & Club
description: A boutique Hamptons day camp with a country-club feel — pools, tennis, and polished programming for Southampton summer families, with flexible weekly enrollment. Small groups and high-touch service.

name: Space Camp
description: The world-famous residential STEM camp at the U.S. Space & Rocket Center in Huntsville, where trainees run simulated shuttle missions, feel 1/6th gravity, and sleep in space-station-style habitats. Kids fly in from around the globe.

name: Spring Heights Camp & Retreat Center
description: A United Methodist camp in the central West Virginia hills near Spencer, offering week-long sessions of swimming, hiking, crafts, and campfire worship in a peaceful rural setting.

name: SpringHill (Michigan Overnight)
description: A huge, high-production Christian camp near Evart where kids sleep in themed villages — teepees, tree houses, covered wagons — between ziplines, water toys, and nightly worship rallies.

name: SpringHill Camps (Indiana)
description: SpringHill's southern Indiana campus brings the same creative housing, adventure elements, and Christ-centered energy to families around Indianapolis, Louisville, and Cincinnati.

name: SpringHill Camps (Michigan)
description: One of the Midwest's biggest Christian camps, with quirky housing like tree houses and covered wagons, huge zip lines, and enthusiastic gospel-centered programming.

name: Spruce Lake
description: A Mennonite-rooted camp and retreat center in the Poconos with a strong outdoor school, mountain biking, and peaceable, Christ-centered community.

name: St. Columba Camp & Retreat Center
description: The Episcopal camp on 145 wooded acres just north of Memphis, running day camp weeks (plus mini overnight tastes) with a lake, trails, and inclusive, gentle spirituality. A green refuge minutes from the city.

name: St. Dorothy's Rest
description: The oldest camp in California, an Episcopal haven under the Sonoma redwoods founded in 1901, running small, tender summer sessions plus renowned camps for seriously ill children. Feels like summering in a storybook.

name: Stagedoor Manor
description: The legendary Catskills theater camp where campers mount full productions every three weeks and alumni include a who's-who of Broadway and Hollywood. Intense, audition-driven, and utterly beloved by theater kids.

name: Stamford JCC Day Camp
description: The Stamford JCC's day camp, offering swim, sports, and specialty tracks with a warm Jewish-cultural community and inclusion support. A hub for lower Fairfield County Jewish families.

name: Steve & Kate's Camp
description: The radically self-directed day camp born in Marin and now at sites across California, where kids choose minute-to-minute among sewing salons, bread baking, coding, and stunt trampolines. Any-day passes and no set schedule - kids drive everything.

name: Camp Ramah Nyack Day Camp
description: The Ramah day camp in Nyack, offering swim, sports, and arts with immersive Hebrew, Israeli culture, and Shabbat in an observant Conservative-movement setting. Kosher and joyfully Jewish for Rockland-area families.

name: Stone Mountain Adventures
description: A small teens-only adventure camp in central Pennsylvania's ridge country offering mountain biking, climbing, wakeboarding on Raystown Lake, and riding, with a tight-knit, phone-free social culture.

name: Storm Mountain Center
description: A United Methodist camp tucked into a pine canyon in the Black Hills outside Rapid City, offering mountain hikes, creek play, and quiet faith weeks.

name: Strong River Camp & Farm
description: A family-run camp and working farm on the Strong River south of Jackson, where river swims, horses, and barefoot farm life have shaped Mississippi childhoods since the 1920s. Word-of-mouth keeps it full.

name: Strong Rock Camp
description: A newer Christian camp near Cleveland, Georgia built by camp-industry veterans, with excellent facilities, separate boys and girls programs, and lots of Atlanta families. Polished but intentionally traditional in feel.

name: Sugar Pine Christian Camps
description: A Christian camp on a private lake in the pines above Oakhurst near Yosemite, hosting affordable church youth weeks with blobs, canoes, and campfire worship. A Central Valley favorite for decades.

name: SummerTrails Day Camp
description: A big traditional day camp on wooded acreage in northern Westchester's Granite Springs, with pools, go-karts, a skate park, and a packed activity roster. Energetic and adventure-leaning.

name: Summit Bechtel Reserve
description: The newest of Scouting's high-adventure bases, built beside the New River Gorge with world-class BMX tracks, skate parks, zip lines, and whitewater access. More X-Games than woodcraft.

name: Sunrise Day Camp
description: The original site of the only network of free day camps for children with cancer and their siblings, offering full summers of swimming, sports, and arts with medical staff on hand and door-to-door transportation. Siblings attend free too.

name: Sunset Lake Camp
description: A Seventh-day Adventist camp on its own lake near Wilkeson in the Carbon River foothills, offering horses, wakeboarding, and a rock wall with vegetarian dining-hall meals. Sabbath traditions anchor the week.

name: Surf Diva Surf Camp
description: The La Jolla surf school founded by twin sisters as the first surf school for women and girls, now running upbeat kids' day camps at gentle La Jolla Shores. Encouraging coaching with a strong girls-can-charge spirit.

name: Surprise Lake Camp
description: The oldest Jewish camp in the country, tucked above Cold Spring since 1902, famous for generous scholarships, an unpretentious crunchy vibe, and alumni ranging from Eddie Cantor to Neil Diamond.

name: Suttle Lake Camp
description: A United Methodist camp on the shore of Suttle Lake in the Cascades near Sisters, with paddle time, ponderosa forest, and inclusive church-camp community. Central Oregon's mellow lakeside classic.

name: Swan Lake Christian Camp
description: A Mennonite-rooted camp on Swan Lake in southeast South Dakota, where prairie church communities send kids for lake weeks, hymn sings, and homemade pie.

name: Flathead Lutheran Bible Camp
description: An ELCA Lutheran camp on the west shore of Flathead Lake near Lakeside, where Montana kids swim, canoe, and sing around the fire through week-long summer sessions. A regional institution with deep alumni roots.

name: T Bar M Camps
description: A sports-focused Christian camp on the Guadalupe River in New Braunfels, blending quality athletic coaching with Hill Country river fun and intentional discipleship.

name: Talisman Camps
description: A western North Carolina adventure camp exclusively for kids with autism, ADHD, and related learning differences, using tight structure, small groups, and mountain adventure to build independence. Decades of specialization show in the programming.

name: Tall Timber Ranch
description: A Presbyterian camp tucked up the White River valley beyond Lake Wenatchee, surrounded by Glacier Peak Wilderness. Small sessions, backpacking trips, and a genuine end-of-the-road mountain feel.

name: Tamarack Camps (Camp Maas)
description: Metro Detroit's Jewish camping institution since 1902 — Camp Maas in Ortonville anchors a system that includes teen travel and wilderness trips, with generous scholarships keeping it open to the whole community.

name: Tamarack Day Camp
description: A big traditional day camp on wooded Morris County acreage in Randolph, with a lake, pools, and a broad activity menu. Strong swim instruction and camp spirit for central-north Jersey families.

name: Tamarack Farm (Farm & Wilderness)
description: Farm & Wilderness's teen village, where older campers run a working farm, build real structures, and practice community decision-making — half camp, half apprenticeship in adulthood.

name: Tanager Lodge
description: A tiny, deeply rustic camp on Upper Chateaugay Lake founded in 1925 — kids sleep in open-air cabins, paddle wooden canoes, and take real wilderness trips. Almost nothing has changed in a century, on purpose.

name: Tanglewood 4-H Camp
description: A University of Maine 4-H camp in the forest along the Ducktrap River near Camden, focused on ecology, wilderness skills, and unhurried time in the woods rather than trophies.

name: Templed Hills Camp
description: A United Church of Christ camp in the hills of north-central Ohio, offering an affirming, nature-centered week of hikes, crafts, and outdoor worship.

name: Ten Mile River Scout Camps
description: New York City Scouting's massive century-old reservation near Narrowsburg — thousands of acres, multiple sub-camps, and generations of city scouts earning merit badges on Catskills lakes.

name: Teton Science Schools Summer Camps
description: Field-science summer camps run by the respected Teton Science Schools in Jackson Hole, where kids track wildlife, study ecology in Grand Teton National Park, and backpack with educator-guides.

name: Teton Valley Ranch Camp
description: Wyoming's storied dude-ranch camp near Dubois, running month-long boys' and girls' sessions built around serious horsemanship and multi-day pack trips into the Absaroka and Wind River ranges.

name: Texas Lions Camp
description: A remarkable Kerrville institution where children with disabilities, diabetes, and cancer attend completely free, funded by Texas Lions Clubs since 1949. Adaptive horseback riding, swimming, and a can-do culture built around each camper's abilities.

name: The Nature Place Day Camp
description: A nature-immersion day camp on the wooded La Foret property in the Black Forest, where kids choose their own activities among ponds, forests, and animals in a gentle, child-led philosophy.

name: The Painted Turtle
description: California's SeriousFun camp north of Los Angeles, where children with serious medical conditions sail, ride, and take the stage for free, backed by a full onsite medical center. Hollywood supporters keep the fundraising engine humming.

name: The Wilds Christian Camp
description: A large, conservative Christian camp in the mountains south of Brevard known for its giant swing, tightly programmed days, and strong revival-style preaching.

name: Theodore Naish Scout Reservation
description: The Heart of America Council's close-in camp on the Kansas bluffs above the Kaw River, busy all summer with Cub and Scouts BSA sessions and sharing the Mic-O-Say tradition with its big brother Bartle.

name: TheZone
description: Oorah's heavily subsidized boys' and girls' campuses in the northern Catskills, packing zip lines, leagues, and high-energy kiruv-style programming into affordable sessions for Jewish kids of all backgrounds.

name: Thorne Nature Experience Summer Camp
description: Boulder's classic nature-connection day camp, running small groups into open space to catch bugs, build forts, and learn ecology from educators who have been doing this for decades.

name: Thousand Pines Christian Camp
description: A busy Christian camp in the pines above San Bernardino, an hour from much of LA, with zip lines, a pool, and week-long summer camps kids can attend individually. One of SoCal's most accessible mountain camp options.

name: Timber Lake West
description: The four-week sibling of Timber Lake Camp near Roscoe, designed for families who want a premium Catskills experience in a shorter format — great facilities and a famously warm first-timer culture.

name: Timber Pointe Outdoor Center
description: Easterseals' fully accessible camp on Lake Bloomington, where kids with disabilities and medical needs get an adapted waterfront, sensory-aware staff, and genuine camp independence.

name: Timber Ridge Camp
description: A traditional coed camp in the West Virginia mountains near Winchester, drawing many DC and Baltimore families for its lake, strong sports and arts, and a warm, choice-friendly community.

name: Timberlake Ranch Camps
description: An Evangelical Covenant ranch camp on the cedar bluffs of the Platte River near Marquette, pairing a genuine horsemanship program with warm Bible-camp community.

name: Tom Sawyer Camps
description: Pasadena-area institution since 1926 where day campers ride horses, hike arroyos, and swim under the San Gabriels, with a barefoot-and-bandana ethos handed down through generations. The oldest continuously running day camp in the region.

name: Tomahawk Ranch
description: Girl Scouts of Colorado's mountain ranch camp near Bailey, where girls of all backgrounds ride horses, tend farm animals, and try archery and crafts in a supportive all-girl setting. Non-Scouts are welcome too.

name: Tomahawk Scout Reservation
description: Northern Star Council's sprawling Scout reservation on Long Lake near Birchwood, running multiple sub-camps each week for thousands of Twin Cities-area Scouts every summer.

name: Total Tennis
description: A dedicated tennis sleepaway camp in Saugerties with five-plus hours of daily court time, match play, and video analysis, softened by a pool, pond, and normal camp evenings.

name: Towering Pines Camp
description: A family-run boys' camp near Eagle River paired with Camp Woodland for girls, where sailing, riflery, and campcraft come with a healthy dose of old-school Northwoods tradition.

name: Town & Country Day Camp
description: A traditional Nassau County day camp in the Bethpage area, with pools, sports, and a broad activity menu for central Long Island families. Reliable swim instruction and classic camp spirit.

name: Trackers Bay Area
description: Wilderness-skills day camps around the East Bay and SF where kids join guilds - Rangers, Wilders, Mariners - to learn archery, foraging, fishing, and blacksmithing from unabashedly nerdy outdoor educators. Forest school meets fantasy novel.

name: Trackers Earth Portland
description: Portland's famously imaginative outdoor camp, where kids choose tracks like wilderness survival, archery, fishing, or 'secret agent' and ride buses to forest sites daily. Equal parts bushcraft school and storytelling guild.

name: Trail Blazers
description: One of America's oldest camps, born of the fresh-air movement, taking mostly NYC kids into a thousand wild acres near the Delaware Water Gap for small-group wilderness living - cooking over fires, sleeping in lean-tos, real nature immersion.

name: Trailside Discovery Camp
description: Anchorage's environmental education day camp, run by the Alaska Center for the Environment, with kids exploring glaciers, tide pools, and boreal forest by bus and trail. Alaska kids learning Alaska, week by week.

name: Tranquillity Camp
description: A century-old nonprofit camp in Greene County founded to give NYC kids an affordable summer, still run on Jewish values, donations, and a fiercely loyal alumni base that keeps tuition remarkably low.

name: Trefoil Ranch
description: Girl Scouts of Utah's horse camp up Provo Canyon, pairing a strong riding program with mountain hikes and campfire traditions in a supportive all-girl environment.

name: Trevor Rees-Jones Scout Camp
description: Circle Ten's newer East Texas camp near Athens, built this century with modern dining, air-conditioned program buildings, and polished facilities that surprise families expecting canvas and dust.

name: Triangle Y Camp
description: The Minot YMCA's camp on the bluffs of Lake Sakakawea, giving prairie kids a big-water summer with tubing, fishing, and classic Y cabin life.

name: Triple C Camp
description: Charlottesville's classic wooded day camp, family-run with pools, a ropes course, and weekly themes — a summer staple for local families since the 1980s.

name: Triple R Ranch
description: A western-themed Christian ranch camp in southeastern Virginia with a strong horse program, lake, and energetic Bible teaching for Hampton Roads families.

name: Trout Lake Camps
description: One of the Midwest's largest Christian camps, on Trout Lake near Pine River, with age-specific campuses, waterslides, and big-production worship that draws thousands of Minnesota kids each summer.

name: Tumbleweed Day Camp
description: A storied Brentwood-canyon day camp dating to 1934, where westside kids rotate through pools, zip lines, gaga, and crafts on a sprawling hillside campus. One of LA's blue-chip day camps, with bus routes across the westside.

name: Camp Nawakwa Tuscarora
description: A Lutheran outdoor ministry site in the Pennsylvania Poconos foothills near the Delaware Water Gap, with hiking, lake time, and gentle faith community.

name: Twin Lakes Camp
description: A Presbyterian-rooted camp on two lakes just south of Jackson, with a famous waterfront (the Blob is legend), ziplines, and single-gender weeks packed all summer. Central Mississippi's go-to first sleepaway camp.

name: Twin Oaks Country Day School & Camp
description: A South Shore fixture in Freeport that pairs a country-day-school campus with a traditional summer camp of swimming, sports, and arts. Small enough that the staff genuinely knows every family.

name: Twin Rocks Friends Camp
description: A Quaker (Friends) camp beside a freshwater lake a few hundred yards from the ocean at Rockaway Beach, running youth camps on the Oregon coast for over a century. Beach days, lake blobs, and gentle evangelical programming.

name: Twinlow Camp
description: A United Methodist camp on Twin Lakes north of Coeur d'Alene, offering waterfront fun, a horse program, and classic cabin weeks with a welcoming, low-key spirit.

name: Twinlow Camp and Retreat Center
description: A United Methodist camp on Lower Twin Lake near Rathdrum in the Idaho panhandle, offering small, friendly weeks of paddling, crafts, and inclusive faith community. A quiet local treasure.

name: United Christian Youth Camp (UCYC)
description: One of Arizona's largest Christian camps, in the granite dells country near Prescott, running polished summer weeks with a huge waterslide complex, ropes courses, and energetic worship for church groups statewide.

name: URJ 6 Points Creative Arts Academy
description: The Reform movement's arts-immersion camp near Philadelphia, where campers major in theater, dance, music, visual art, or filmmaking under professional teaching artists, capped by showcase performances. Small, expressive, and warm.

name: URJ 6 Points Sci-Tech Academy
description: A Reform movement STEM camp on a New England prep-school campus, with workshops in robotics, coding, video game design, and science alongside a gentle, geek-positive Jewish community. A haven for kids who never clicked with sports camp.

name: URJ 6 Points Sports Academy
description: The Reform movement's sports camp on a North Carolina boarding-school campus, where campers train in a chosen sport with college-level coaches, then bring the intensity down for Shabbat. For the kid who lives in cleats.

name: URJ Camp Coleman
description: The Reform movement's Southeast camp, tucked into the north Georgia mountains with song sessions, creative worship, and a famously tight-knit community. Campers come from across the South and stay connected year-round.

name: URJ Camp Kalsman
description: The Reform movement's Pacific Northwest camp, built new in 2007 on 300 acres near Arlington with modern lodges, a pool, and a small farm. Music-filled, inclusive, and welcoming to interfaith families.

name: URJ Camp Newman
description: The Reform movement's Northern California camp in the Sonoma hills, famous for song sessions and joyful, music-driven Judaism. It rebuilt with remarkable spirit after the 2017 Tubbs Fire destroyed much of the site.

name: URJ Crane Lake Camp
description: A Reform Jewish movement camp in the Berkshires where song sessions and Shabbat under the trees anchor a full sports-and-lake program. Warm, ruach-filled, and welcoming to first-timers.

name: URJ Goldman Union Camp Institute (GUCI)
description: The Reform movement's heartland camp outside Indianapolis, beloved for its earnest community, creative worship, and generations of Midwest Reform families. Produces an outsized share of rabbis and Jewish educators.

name: URJ Olin-Sang-Ruby Union Institute (OSRUI)
description: The Reform movement's first camp, on Lac La Belle in Wisconsin, organized into distinct units with their own personalities - including a Hebrew-immersion program that's rare in Reform camping. A Chicago-area institution since 1952.

name: Usdan Summer Camp for the Arts
description: Long Island's famed arts day camp, set on a wooded 140-acre campus where thousands of kids study music, theater, dance, and visual art each summer. Buses run from across Long Island and NYC.

name: Valley Mill Camp
description: A beloved outdoorsy day camp on wooded Montgomery County acreage with a private lake, canoeing, and a famous kayaking program. Nature-first and gently paced, a DC-metro classic since the 1950s.

name: Valley View Ranch Equestrian Camp
description: A girls' horse camp on 600 acres atop Lookout Mountain, family-run since 1954, where each camper is matched with a horse and rides every single day. For the truly horse-crazy, there's nothing like it in the region.

name: Van der Meer Summer Tennis Camp
description: Junior tennis camps at the Hilton Head academy built by famed teaching pro Dennis Van der Meer, whose standardized coaching method trained thousands of instructors. Beach afternoons soften the drill-heavy mornings.

name: Vanderkamp
description: A wooded Christian camp on Panther Lake near Cleveland, north of Oneida Lake, offering classic central New York summer weeks of swimming, high ropes, and faith exploration.

name: Victory Bible Camp
description: One of Alaska's oldest camps, set beneath the Chugach and Talkeetna ranges off the Glenn Highway northeast of Palmer, with alpine lakes, a zip line, and old-school Bible camp weeks. Mountain scenery that outclasses most postcards.

name: Victory Junction
description: The NASCAR-themed SeriousFun camp founded by the Petty racing family in memory of Adam Petty, where children with chronic and serious illnesses ride adaptive go-karts, fish, and zipline for free. The race-shop aesthetic delights motor-crazy kids.

name: Vista Verde Ranch Kids Program
description: A luxury guest ranch north of Steamboat Springs whose summer kids' and teens' programs immerse young riders in real horsemanship, ranch life, and mountain adventure with high-end facilities.

name: Walden School Young Musicians Program
description: A five-week program in southern New Hampshire where young musicians learn to compose, improvise, and really hear music through its famous musicianship curriculum. It attracts creative kids who want to write music, not just perform it.

name: Waldsee (Concordia Language Villages)
description: Concordia Language Villages' German immersion village near Bemidji, an architecturally authentic 'BioHaus' campus where campers live entirely in German — passports stamped at the gate, euros at the store.

name: Walnut Hill Summer Dance
description: An intensive residential dance program at the Walnut Hill School for the Arts outside Boston, with serious ballet training and boarding-school facilities. For dancers ready to train, not dabble.

name: Walton's Grizzly Lodge
description: A century-old family-run camp on its own lake near Graeagle in the Lost Sierra, with waterskiing, mountain biking, and traditions passed down through four generations of the same family. One of California's classic old-guard camps.

name: Warm Beach Camp
description: A large Free Methodist camp and conference center overlooking Port Susan Bay near Stanwood, with a well-known horsemanship program, zip lines, and BMX. Big facilities, energetic worship, and lots of program choices.

name: Warren Willis Camp
description: The Florida United Methodists' flagship camp on Lake Griffin, cycling thousands of kids through joyful, music-filled weeks each summer with a beloved college-age staff culture. The state's iconic Methodist camp.

name: The Watersports Camp
description: Run at the Mission Bay Aquatic Center, this day camp puts kids on sailboats, wakeboards, paddleboards, and windsurfers in San Diego's flat-water playground. Arguably the best-equipped youth watersports program on the West Coast.

name: Watson Homestead
description: A United Methodist camp and retreat center in the Southern Tier hills near Painted Post, offering gentle, affordable summer weeks of hiking, crafts, and campfire faith on the historic Watson family homestead.

name: Wavus Camp for Girls
description: Kieve's sister camp on Damariscotta Lake, where girls work up to legitimate backcountry canoe and hiking expeditions. Confidence-building and kindness are the currency here, not competition.

name: W.E. Skelton 4-H Educational Conference Center
description: A 4-H center right on Smith Mountain Lake, where county camp weeks come with a true big-lake waterfront — canoes, fishing docks, and swim beaches included.

name: WeHaKee Camp for Girls
description: A girls' camp on Hunter Lake founded by Dominican sisters in 1923, now warmly ecumenical, where campers choose their own activities and the emphasis is relationships over competition.

name: Wente Scout Reservation
description: A large Scout reservation on its own lake in the Mendocino County hills, prized for waterskiing and boating programs rare at Scout camps, plus classic ridge-top campsites. Troops from the East Bay make the pilgrimage every July.

name: Wesley Acres Camp
description: A small United Methodist camp in the Sheyenne River valley near Dazey, keeping simple, affordable church-camp weeks alive for eastern North Dakota kids.

name: West End House Camp
description: A nonprofit boys' camp on Long Pond in East Parsonsfield, historically tied to Boston's West End House club, that delivers a real Maine sleepaway summer at a fraction of private-camp prices.

name: West Hills Day Camp
description: A family-run Huntington camp on a shady, old-school campus with horses, pools, and a strong country-camp feel. Less flash than some neighbors, more dirt-under-the-fingernails fun.

name: West River United Methodist Center
