description: A United Methodist camp and retreat center on the West River off the Chesapeake south of Annapolis, offering affordable week-long sessions with swimming, canoeing, and waterfront faith community.

name: Westminster Woods
description: A Presbyterian camp among the redwoods of western Sonoma County, pairing challenge courses and creek play with a gentle, open-hearted faith tradition.

name: Westview on the James
description: A United Methodist camp on the banks of the James River west of Richmond, with horses, river paddles, and week-long sessions priced for every congregation family.

name: White Memorial Camp
description: A Disciples of Christ camp on a peninsula in Council Grove Lake in the Flint Hills, offering quiet, water-wrapped church camp weeks with prairie sunsets on three sides.

name: Wilderness Adventures
description: A Jackson-based teen travel program running small-group expeditions — backpacking, rafting, and service trips across the Rockies, Alaska, and beyond — with a decades-long safety record.

name: Wilderness Awareness School Summer Camps
description: Nature-connection camps in the woods near Duvall built around tracking, bird language, fire-making, and stealth games, from a school that helped define the field. Also runs teen overnight expeditions.

name: Wilderness Ranch (Young Life)
description: Young Life's backpacking outpost in the remote San Juans near Creede, trading the resort-camp model for five-day treks where small crews carry their own packs and talk faith on the trail.

name: William Lawrence Camp
description: A century-old nonprofit boys camp near Lake Winnipesaukee that keeps things simple: archery, riflery, a private pond, and cabin brotherhood at a fair price. Small enough that every boy is known.

name: Willow Grove Day Camp
description: A large traditional day camp on wooded acreage in Bucks County's Richboro, with pools, a lake, and a full activity program. A longtime favorite for suburban Philadelphia families.

name: Windells Camp
description: The self-styled 'funnest place on earth' at the base of Mt. Hood — summer ski and snowboard laps on Palmer plus a massive on-campus skatepark and BMX setup. Alongside High Cascade, it defined American summer shred camps.

name: Windermere Baptist Conference Center
description: A sprawling Baptist conference ground on a mile of Lake of the Ozarks shoreline, hosting church youth camps all summer with lake inflatables, worship rallies, and cliff-lined coves. Many Missouri Baptist kids mark their spiritual milestones here.

name: Winding Trails Summer Day Camp
description: A Farmington Valley institution set on 380 acres of woods and spring-fed ponds, where generations of Hartford-area kids learned to swim, paddle, and shoot a bow. Old-school outdoor day camp at its best.

name: Windridge Tennis & Sports Camps
description: Vermont's classic tennis camp, now in Roxbury, where serious court instruction shares the day with soccer, riding, and pond swims in a friendly, preppy-but-kind atmosphere.

name: Windsor Mountain International Camp
description: The former Interlocken camp in the Monadnock hills, long known for its noncompetitive, arts-rich, globally minded community with counselors from dozens of countries.

name: Winnebago Scout Reservation
description: The Patriots' Path Council's summer camp on Durham Pond in Morris County's highlands, a full-program week within an hour of most of northern New Jersey.

name: Winona Camps
description: Wyonegonic's brother camp on Moose Pond, where boys live in platform tents and spend real time on the water and in the mountains. Same family leadership for generations gives it a steady, grounded feel.

name: WinShape Camps
description: The Cathy family's (Chick-fil-A) polished Christian camps for boys and girls on Berry College's enormous campus near Rome, with high-energy worship, skill tracks, and famously well-trained staff. Sessions sell out remarkably fast.

name: Wisconsin Lions Camp
description: A remarkable camp in Rosholt run by the Wisconsin Lions Foundation, serving children who are blind, deaf, or have disabilities — at no cost to families — with a fully adapted waterfront and ropes course.

name: Wohelo Camps
description: A landmark girls' camp on a mile of Sebago Lake shorefront, founded by the Gulick family in 1907 and still run by their descendants. Waterskiing, sailing, and sunset ceremonies steeped in Camp Fire heritage.

name: Wolf Ridge Environmental Learning Center
description: A renowned environmental learning center perched above Lake Superior's North Shore, running summer camps heavy on ecology, rock climbing, and Superior hiking with naturalist-grade instruction.

name: Wonderland Camp
description: A Lake of the Ozarks camp built entirely for children and adults with disabilities, with accessible docks, a zero-entry pool, and staff trained for high-support needs. Campers fish, swim, and dance at weekly luaus that families rave about.

name: Woodland Park Zoo Summer Camp
description: Day camp inside Seattle's Woodland Park Zoo, where groups tour exhibits before gates open, meet keepers, and learn conservation science. A dream week for animal-obsessed kids.

name: Woodleaf (Young Life)
description: Young Life's Northern California high school camp in the Sierra foothills, engineered for 'the best week of your life' with a pool complex, ridge runner zip line, and nightly club talks. Teens come with their local Young Life groups.

name: Woodmont Day Camp
description: A large day camp on scenic wooded acreage in Kinnelon, with pools, a lake, and adventure activities like ziplines and a skate park. A high-energy option for Morris and Passaic County families.

name: Woodward Copper
description: An action-sports mecca at Copper Mountain where skiers, snowboarders, skaters, and scooter kids train on summer snow lanes, foam pits, and the indoor Barn with coaches who ride at a high level.

name: Woodward Park City
description: An action-sports playground outside Park City with indoor skateparks, foam pits, trampolines, and mountain-bike trails, running summer camps for skaters, riders, and freestyle athletes of all levels.

name: Word of Life Island
description: An evangelical teen camp filling an entire island on Schroon Lake, mixing high-energy waterfront and rec programming with nightly worship and preaching. Big, loud, and unabashedly faith-forward.

name: Worth Ranch
description: The Longhorn Council's historic camp on a bend of the Brazos River west of Fort Worth, with stone lodges from the 1930s and a rugged canyon-country character.

name: WV State 4-H Camp at Jackson's Mill
description: The historic state 4-H camp on the boyhood homestead of Stonewall Jackson, cradle of the American 4-H camping movement, where West Virginia youth gather for tribe-based weeks of outdoor skills and leadership.

name: Wyonegonic Camps
description: The oldest continuously operating girls' camp in America, on Moose Pond since 1902. Proudly rustic - cabins without electricity, strong canoeing and sailing - with an unusually loyal multi-generation following.

name: Yawgoog Scout Reservation
description: Rhode Island's legendary Scout reservation - three camps on 1,800 acres around Yawgoog Pond, running continuously since 1916. For many New England Scouts, summer simply means Yawgoog.

name: YMCA Camp Abnaki
description: The Greater Burlington YMCA's boys camp on the Lake Champlain islands, running since 1901 with cabin brotherhood, island sunsets, and a proudly simple program.

name: YMCA Camp Al-Gon-Quian
description: The Ann Arbor Y's northern Michigan camp on Burt Lake, a small, sandy-shored classic where campers sail, ride, and take trips through the Inland Waterway.

name: YMCA Camp Anokijig
description: A large, affordable coed camp on Little Elkhart Lake near Plymouth with one of Wisconsin's biggest camp horse programs and a 'campers first' culture that has kept it independent and thriving since 1926.

name: YMCA Camp Arroyo
description: A modern, eco-designed YMCA camp in the Livermore hills with straw-bale buildings, an organic garden, and pool-centered summer weeks; it also hosts renowned medical camps for children with serious illnesses. Thoughtfully accessible throughout.

name: YMCA Camp Becket
description: One of the country's great traditional boys' camps, in the Berkshires since 1903, where four-week sessions, candlelight chapel talks, and a values-first culture produce famously loyal alumni.

name: YMCA Camp Belknap
description: A storied boys' camp on Lake Winnipesaukee where leadership is famously handed to the campers themselves — no electricity in the cabins, huge spirit, and generations of returning families.

name: YMCA Camp Benson
description: A northwest Illinois YMCA camp in the bluffs along the Waukarusa Valley near Mount Carroll, known for canoe trips, climbing, and a tight-knit, old-school feel.

name: YMCA Camp Bernie
description: A friendly YMCA camp in the rolling hills of Hunterdon County with horses, a small lake, and a strong first-timer track. Many north Jersey kids do their first overnight week here.

name: YMCA Camp Bishop
description: The Grays Harbor YMCA's camp on Lost Lake outside Shelton, a modest and friendly camp serving Olympic Peninsula and south Sound families. Swimming, boating, crafts, and campfire without the big-camp crowds.

name: YMCA Camp Burgess
description: A boys' camp on Cape Cod's Spectacle Pond, paired with neighboring Camp Hayward for girls, with kettle-pond swimming, a working farm, and easygoing Cape summers.

name: YMCA Camp Campbell
description: YMCA of Silicon Valley's camp on the San Lorenzo River amid Boulder Creek redwoods, mixing river wading, archery, and campfire traditions for South Bay kids. Well-worn in the best way.

name: YMCA Camp Campbell Gard
description: A traditional YMCA camp on the Great Miami River near Hamilton with a strong reputation for including campers of all abilities alongside classic camp fun.

name: YMCA Camp Carson
description: Southern Indiana's standout YMCA camp near Evansville, with a private lake, wake sports, horses, and a director-led culture that draws families from three states.

name: YMCA Camp Carter
description: Fort Worth's in-town YMCA resident camp on Lake Worth, offering horses, ziplines, and canoeing minutes from the city at Y prices. A dependable, inclusive first sleepaway experience for Metroplex kids.

name: YMCA Camp Chandler
description: The Montgomery YMCA's camp on Lake Jordan, serving central Alabama for generations with skiing, blobs, horses, and classic Y values. An easy, affordable first overnight camp.

name: YMCA Camp Chingachgook
description: A big-hearted YMCA camp on the east shore of Lake George with serious sailing and Adirondack trip programs at a fraction of private-camp prices. One- and two-week sessions make it an easy first sleepaway.

name: YMCA Camp Classen
description: Oklahoma's landmark YMCA camp on 2,400 acres in the Arbuckle Mountains near Turner Falls, with horses, lakes, and granite-hill hikes that have shaped generations of Oklahoma kids since 1930. Affordable and welcoming.

name: YMCA Camp Collins
description: The Portland YMCA's classic camp on a forested bend of the Sandy River near Gresham, with high ropes, horses, and river swimming minutes from the city. Reliable, inclusive, and well-scholarshipped.

name: YMCA Camp Colman
description: One of the oldest YMCA camps in the country, set on a saltwater inlet of Puget Sound at the tip of the Key Peninsula. Kids split time between beach exploration, boating, and classic camp traditions in old-growth forest.

name: YMCA Camp Coniston
description: A hugely popular independent YMCA camp on its own private lake near Sunapee, known for exceptional staff culture, generous financial aid, and sessions that sell out almost instantly.

name: YMCA Camp Conrad Weiser
description: The South Mountain YMCA's flagship overnight camp on a Blue Mountain ridge near Reading, known for its big international staff and a wide menu from sports to STEM.

name: YMCA Camp Copneconic
description: Flint YMCA's century-old camp on Lake Copneconic near Fenton, a full-service classic with high ropes, sailing, and day and overnight tracks serving mid-Michigan families.

name: YMCA Camp Cormorant
description: The Fargo-Moorhead Y's camp on Big Cormorant Lake in Minnesota lake country, the default first sleepaway for Red River Valley kids, with skiing, horses, and big lake sunsets.

name: YMCA Camp Cory
description: A century-old YMCA camp on Keuka Lake known for one of the best sailing programs in the Finger Lakes, affordable week sessions, and a big-spirited song-and-campfire culture.

name: YMCA Camp Cosby
description: Birmingham YMCA's century-old camp on Logan Martin Lake, with a giant waterfront, wake sports, and a well-run week-based program that draws kids from across the state. Big camp energy with Y affordability.

name: YMCA Camp Crosley
description: The Muncie YMCA's century-old camp on Lake Webster in northern Indiana's lake country, with wakeboarding, sailing, and a friendly, unfussy Y vibe.

name: YMCA Camp Cullen
description: The Houston YMCA's flagship overnight camp on Lake Livingston, rebuilt with modern cabins and a big sailing and robotics lineup. It delivers a broad, affordable program with the Y's welcoming ethos.

name: YMCA Camp Dudley at White Pass
description: The Yakima Family YMCA's mountain camp near White Pass, on Clear Lake in the Cascades west of Naches. A friendly, affordable week of canoeing, hiking, and cabin life for central Washington kids.

name: YMCA Camp Duncan
description: The YMCA of Metro Chicago's century-old overnight camp on Fish Lake in Lake County, an easy first sleepaway for city and suburban kids with a big waterfront and day camp too.

name: YMCA Camp Eberhart
description: One of the Midwest's oldest YMCA camps, on Corey Lake near Three Rivers since 1909, with horses, sailing, and a century's worth of traditions serving Michiana and Chicago-area families.

name: YMCA Camp Edwards
description: A small lakefront camp on Lake Beulah in southeast Wisconsin with a long history of serving Chicago and Milwaukee kids, keeping overnight camp simple, warm, and affordable.

name: YMCA Camp H.R. Erdman
description: The YMCA of Honolulu's storied oceanfront camp at Mokule'ia on Oahu's North Shore, with a hundred years of campfires, tide-pool science, and high ropes between the mountains and the sea. Film buffs know its cabins from the TV show Lost.

name: YMCA Camp Ernst
description: Northern Kentucky's beloved YMCA camp near Cincinnati, famous for its lake blob, zipline, and giant swing, and for counselors who keep energy sky-high all week. Sessions fill within days of registration opening.

name: YMCA Camp Fitch
description: A century-old YMCA camp on the Lake Erie bluffs in Pennsylvania's far northwest corner, beloved across the Youngstown-Erie region for horseback riding, lake adventures, and famously loyal staff.

name: YMCA Camp Flaming Arrow
description: The YMCA's foothold in the famous Hunt camp belt, offering Guadalupe River swimming, archery, and ropes courses at a fraction of neighboring private-camp prices. A friendly, no-pressure way into the Hill Country tradition.

name: YMCA Camp Foss
description: Camp Mi-Te-Na's sister camp on Bow Lake — a low-key girls' camp where the focus is friendship, confidence, and a lot of waterfront time, without the price tag of the fancy camps nearby.

name: YMCA Camp Fox
description: The Glendale YMCA's beachfront camp on Catalina Island, reached by boat and famous for ocean swims, kayak trips, and a giant water trampoline in its own cove. Island camp magic at YMCA prices.

name: YMCA Camp Frank A. Day
description: Run by the West Suburban YMCA of Newton, this century-old coed camp on Lake Lashaway blends classic bunk life with strong waterfront and arts programs.

name: YMCA Camp Fuller
description: A historic YMCA camp on Point Judith Pond where the salt-water waterfront - sailing, windsurfing, kayaking - is the whole point. Rustic cabins, ocean breezes, and 130-plus years of stories.

name: YMCA Camp Gorham
description: A Rochester YMCA camp on Darts Lake deep in the Adirondacks, with a large horse barn, classic waterfront, and week-based sessions that make wilderness camp accessible to almost any budget.

name: YMCA Camp Grady Spruce
description: The YMCA of Dallas' big lakefront camp on Possum Kingdom Lake, where generations of North Texas kids have learned to sail, ski, and jump off the blob. Affordable, high-energy, and split into classic boys' and girls' sides.

name: YMCA Camp Greenkill
description: Part of the YMCA of Greater New York's camp complex in Huguenot, giving city kids affordable one- and two-week sessions of lake swims, ropes courses, and cabin life in the lower Catskills.

name: YMCA Camp Greenville
description: A century-old YMCA camp on 1,400 mountaintop acres at the NC line, home to the famous 'Pretty Place' open-air chapel with its sunrise view over the Blue Ridge escarpment.

name: YMCA Camp Hanes
description: A century-old YMCA camp at the foot of Sauratown Mountain near Hanging Rock, serving Triad families with lake days, climbing, and a famously warm staff culture.

name: YMCA Camp Harrison
description: A YMCA of Greater Charlotte overnight camp in the Brushy Mountain foothills at Herring Ridge, with a big waterfront lake, equestrian center, and classic Y camp spirit.

name: YMCA Camp Hayward
description: Camp Burgess's sister camp for girls on the same Cape Cod property, with riding, a farm-and-garden program, and shared evening events with the boys' side.

name: YMCA Camp Hazen
description: A welcoming coed camp on Cedar Lake in the Connecticut River Valley, known for letting campers choose their own activities and for a strong culture of kindness.

name: YMCA Camp Hi-Rock
description: A remote YMCA camp on a private lake in the far southwest corner of Massachusetts, surrounded by state forest. Unplugged, inclusive, and beloved for its mountaintop setting.

name: YMCA Camp High Harbour
description: Metro Atlanta YMCA's flagship overnight camp on Lake Burton (with a second site on Lake Allatoona), famous for its lakefront, color-war spirit, and long waitlists. A classic first sleepaway for Atlanta kids.

name: Newark YMCA Day Camp
description: The Newark Y's community day camp, offering swim, sports, and enrichment at a low cost with financial aid for urban Essex County families. An accessible, mission-driven neighborhood program.

name: YMCA Camp Horseshoe
description: Tucked in a bend of the Cheat River in West Virginia's high country, Camp Horseshoe mixes river swims, mountain hikes, and a proud tradition of youth leadership programs.

name: YMCA Camp Horsethief Reservoir
description: The Treasure Valley YMCA's overnight camp on Horsethief Reservoir in the mountains near Cascade, with swimming, canoeing, archery, and a strong first-time-camper focus.

name: YMCA Camp Huckins
description: A beloved YMCA girls camp on Lake Ossipee whose registration famously fills within minutes each fall — big-hearted counselors, riding, sailing, and generations of 'Huckins girls'.

name: YMCA Camp Hyde
description: The Greater Wichita YMCA's outdoor day camp on the Little Arkansas River north of the city, giving Wichita kids a woodsy, old-fashioned camp day with bus service from area Ys.

name: YMCA Camp Icaghowan
description: A cozy YMCA of the North camp on Lake Wapogasset in Amery, Wisconsin, serving Twin Cities families since 1908 with horses, sailing, and treehouse-style cabins kids adore.

name: YMCA Camp Ihduhapi
description: The Twin Cities' close-to-home YMCA overnight camp on Lake Independence, ideal for first-timers, with sailing, horses, and a gentle one-week format 30 minutes from Minneapolis.

name: YMCA Camp Immokalee
description: The Jacksonville YMCA's historic camp on Lake Brooklyn in Keystone Heights, running since 1908 with sandy-bottom lake swims, skiing, and Y-values programming. North Florida's grand old sleepaway camp.

name: YMCA Camp Iroquois
description: Camp Russell's longtime sister camp on White Lake near Woodgate, sharing the same century-old YMCA traditions with a slightly smaller, gentler feel for younger and first-time campers.

name: YMCA Camp Jackson
description: A century-old YMCA camp tucked under the Spanish Peaks south of Pueblo, serving southern Colorado kids with classic cabin weeks at a genuinely accessible price. Small, scrappy, and warm.

name: YMCA Camp Jewell
description: A big-hearted YMCA camp in the Berkshire foothills of northwest Connecticut with a large horse barn, private pond, and more than a century of traditions. Draws families from Hartford to New York.

name: YMCA Camp Jones Gulch
description: The YMCA of San Francisco's redwood camp in La Honda, where cabin groups hike among old growth, ride horses, and learn banana-slug lore an hour from the city. A first-camp staple for Peninsula and SF families.

name: YMCA Camp Kanata
description: A YMCA of the Triangle camp minutes from Raleigh offering both day and overnight weeks around a classic lake-and-woods campus — a common first overnight camp for Triangle kids.

name: YMCA Camp Ke'anae
description: The Maui YMCA's camp on the dramatic Ke'anae Peninsula along the Road to Hana, surrounded by taro fields, sea cliffs, and rainforest. Summer sessions weave Hawaiian culture into oceanfront camp life.

name: YMCA Camp Kekoka
description: A YMCA overnight camp on a tidal creek in Virginia's Northern Neck, where kayaking, sailing, and crab pots give kids a taste of Chesapeake Bay life.

name: YMCA Camp Kenan
description: A YMCA camp on the Lake Ontario shoreline at Barker where campers kayak under big-lake skies, tend the nature center, and end each night with fires above the bluff.

name: YMCA Camp Kern
description: A century-old YMCA camp on the Little Miami River between Cincinnati and Dayton, with everything from horse camps to teen adventure trips. Big, busy, and well run.

name: YMCA Camp Kingsley
description: A YMCA camp in the Tug Hill foothills near Ava, north of Rome, offering affordable weeks of horseback riding, lake sports, and cabin life for central New York families.

name: YMCA Camp Kitaki
description: Nebraska's flagship YMCA camp on the Platte River between Lincoln and Omaha, running for over a century with river bluffs, horses, and famously spirited counselors.

name: YMCA Camp Kon-O-Kwee Spencer
description: Pittsburgh's classic YMCA sleepaway camp on a bend of Connoquenessing Creek north of the city, with zip lines, pool, riding, and one-week sessions that generations of western PA kids grew up on.

name: YMCA Camp Lakewood
description: The YMCA of the Ozarks' big resident camp on Sunnen Lake near Potosi, pairing a 360-acre lake with horse trails and high ropes in classic Y-camp fashion. A dependable, inclusive choice for St. Louis families.

name: YMCA Camp Lawrence
description: A YMCA boys camp reached by boat on Bear Island in Lake Winnipesaukee — no cars, no distractions, just docks, cabins, and island traditions dating back over a century.

name: YMCA Camp Letts
description: A historic YMCA camp on the Rhode River near Annapolis, where campers sail and crab on the Chesapeake, ride horses, and live the classic bayfront summer at accessible prices.

name: Arlington County Summer Rec Camp
description: Arlington's county parks-and-rec day camps, a network of affordable neighborhood programs with sports, games, and field trips at local parks and schools. The reliable hometown choice for DC-close families.

name: YMCA Camp Lincoln
description: A big, well-run YMCA day camp on Kingston's Great Pond serving seacoast families for nearly a century, with swim lessons woven into every week and busing from nearby towns.

name: YMCA Camp Loma Mar
description: A YMCA camp on Pescadero Creek in the coastal redwoods between the Peninsula and the sea, with creek hikes, crafts, and a mellow pace suited to younger and first-time campers. Quietly lovely.

name: YMCA Camp MacLean
description: A Chicago-area Y tradition across the Wisconsin line on Lake Beulah, where a classic waterfront, horses, and Friday banquets have anchored summers since the 1940s.

name: YMCA Camp Manitou-Lin
description: The Grand Rapids Y's camp on Barlow Lake south of the city, with a strong horse program, classic waterfront, and a welcoming small-camp feel that suits first-timers.

name: YMCA Camp Marston
description: San Diego's century-old YMCA camp in the oak-and-pine mountains of Julian, with a pond, zip lines, and the friendly, well-drilled program you would expect from a hundred summers of practice. A dependable first sleepaway.

name: YMCA Camp Mason
description: An independent YMCA camp near the Delaware Water Gap with a famously inclusive, international community, skate park, ranch program, and a well-run classic camp core. Draws campers from around the world.

name: YMCA Camp Ralph S. Mason
description: A coed camp beside the Delaware Water Gap with a skate park, ranch program, and a famously welcoming take on classic camp traditions, drawing lots of first-generation campers from the NYC area.

name: YMCA Camp Matawa
description: A cozy camp in Wisconsin's Kettle Moraine forest run by the Kettle Moraine YMCA, with a spring-fed lake, glacial hiking trails, and a gentle pace suited to younger campers.

name: YMCA Camp Matollionequay
description: The girls' camp at YMCA of the Pines, sharing the Pine Barrens lakes with brother camp Ockanickon and known for tight cabin bonds and long-tenured staff.

name: YMCA Camp Menogyn
description: A wilderness canoeing, backpacking, and climbing camp on West Bearskin Lake near the end of the Gunflint Trail — reachable only by boat — sending small single-gender and coed crews deep into the Boundary Waters and beyond.

name: YMCA Camp Mi-Te-Na
description: The Granite YMCA's century-old boys camp on Half Moon Lake, a brotherhood-and-campfires kind of place where many Manchester-area dads send sons to their old cabins.

name: YMCA Camp Miller
description: The Duluth Area YMCA's camp on Sturgeon Lake, one of Minnesota's oldest, offering week-long classic sessions — canoeing, riding, campouts — at working-family prices since the 1890s.

name: YMCA Camp Minikani
description: Milwaukee's hometown overnight camp on Amy Belle Lake, run by the YMCA since 1919, known for exceptional counselor culture and a leadership ladder many campers climb all the way to staff.

name: YMCA Camp Mohawk
description: A century-old YMCA camp for girls in the Litchfield hills, loved for its horseback program, lake days, and big-sister culture that keeps alumnae sending their daughters back.

name: YMCA Camp Montvale
description: Knoxville-area YMCA camp in the foothills of the Smokies near Maryville, with day and overnight weeks built around creeks, trails, and a small lake. Low-key, affordable, and close to the mountains.

name: YMCA Camp Nissokone
description: The Detroit Y's northern camp on Van Etten Lake near Lake Huron, where up-north Michigan summers mean sailing, horses, and canoe trips down the Au Sable River.

name: YMCA Camp Nokomis
description: Camp Lawrence's sister camp on the other end of Bear Island, where girls swim, sail, and sing on a car-free Winnipesaukee island. Big sisterhood energy at a YMCA price.

name: YMCA Camp Oakes
description: YMCA of Greater Long Beach's big mountain camp near Big Bear at 7,000 feet, with a lake, high ropes, and an astronomy observatory under dark Sierra skies. A workhorse camp that has served generations of LA-area kids.

name: YMCA Camp Ockanickon
description: A boys' camp in the New Jersey Pine Barrens run by YMCA of the Pines, with cedar lakes, mountain biking, and a brother camp culture that pairs with Matollionequay next door.

name: YMCA Camp Ocoee
description: A century-old YMCA camp on Lake Ocoee in the Cherokee National Forest, near some of the Southeast's best whitewater, with a classic cabin-and-campfire program. Chattanooga's traditional summer escape.

name: YMCA Camp of Maine
description: A century-old YMCA camp on Cobbossee Lake offering the classic Maine overnight experience - waterfront, archery, campfires - at Y prices with plenty of financial aid.

name: YMCA Camp Ohiyesa
description: The YMCA of Metropolitan Detroit's overnight camp on Fish Lake in Holly, giving metro kids a week of horses, sailing, and cabin life barely an hour from the city.

name: YMCA Camp Olson
description: A big-hearted camp on Little Boy Lake in northern Minnesota with sailing, horses, and a strong scholarship tradition that brings kids from all over the state up north.

name: YMCA Camp Onyahsa
description: One of the oldest Y camps in the country, on Chautauqua Lake since the 1890s, with sailing, kayaking, and a generous scholarship fund that keeps bunks open to every family.

name: YMCA Camp Pendalouan
description: A hundred-year-old YMCA camp on Big Blue Lake near the Lake Michigan shore, where classic cabin weeks come with fishing, archery, and famously loyal returning staff.

name: YMCA Camp Piomingo
description: Louisville's YMCA overnight camp in the knobs near Otter Creek, running classic cabin weeks with horses, a pool, and strong counselor-camper bonds since the 1930s. Kentucky's most familiar first sleepaway.

name: YMCA Camp Potawotami
description: Fort Wayne's YMCA camp on Blackman Lake, over a century old, offering a gentle traditional overnight program in Indiana's northeast lakes region.

name: YMCA Camp Reed
description: Spokane's beloved YMCA camp on Fan Lake north of the city, running for over a century with famous all-camp traditions and a fiercely loyal alumni base. The definitive sleepaway camp for the Inland Northwest.

name: YMCA Camp Russell
description: A historic YMCA of the Greater Tri-Valley camp on White Lake near Woodgate, at the southwestern edge of the Adirondacks, running affordable traditional sessions of swimming, sailing, and campfires for over a century.

name: YMCA Camp Seymour
description: A century-old YMCA camp on Glen Cove near Gig Harbor, run by the YMCA of Pierce and Kitsap Counties. Marine biology touch tanks, kayaking, and a high-ropes course keep the program varied without feeling frantic.

name: YMCA Camp Shady Brook
description: The Harrisburg-area YMCA's resident camp in the Doubling Gap valley of the Blue Mountains, offering affordable weeks of pool time, creek stomping, archery, and campfires with a friendly, come-as-you-are crowd.

name: YMCA Camp Shand
description: The Lancaster Y's historic camp in the wooded hills near Mt. Gretna, with a strong scholarship mission that sends many first-time campers to the woods each summer.

name: YMCA Camp Silver Beach
description: A YMCA camp on a private Chesapeake Bay beach on Virginia's Eastern Shore, with sailing, wakeboarding, and bayfront campfires — the rare camp with a sandy saltwater waterfront.

name: YMCA Camp Sky-Y
description: The Valley of the Sun YMCA's flagship camp in the cool pines south of Prescott, a rite of passage for Phoenix kids for generations with classic cabins, horses, and a huge alumni family.

name: YMCA Camp Sloane
description: A big, diverse coed camp in Connecticut's Litchfield Hills where campers sleep in platform tents, with riding, sailing, and a notably international camper body.

name: YMCA Camp Sloper
description: Southington's YMCA outdoor center turned summer powerhouse, with a pond waterfront, mountain bike trails, and a slate of themed camps serving hundreds of central Connecticut kids a week.

name: YMCA Camp Soles
description: A small, woodsy YMCA camp on its own lake in the Laurel Highlands, where campfires, canoeing, and mountain hikes matter more than fancy facilities. A quiet gem for kids who like the outdoors.

name: YMCA Camp Speers
description: A big-hearted YMCA camp on a private lake in the Poconos with sailing, horses, and a strong character-building tradition at a fraction of private-camp prices. One-week sessions make it an easy first try.

name: YMCA Camp St. Croix
description: A YMCA of the North classic on the St. Croix River just past Hudson, where river canoeing, campouts, and a century of traditions make an easy on-ramp to overnight camp for Twin Cities kids.

name: YMCA Camp Surf
description: A rare beachfront sleepaway camp on 45 oceanfront acres near the Mexican border, where YMCA campers surf, bodyboard, and beach-bonfire all week at YMCA prices. One of the most affordable ways for a kid to learn to surf overnight-camp style.

name: YMCA Camp Takatoka
description: The Tulsa YMCA's lakefront camp on Fort Gibson Lake, running classic cabin weeks of sailing, riflery, and campfires since the 1920s. A dependable, budget-friendly Oklahoma overnight tradition.

name: YMCA Camp Takodah
description: A century-old Cheshire YMCA camp on Cass Pond with separate boys' and girls' villages, famous friendship traditions, and a price that keeps camp within reach for Monadnock-region families.

name: YMCA Camp Tecumseh
description: Indiana's powerhouse Y camp on the Tippecanoe River, drawing thousands each summer with a huge horse barn, river tubing, and an openly Christian-values program woven through camp life.

name: YMCA Camp Thunderbird
description: The YMCA of Greater Charlotte's big flagship camp on Lake Wylie, famous for its waterskiing and wakeboarding program and a spirited, well-oiled Y camp culture.

name: YMCA Camp Tippecanoe
description: A rustic YMCA camp on 1,100 hilly acres above Clendening Lake in eastern Ohio, big on horses, fishing, and unplugged outdoor time at a modest price.

name: YMCA Camp Tockwogh
description: A big, well-equipped YMCA camp on the Chesapeake Bay shore of Maryland's Eastern Shore, famous for its sailing and windsurfing program alongside classic land activities. Run by the YMCA of Delaware.

name: YMCA Camp U-Nah-Li-Ya
description: The Green Bay Y's Northwoods camp on spring-fed Chute Pond, an unfussy, affordable classic where kids fish off the dock, paddle, and unplug for a week or two.

name: YMCA Camp Wapsie
description: Cedar Rapids' YMCA camp on the Wapsipinicon River, a hundred-plus years of canoes, horses, and cabin skits with an unusually devoted local following.

name: YMCA Camp Warren
description: A classic YMCA of the North camp on Half Moon Lake near Eveleth, running separate boys' and girls' sessions with sailing, riding, and log-cabin traditions dating to 1927.

name: YMCA Camp Watia
description: The YMCA of Western North Carolina's overnight camp near the Nantahala, with newer facilities and easy access to some of the best paddling and hiking terrain in the Smokies.

name: YMCA Camp Weaver
description: Greensboro's big YMCA day camp on a wooded lakefront property, running summer-long weekly sessions with swimming, boating, and a small overnight program.

name: YMCA Camp Weona
description: The Buffalo-area YMCA's overnight camp in the Wyoming County hills, a century-old tradition of pond swims, ropes courses, and cabin cheers at working-family prices.

name: YMCA Camp Westwood
description: The YMCA of Pawtucket's 80-acre day camp in Coventry with a private waterfront, boating, and classic camp activities. A dependable, affordable staple for central Rhode Island families.

name: YMCA Camp Wewa
description: Central Florida YMCA's classic overnight camp in Apopka, with a spring-fed lake, banana boats, and campfire traditions dating to the 1940s. One of the Orlando area's few true sleepaway options.

name: YMCA Camp Whittle
description: A YMCA camp in the pines on the quiet north side of Big Bear Lake, running classic cabin weeks with mountain biking, archery, and lake trips for LA and Inland Empire kids. Sturdy, friendly, and well-priced.

name: YMCA Camp Widjiwagan
description: The famed YMCA wilderness-tripping camp on Burntside Lake outside Ely, where teens paddle the Boundary Waters and, as they advance, take month-long expeditions to the Arctic. Base camp is just the launching pad.

name: YMCA Camp Widjiwagan
description: Nashville's big YMCA camp on Percy Priest Lake, running both day and overnight programs with an epic waterfront - wakeboarding, blobs, water slides - and modern lodges. It books up almost instantly each winter.

name: YMCA Camp Willson
description: A 500-acre YMCA camp north of Columbus known especially for its big horseback program and welcoming, no-frills Y traditions. A solid affordable first sleepaway camp.

name: YMCA Camp Winona
description: One of Florida's oldest camps, run by the Volusia YMCA on a pristine lake near DeLeon Springs, with skiing, blobs, horses, and a century of traditions. Old-Florida woods and water at its best.

name: YMCA Camp Woodstock
description: A hundred-year-old YMCA camp on Black Pond in Connecticut's Quiet Corner, where generations of families come back for candlelight ceremonies and lake swims. Unhurried, affordable, and deeply traditional.

name: YMCA Camp Y-Noah
description: The Akron-area YMCA's lakefront camp south of the city, running both a large day camp and overnight weeks with horses, boating, and a busy schedule.

name: YMCA of Central & Northern Westchester Camp
description: The Y's outdoorsy day camp on a wooded property in northern Westchester, with a lake, hiking, and classic camp skills at an accessible price. A community anchor with financial aid and inclusion support.

name: YMCA Raintree Ranch
description: The horse camp next door to Camp Marston, where YMCA campers ride twice a day, learn barn care, and end the week with a horse show for parents. Small herd, small camp, big horse-kid energy.

name: YMCA Storer Camps
description: A big, century-old camp on Stony Lake in southern Michigan with a huge herd of horses, a working nature center, and deep ties to Toledo and Detroit families alike.

name: Young Life Carolina Point
description: Young Life's newest Southeast camp, perched above the Blue Ridge Escarpment with modern lodges, a wake pond, and high-adventure elements aimed squarely at teenagers.

name: Young Life Castaway Club
description: Young Life's lakefront showpiece on Pelican Lake in Minnesota, where high schoolers get parasailing, inflatables, and polished club talks designed for kids new to faith.

name: Young Life Crooked Creek Ranch
description: Young Life's ranch in the Fraser Valley pairs resort-quality amenities — zip lines, horses, mountain views — with the movement's signature mix of hilarity and heartfelt faith talks.

name: Young Life Frontier Ranch
description: One of Young Life's flagship Colorado properties at the foot of Mount Princeton, where high schoolers come with their local YL group for a week of ropes courses, pool parties, and gospel talks.

name: Young Life Lake Champion
description: Young Life's property in New York's western Catskills region, a private lake with go-karts, a pool complex, and gospel presentations tuned for skeptical city and suburban teens.

name: Young Life Lost Canyon
description: Young Life's high-desert resort camp near the Grand Canyon, with a wake park, skate plaza, and polished lodges built to wow teenagers who'd never pick a church camp.

name: Young Life Saranac Village
description: Young Life's Adirondack lakefront camp where Northeast high schoolers get a week of blob launches, banquet nights, and honest talks about faith.

name: Young Life Timber Wolf Lake
description: Young Life's Midwest lakefront property in northern Michigan, built to give high schoolers a resort-style week — wakeboarding, giant swings, great food — wrapped around nightly gospel talks.

name: Young Life Washington Family Ranch
description: Built on the remote former Rajneeshpuram site in Oregon's high desert, this jaw-dropping Young Life ranch has a water park, gym complexes, and week-long camps for both teens and preteens.

name: Young Life Windy Gap
description: Young Life's Blue Ridge property north of Asheville, giving Southeast high schoolers a week of ridge views, pool antics, and disarming talks about Jesus.

name: Young Life Woodleaf
description: Young Life's Northern California property in the Sierra foothills, where high schoolers get a fast-paced week of pools, ridge runners, and club talks aimed at kids far from church.

name: Young People's Day Camp
description: A budget-friendly day camp network that has served NYC's five boroughs, Long Island, and nearby suburbs for decades, with door-to-door busing and daily trips to pools and parks. No-frills, but a rite of passage for many city kids.

name: YWCO Camp for Girls
description: The Athens YWCO's century-old girls camp in the northeast Georgia mountains, pairing lake swims and horseback riding with sweet, low-pressure traditions. It has a hometown feel, with many campers coming from Athens-area families.

name: Z Frank Apachi Day Camp (JCC Chicago)
description: JCC Chicago's big North Shore day camp on a wooded Northbrook campus, with pools, sports, and Shabbat sing-alongs - a first camp experience for thousands of Chicago-area Jewish kids.
